67/* GUC parameters */
68double		cursor_tuple_fraction = DEFAULT_CURSOR_TUPLE_FRACTION;
69int			debug_parallel_query = DEBUG_PARALLEL_OFF;
70bool		parallel_leader_participation = true;
71bool		enable_distinct_reordering = true;
72
73/* Hook for plugins to get control in planner() */
74planner_hook_type planner_hook = NULL;
75
76/* Hook for plugins to get control when grouping_planner() plans upper rels */
77create_upper_paths_hook_type create_upper_paths_hook = NULL;
78
79
80/* Expression kind codes for preprocess_expression */
81#define EXPRKIND_QUAL				0
82#define EXPRKIND_TARGET				1
83#define EXPRKIND_RTFUNC				2
84#define EXPRKIND_RTFUNC_LATERAL		3
85#define EXPRKIND_VALUES				4
86#define EXPRKIND_VALUES_LATERAL		5
87#define EXPRKIND_LIMIT				6
88#define EXPRKIND_APPINFO			7
89#define EXPRKIND_PHV				8
90#define EXPRKIND_TABLESAMPLE		9
91#define EXPRKIND_ARBITER_ELEM		10
92#define EXPRKIND_TABLEFUNC			11
93#define EXPRKIND_TABLEFUNC_LATERAL	12
94#define EXPRKIND_GROUPEXPR			13
95
96/*
97 * Data specific to grouping sets
98 */
99typedef struct
100{
101	List	   *rollups;
102	List	   *hash_sets_idx;
103	double		dNumHashGroups;
104	bool		any_hashable;
105	Bitmapset  *unsortable_refs;
106	Bitmapset  *unhashable_refs;
107	List	   *unsortable_sets;
108	int		   *tleref_to_colnum_map;
109} grouping_sets_data;
110
111/*
112 * Temporary structure for use during WindowClause reordering in order to be
113 * able to sort WindowClauses on partitioning/ordering prefix.
114 */
115typedef struct
116{
117	WindowClause *wc;
118	List	   *uniqueOrder;	/* A List of unique ordering/partitioning
119								 * clauses per Window */
120} WindowClauseSortData;
121
122/* Passthrough data for standard_qp_callback */
123typedef struct
124{
125	List	   *activeWindows;	/* active windows, if any */
126	grouping_sets_data *gset_data;	/* grouping sets data, if any */
127	SetOperationStmt *setop;	/* parent set operation or NULL if not a
128								 * subquery belonging to a set operation */
129} standard_qp_extra;
130
131/*
132 * Context for the find_having_collation_conflicts walker.
133 *
134 * ancestor_collids is a stack of inputcollids contributed by collation-aware
135 * ancestors of the current node.  Entries are pushed before recursing into a
136 * node's children and popped afterwards, so the stack reflects exactly the
137 * inputcollids on the current root-to-node path.
138 */
139typedef struct
140{
141	Index		group_rtindex;
142	List	   *ancestor_collids;
143} having_collation_ctx;
144

147static void preprocess_qual_conditions(PlannerInfo *root, Node *jtnode);

159							   double tuple_fraction,
160							   int64 *offset_est, int64 *count_est);
161static List *preprocess_groupclause(PlannerInfo *root, List *force);
162static List *extract_rollup_sets(List *groupingSets);
163static List *reorder_grouping_sets(List *groupingSets, List *sortclause);

165static double get_number_of_groups(PlannerInfo *root,
166								   double path_rows,
167								   grouping_sets_data *gd,
168								   List *target_list);

170										 RelOptInfo *input_rel,
171										 PathTarget *target,
172										 bool target_parallel_safe,
173										 grouping_sets_data *gd);
174static bool is_degenerate_grouping(PlannerInfo *root);
175static void create_degenerate_grouping_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
176											 RelOptInfo *input_rel,
177											 RelOptInfo *grouped_rel);
178static RelOptInfo *make_grouping_rel(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *input_rel,
179									 PathTarget *target, bool target_parallel_safe,
180									 Node *havingQual);
181static void create_ordinary_grouping_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
182										   RelOptInfo *input_rel,
183										   RelOptInfo *grouped_rel,
184										   const AggClauseCosts *agg_costs,
185										   grouping_sets_data *gd,
186										   GroupPathExtraData *extra,
187										   RelOptInfo **partially_grouped_rel_p);
188static void consider_groupingsets_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
189										RelOptInfo *grouped_rel,
190										Path *path,
191										bool is_sorted,
192										bool can_hash,
193										grouping_sets_data *gd,
194										const AggClauseCosts *agg_costs,
195										double dNumGroups);
196static RelOptInfo *create_window_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
197									   RelOptInfo *input_rel,
198									   PathTarget *input_target,
199									   PathTarget *output_target,
200									   bool output_target_parallel_safe,
201									   WindowFuncLists *wflists,
202									   List *activeWindows);
203static void create_one_window_path(PlannerInfo *root,
204								   RelOptInfo *window_rel,
205								   Path *path,
206								   PathTarget *input_target,
207								   PathTarget *output_target,
208								   WindowFuncLists *wflists,
209								   List *activeWindows);
210static RelOptInfo *create_distinct_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
211										 RelOptInfo *input_rel,
212										 PathTarget *target);
213static void create_partial_distinct_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
214										  RelOptInfo *input_rel,
215										  RelOptInfo *final_distinct_rel,
216										  PathTarget *target);
217static RelOptInfo *create_final_distinct_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
218											   RelOptInfo *input_rel,
219											   RelOptInfo *distinct_rel);
220static List *get_useful_pathkeys_for_distinct(PlannerInfo *root,
221											  List *needed_pathkeys,
222											  List *path_pathkeys);
223static RelOptInfo *create_ordered_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
224										RelOptInfo *input_rel,
225										PathTarget *target,
226										bool target_parallel_safe,
227										double limit_tuples);
228static PathTarget *make_group_input_target(PlannerInfo *root,
229										   PathTarget *final_target);
230static PathTarget *make_partial_grouping_target(PlannerInfo *root,
231												PathTarget *grouping_target,
232												Node *havingQual);
233static List *postprocess_setop_tlist(List *new_tlist, List *orig_tlist);
234static void optimize_window_clauses(PlannerInfo *root,
235									WindowFuncLists *wflists);
236static List *select_active_windows(PlannerInfo *root, WindowFuncLists *wflists);
237static void name_active_windows(List *activeWindows);
238static PathTarget *make_window_input_target(PlannerInfo *root,
239											PathTarget *final_target,
240											List *activeWindows);
241static List *make_pathkeys_for_window(PlannerInfo *root, WindowClause *wc,
242									  List *tlist);
243static PathTarget *make_sort_input_target(PlannerInfo *root,
244										  PathTarget *final_target,
245										  bool *have_postponed_srfs);
246static void adjust_paths_for_srfs(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *rel,
247								  List *targets, List *targets_contain_srfs);
248static void add_paths_to_grouping_rel(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *input_rel,
249									  RelOptInfo *grouped_rel,
250									  RelOptInfo *partially_grouped_rel,
251									  const AggClauseCosts *agg_costs,
252									  grouping_sets_data *gd,
253									  double dNumGroups,
254									  GroupPathExtraData *extra);
255static RelOptInfo *create_partial_grouping_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
256												 RelOptInfo *grouped_rel,
257												 RelOptInfo *input_rel,
258												 grouping_sets_data *gd,
259												 GroupPathExtraData *extra,
260												 bool force_rel_creation);
261static Path *make_ordered_path(PlannerInfo *root,
262							   RelOptInfo *rel,
263							   Path *path,
264							   Path *cheapest_path,
265							   List *pathkeys,
266							   double limit_tuples);
267static void gather_grouping_paths(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *rel);
268static bool can_partial_agg(PlannerInfo *root);
269static void apply_scanjoin_target_to_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
270										   RelOptInfo *rel,
271										   List *scanjoin_targets,
272										   List *scanjoin_targets_contain_srfs,
273										   bool scanjoin_target_parallel_safe,
274										   bool tlist_same_exprs);
275static void create_partitionwise_grouping_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
276												RelOptInfo *input_rel,
277												RelOptInfo *grouped_rel,
278												RelOptInfo *partially_grouped_rel,
279												const AggClauseCosts *agg_costs,
280												grouping_sets_data *gd,
281												PartitionwiseAggregateType patype,
282												GroupPathExtraData *extra);
283static bool group_by_has_partkey(RelOptInfo *input_rel,
284								 List *targetList,
285								 List *groupClause);
286static int	common_prefix_cmp(const void *a, const void *b);
287static List *generate_setop_child_grouplist(SetOperationStmt *op,
288											List *targetlist);
289
290
291/*****************************************************************************
292 *
293 *	   Query optimizer entry point
294 *
295 * To support loadable plugins that monitor or modify planner behavior,
296 * we provide a hook variable that lets a plugin get control before and
297 * after the standard planning process.  The plugin would normally call
298 * standard_planner().
299 *
300 * Note to plugin authors: standard_planner() scribbles on its Query input,
301 * so you'd better copy that data structure if you want to plan more than once.
302 *
303 *****************************************************************************/
304PlannedStmt *
305planner(Query *parse, const char *query_string, int cursorOptions,
306		ParamListInfo boundParams)
307{
308	PlannedStmt *result;
309
310	if (planner_hook)
311		result = (*planner_hook) (parse, query_string, cursorOptions, boundParams);
312	else
313		result = standard_planner(parse, query_string, cursorOptions, boundParams);
314
315	pgstat_report_plan_id(result->planId, false);
316
317	return result;
318}
319
320PlannedStmt *
321standard_planner(Query *parse, const char *query_string, int cursorOptions,
322				 ParamListInfo boundParams)
323{
324	PlannedStmt *result;
325	PlannerGlobal *glob;
326	double		tuple_fraction;
327	PlannerInfo *root;
328	RelOptInfo *final_rel;
329	Path	   *best_path;
330	Plan	   *top_plan;
331	ListCell   *lp,
332			   *lr;
333
334	/*
335	 * Set up global state for this planner invocation.  This data is needed
336	 * across all levels of sub-Query that might exist in the given command,
337	 * so we keep it in a separate struct that's linked to by each per-Query
338	 * PlannerInfo.
339	 */
340	glob = makeNode(PlannerGlobal);
341
342	glob->boundParams = boundParams;
343	glob->subplans = NIL;
344	glob->subpaths = NIL;
345	glob->subroots = NIL;
346	glob->rewindPlanIDs = NULL;
347	glob->finalrtable = NIL;
348	glob->allRelids = NULL;
349	glob->prunableRelids = NULL;
350	glob->finalrteperminfos = NIL;
351	glob->finalrowmarks = NIL;
352	glob->resultRelations = NIL;
353	glob->appendRelations = NIL;
354	glob->partPruneInfos = NIL;
355	glob->relationOids = NIL;
356	glob->invalItems = NIL;
357	glob->paramExecTypes = NIL;
358	glob->lastPHId = 0;
359	glob->lastRowMarkId = 0;
360	glob->lastPlanNodeId = 0;
361	glob->transientPlan = false;
362	glob->dependsOnRole = false;
363	glob->partition_directory = NULL;
364
365	/*
366	 * Assess whether it's feasible to use parallel mode for this query. We
367	 * can't do this in a standalone backend, or if the command will try to
368	 * modify any data, or if this is a cursor operation, or if GUCs are set
369	 * to values that don't permit parallelism, or if parallel-unsafe
370	 * functions are present in the query tree.
371	 *
372	 * (Note that we do allow CREATE TABLE AS, SELECT INTO, and CREATE
373	 * MATERIALIZED VIEW to use parallel plans, but this is safe only because
374	 * the command is writing into a completely new table which workers won't
375	 * be able to see.  If the workers could see the table, the fact that
376	 * group locking would cause them to ignore the leader's heavyweight GIN
377	 * page locks would make this unsafe.  We'll have to fix that somehow if
378	 * we want to allow parallel inserts in general; updates and deletes have
379	 * additional problems especially around combo CIDs.)
380	 *
381	 * For now, we don't try to use parallel mode if we're running inside a
382	 * parallel worker.  We might eventually be able to relax this
383	 * restriction, but for now it seems best not to have parallel workers
384	 * trying to create their own parallel workers.
385	 */
386	if ((cursorOptions & CURSOR_OPT_PARALLEL_OK) != 0 &&
387		IsUnderPostmaster &&
388		parse->commandType == CMD_SELECT &&
389		!parse->hasModifyingCTE &&
390		max_parallel_workers_per_gather > 0 &&
391		!IsParallelWorker())
392	{
393		/* all the cheap tests pass, so scan the query tree */
394		glob->maxParallelHazard = max_parallel_hazard(parse);
395		glob->parallelModeOK = (glob->maxParallelHazard != PROPARALLEL_UNSAFE);
396	}
397	else
398	{
399		/* skip the query tree scan, just assume it's unsafe */
400		glob->maxParallelHazard = PROPARALLEL_UNSAFE;
401		glob->parallelModeOK = false;
402	}
403
404	/*
405	 * glob->parallelModeNeeded is normally set to false here and changed to
406	 * true during plan creation if a Gather or Gather Merge plan is actually
407	 * created (cf. create_gather_plan, create_gather_merge_plan).
408	 *
409	 * However, if debug_parallel_query = on or debug_parallel_query =
410	 * regress, then we impose parallel mode whenever it's safe to do so, even
411	 * if the final plan doesn't use parallelism.  It's not safe to do so if
412	 * the query contains anything parallel-unsafe; parallelModeOK will be
413	 * false in that case.  Note that parallelModeOK can't change after this
414	 * point. Otherwise, everything in the query is either parallel-safe or
415	 * parallel-restricted, and in either case it should be OK to impose
416	 * parallel-mode restrictions.  If that ends up breaking something, then
417	 * either some function the user included in the query is incorrectly
418	 * labeled as parallel-safe or parallel-restricted when in reality it's
419	 * parallel-unsafe, or else the query planner itself has a bug.
420	 */
421	glob->parallelModeNeeded = glob->parallelModeOK &&
422		(debug_parallel_query != DEBUG_PARALLEL_OFF);
423
424	/* Determine what fraction of the plan is likely to be scanned */
425	if (cursorOptions & CURSOR_OPT_FAST_PLAN)
426	{
427		/*
428		 * We have no real idea how many tuples the user will ultimately FETCH
429		 * from a cursor, but it is often the case that he doesn't want 'em
430		 * all, or would prefer a fast-start plan anyway so that he can
431		 * process some of the tuples sooner.  Use a GUC parameter to decide
432		 * what fraction to optimize for.
433		 */
434		tuple_fraction = cursor_tuple_fraction;
435
436		/*
437		 * We document cursor_tuple_fraction as simply being a fraction, which
438		 * means the edge cases 0 and 1 have to be treated specially here.  We
439		 * convert 1 to 0 ("all the tuples") and 0 to a very small fraction.
440		 */
441		if (tuple_fraction >= 1.0)
442			tuple_fraction = 0.0;
443		else if (tuple_fraction <= 0.0)
444			tuple_fraction = 1e-10;
445	}
446	else
447	{
448		/* Default assumption is we need all the tuples */
449		tuple_fraction = 0.0;
450	}
451
452	/* primary planning entry point (may recurse for subqueries) */
453	root = subquery_planner(glob, parse, NULL, false, tuple_fraction, NULL);
454
455	/* Select best Path and turn it into a Plan */
456	final_rel = fetch_upper_rel(root, UPPERREL_FINAL, NULL);
457	best_path = get_cheapest_fractional_path(final_rel, tuple_fraction);
458
459	top_plan = create_plan(root, best_path);
460
461	/*
462	 * If creating a plan for a scrollable cursor, make sure it can run
463	 * backwards on demand.  Add a Material node at the top at need.
464	 */
465	if (cursorOptions & CURSOR_OPT_SCROLL)
466	{
467		if (!ExecSupportsBackwardScan(top_plan))
468			top_plan = materialize_finished_plan(top_plan);
469	}
470
471	/*
472	 * Optionally add a Gather node for testing purposes, provided this is
473	 * actually a safe thing to do.
474	 *
475	 * We can add Gather even when top_plan has parallel-safe initPlans, but
476	 * then we have to move the initPlans to the Gather node because of
477	 * SS_finalize_plan's limitations.  That would cause cosmetic breakage of
478	 * regression tests when debug_parallel_query = regress, because initPlans
479	 * that would normally appear on the top_plan move to the Gather, causing
480	 * them to disappear from EXPLAIN output.  That doesn't seem worth kluging
481	 * EXPLAIN to hide, so skip it when debug_parallel_query = regress.
482	 */
483	if (debug_parallel_query != DEBUG_PARALLEL_OFF &&
484		top_plan->parallel_safe &&
485		(top_plan->initPlan == NIL ||
486		 debug_parallel_query != DEBUG_PARALLEL_REGRESS))
487	{
488		Gather	   *gather = makeNode(Gather);
489		Cost		initplan_cost;
490		bool		unsafe_initplans;
491
492		gather->plan.targetlist = top_plan->targetlist;
493		gather->plan.qual = NIL;
494		gather->plan.lefttree = top_plan;
495		gather->plan.righttree = NULL;
496		gather->num_workers = 1;
497		gather->single_copy = true;
498		gather->invisible = (debug_parallel_query == DEBUG_PARALLEL_REGRESS);
499
500		/* Transfer any initPlans to the new top node */
501		gather->plan.initPlan = top_plan->initPlan;
502		top_plan->initPlan = NIL;
503
504		/*
505		 * Since this Gather has no parallel-aware descendants to signal to,
506		 * we don't need a rescan Param.
507		 */
508		gather->rescan_param = -1;
509
510		/*
511		 * Ideally we'd use cost_gather here, but setting up dummy path data
512		 * to satisfy it doesn't seem much cleaner than knowing what it does.
513		 */
514		gather->plan.startup_cost = top_plan->startup_cost +
515			parallel_setup_cost;
516		gather->plan.total_cost = top_plan->total_cost +
517			parallel_setup_cost + parallel_tuple_cost * top_plan->plan_rows;
518		gather->plan.plan_rows = top_plan->plan_rows;
519		gather->plan.plan_width = top_plan->plan_width;
520		gather->plan.parallel_aware = false;
521		gather->plan.parallel_safe = false;
522
523		/*
524		 * Delete the initplans' cost from top_plan.  We needn't add it to the
525		 * Gather node, since the above coding already included it there.
526		 */
527		SS_compute_initplan_cost(gather->plan.initPlan,
528								 &initplan_cost, &unsafe_initplans);
529		top_plan->startup_cost -= initplan_cost;
530		top_plan->total_cost -= initplan_cost;
531
532		/* use parallel mode for parallel plans. */
533		root->glob->parallelModeNeeded = true;
534
535		top_plan = &gather->plan;
536	}
537
538	/*
539	 * If any Params were generated, run through the plan tree and compute
540	 * each plan node's extParam/allParam sets.  Ideally we'd merge this into
541	 * set_plan_references' tree traversal, but for now it has to be separate
542	 * because we need to visit subplans before not after main plan.
543	 */
544	if (glob->paramExecTypes != NIL)
545	{
546		Assert(list_length(glob->subplans) == list_length(glob->subroots));
547		forboth(lp, glob->subplans, lr, glob->subroots)
548		{
549			Plan	   *subplan = (Plan *) lfirst(lp);
550			PlannerInfo *subroot = lfirst_node(PlannerInfo, lr);
551
552			SS_finalize_plan(subroot, subplan);
553		}
554		SS_finalize_plan(root, top_plan);
555	}
556
557	/* final cleanup of the plan */
558	Assert(glob->finalrtable == NIL);
559	Assert(glob->finalrteperminfos == NIL);
560	Assert(glob->finalrowmarks == NIL);
561	Assert(glob->resultRelations == NIL);
562	Assert(glob->appendRelations == NIL);
563	top_plan = set_plan_references(root, top_plan);
564	/* ... and the subplans (both regular subplans and initplans) */
565	Assert(list_length(glob->subplans) == list_length(glob->subroots));
566	forboth(lp, glob->subplans, lr, glob->subroots)
567	{
568		Plan	   *subplan = (Plan *) lfirst(lp);
569		PlannerInfo *subroot = lfirst_node(PlannerInfo, lr);
570
571		lfirst(lp) = set_plan_references(subroot, subplan);
572	}
573
574	/* build the PlannedStmt result */
575	result = makeNode(PlannedStmt);
576
577	result->commandType = parse->commandType;
578	result->queryId = parse->queryId;
579	result->hasReturning = (parse->returningList != NIL);
580	result->hasModifyingCTE = parse->hasModifyingCTE;
581	result->canSetTag = parse->canSetTag;
582	result->transientPlan = glob->transientPlan;
583	result->dependsOnRole = glob->dependsOnRole;
584	result->parallelModeNeeded = glob->parallelModeNeeded;
585	result->planTree = top_plan;
586	result->partPruneInfos = glob->partPruneInfos;
587	result->rtable = glob->finalrtable;
588	result->unprunableRelids = bms_difference(glob->allRelids,
589											  glob->prunableRelids);
590	result->permInfos = glob->finalrteperminfos;
591	result->resultRelations = glob->resultRelations;
592	result->appendRelations = glob->appendRelations;
593	result->subplans = glob->subplans;
594	result->rewindPlanIDs = glob->rewindPlanIDs;
595	result->rowMarks = glob->finalrowmarks;
596	result->relationOids = glob->relationOids;
597	result->invalItems = glob->invalItems;
598	result->paramExecTypes = glob->paramExecTypes;
599	/* utilityStmt should be null, but we might as well copy it */
600	result->utilityStmt = parse->utilityStmt;
601	result->stmt_location = parse->stmt_location;
602	result->stmt_len = parse->stmt_len;
603
604	result->jitFlags = PGJIT_NONE;
605	if (jit_enabled && jit_above_cost >= 0 &&
606		top_plan->total_cost > jit_above_cost)
607	{
608		result->jitFlags |= PGJIT_PERFORM;
609
610		/*
611		 * Decide how much effort should be put into generating better code.
612		 */
613		if (jit_optimize_above_cost >= 0 &&
614			top_plan->total_cost > jit_optimize_above_cost)
615			result->jitFlags |= PGJIT_OPT3;
616		if (jit_inline_above_cost >= 0 &&
617			top_plan->total_cost > jit_inline_above_cost)
618			result->jitFlags |= PGJIT_INLINE;
619
620		/*
621		 * Decide which operations should be JITed.
622		 */
623		if (jit_expressions)
624			result->jitFlags |= PGJIT_EXPR;
625		if (jit_tuple_deforming)
626			result->jitFlags |= PGJIT_DEFORM;
627	}
628
629	if (glob->partition_directory != NULL)
630		DestroyPartitionDirectory(glob->partition_directory);
631
632	return result;
633}
634
635
636/*--------------------
637 * subquery_planner
638 *	  Invokes the planner on a subquery.  We recurse to here for each
639 *	  sub-SELECT found in the query tree.
640 *
641 * glob is the global state for the current planner run.
642 * parse is the querytree produced by the parser & rewriter.
643 * parent_root is the immediate parent Query's info (NULL at the top level).
644 * hasRecursion is true if this is a recursive WITH query.
645 * tuple_fraction is the fraction of tuples we expect will be retrieved.
646 * tuple_fraction is interpreted as explained for grouping_planner, below.
647 * setops is used for set operation subqueries to provide the subquery with
648 * the context in which it's being used so that Paths correctly sorted for the
649 * set operation can be generated.  NULL when not planning a set operation
650 * child, or when a child of a set op that isn't interested in sorted input.
651 *
652 * Basically, this routine does the stuff that should only be done once
653 * per Query object.  It then calls grouping_planner.  At one time,
654 * grouping_planner could be invoked recursively on the same Query object;
655 * that's not currently true, but we keep the separation between the two
656 * routines anyway, in case we need it again someday.
657 *
658 * subquery_planner will be called recursively to handle sub-Query nodes
659 * found within the query's expressions and rangetable.
660 *
661 * Returns the PlannerInfo struct ("root") that contains all data generated
662 * while planning the subquery.  In particular, the Path(s) attached to
663 * the (UPPERREL_FINAL, NULL) upperrel represent our conclusions about the
664 * cheapest way(s) to implement the query.  The top level will select the
665 * best Path and pass it through createplan.c to produce a finished Plan.
666 *--------------------
667 */
668PlannerInfo *
669subquery_planner(PlannerGlobal *glob, Query *parse, PlannerInfo *parent_root,
670				 bool hasRecursion, double tuple_fraction,
671				 SetOperationStmt *setops)
672{
673	PlannerInfo *root;
674	List	   *newWithCheckOptions;
675	List	   *newHaving;
676	Bitmapset  *havingCollationConflicts;
677	int			havingIdx;
678	bool		hasOuterJoins;
679	bool		hasResultRTEs;
680	RelOptInfo *final_rel;
681	ListCell   *l;
682
683	/* Create a PlannerInfo data structure for this subquery */
684	root = makeNode(PlannerInfo);
685	root->parse = parse;
686	root->glob = glob;
687	root->query_level = parent_root ? parent_root->query_level + 1 : 1;
688	root->parent_root = parent_root;
689	root->plan_params = NIL;
690	root->outer_params = NULL;
691	root->planner_cxt = CurrentMemoryContext;
692	root->init_plans = NIL;
693	root->cte_plan_ids = NIL;
694	root->multiexpr_params = NIL;
695	root->join_domains = NIL;
696	root->eq_classes = NIL;
697	root->ec_merging_done = false;
698	root->last_rinfo_serial = 0;
699	root->all_result_relids =
700		parse->resultRelation ? bms_make_singleton(parse->resultRelation) : NULL;
701	root->leaf_result_relids = NULL;	/* we'll find out leaf-ness later */
702	root->append_rel_list = NIL;
703	root->row_identity_vars = NIL;
704	root->rowMarks = NIL;
705	memset(root->upper_rels, 0, sizeof(root->upper_rels));
706	memset(root->upper_targets, 0, sizeof(root->upper_targets));
707	root->processed_groupClause = NIL;
708	root->processed_distinctClause = NIL;
709	root->processed_tlist = NIL;
710	root->update_colnos = NIL;
711	root->grouping_map = NULL;
712	root->minmax_aggs = NIL;
713	root->qual_security_level = 0;
714	root->hasPseudoConstantQuals = false;
715	root->hasAlternativeSubPlans = false;
716	root->placeholdersFrozen = false;
717	root->hasRecursion = hasRecursion;
718	if (hasRecursion)
719		root->wt_param_id = assign_special_exec_param(root);
720	else
721		root->wt_param_id = -1;
722	root->non_recursive_path = NULL;
723	root->partColsUpdated = false;
724
725	/*
726	 * Create the top-level join domain.  This won't have valid contents until
727	 * deconstruct_jointree fills it in, but the node needs to exist before
728	 * that so we can build EquivalenceClasses referencing it.
729	 */
730	root->join_domains = list_make1(makeNode(JoinDomain));
731
732	/*
733	 * If there is a WITH list, process each WITH query and either convert it
734	 * to RTE_SUBQUERY RTE(s) or build an initplan SubPlan structure for it.
735	 */
736	if (parse->cteList)
737		SS_process_ctes(root);
738
739	/*
740	 * If it's a MERGE command, transform the joinlist as appropriate.
741	 */
742	transform_MERGE_to_join(parse);
743
744	/*
745	 * If the FROM clause is empty, replace it with a dummy RTE_RESULT RTE, so
746	 * that we don't need so many special cases to deal with that situation.
747	 */
748	replace_empty_jointree(parse);
749
750	/*
751	 * Look for ANY and EXISTS SubLinks in WHERE and JOIN/ON clauses, and try
752	 * to transform them into joins.  Note that this step does not descend
753	 * into subqueries; if we pull up any subqueries below, their SubLinks are
754	 * processed just before pulling them up.
755	 */
756	if (parse->hasSubLinks)
757		pull_up_sublinks(root);
758
759	/*
760	 * Scan the rangetable for function RTEs, do const-simplification on them,
761	 * and then inline them if possible (producing subqueries that might get
762	 * pulled up next).  Recursion issues here are handled in the same way as
763	 * for SubLinks.
764	 */
765	preprocess_function_rtes(root);
766
767	/*
768	 * Scan the rangetable for relations with virtual generated columns, and
769	 * replace all Var nodes in the query that reference these columns with
770	 * the generation expressions.  Recursion issues here are handled in the
771	 * same way as for SubLinks.
772	 */
773	parse = root->parse = expand_virtual_generated_columns(root);
774
775	/*
776	 * Check to see if any subqueries in the jointree can be merged into this
777	 * query.
778	 */
779	pull_up_subqueries(root);
780
781	/*
782	 * If this is a simple UNION ALL query, flatten it into an appendrel. We
783	 * do this now because it requires applying pull_up_subqueries to the leaf
784	 * queries of the UNION ALL, which weren't touched above because they
785	 * weren't referenced by the jointree (they will be after we do this).
786	 */
787	if (parse->setOperations)
788		flatten_simple_union_all(root);
789
790	/*
791	 * Survey the rangetable to see what kinds of entries are present.  We can
792	 * skip some later processing if relevant SQL features are not used; for
793	 * example if there are no JOIN RTEs we can avoid the expense of doing
794	 * flatten_join_alias_vars().  This must be done after we have finished
795	 * adding rangetable entries, of course.  (Note: actually, processing of
796	 * inherited or partitioned rels can cause RTEs for their child tables to
797	 * get added later; but those must all be RTE_RELATION entries, so they
798	 * don't invalidate the conclusions drawn here.)
799	 */
800	root->hasJoinRTEs = false;
801	root->hasLateralRTEs = false;
802	root->group_rtindex = 0;
803	hasOuterJoins = false;
804	hasResultRTEs = false;
805	foreach(l, parse->rtable)
806	{
807		RangeTblEntry *rte = lfirst_node(RangeTblEntry, l);
808
809		switch (rte->rtekind)
810		{
811			case RTE_RELATION:
812				if (rte->inh)
813				{
814					/*
815					 * Check to see if the relation actually has any children;
816					 * if not, clear the inh flag so we can treat it as a
817					 * plain base relation.
818					 *
819					 * Note: this could give a false-positive result, if the
820					 * rel once had children but no longer does.  We used to
821					 * be able to clear rte->inh later on when we discovered
822					 * that, but no more; we have to handle such cases as
823					 * full-fledged inheritance.
824					 */
825					rte->inh = has_subclass(rte->relid);
826				}
827				break;
828			case RTE_JOIN:
829				root->hasJoinRTEs = true;
830				if (IS_OUTER_JOIN(rte->jointype))
831					hasOuterJoins = true;
832				break;
833			case RTE_RESULT:
834				hasResultRTEs = true;
835				break;
836			case RTE_GROUP:
837				Assert(parse->hasGroupRTE);
838				root->group_rtindex = list_cell_number(parse->rtable, l) + 1;
839				break;
840			default:
841				/* No work here for other RTE types */
842				break;
843		}
844
845		if (rte->lateral)
846			root->hasLateralRTEs = true;
847
848		/*
849		 * We can also determine the maximum security level required for any
850		 * securityQuals now.  Addition of inheritance-child RTEs won't affect
851		 * this, because child tables don't have their own securityQuals; see
852		 * expand_single_inheritance_child().
853		 */
854		if (rte->securityQuals)
855			root->qual_security_level = Max(root->qual_security_level,
856											list_length(rte->securityQuals));
857	}
858
859	/*
860	 * If we have now verified that the query target relation is
861	 * non-inheriting, mark it as a leaf target.
862	 */
863	if (parse->resultRelation)
864	{
865		RangeTblEntry *rte = rt_fetch(parse->resultRelation, parse->rtable);
866
867		if (!rte->inh)
868			root->leaf_result_relids =
869				bms_make_singleton(parse->resultRelation);
870	}
871
872	/*
873	 * This would be a convenient time to check access permissions for all
874	 * relations mentioned in the query, since it would be better to fail now,
875	 * before doing any detailed planning.  However, for historical reasons,
876	 * we leave this to be done at executor startup.
877	 *
878	 * Note, however, that we do need to check access permissions for any view
879	 * relations mentioned in the query, in order to prevent information being
880	 * leaked by selectivity estimation functions, which only check view owner
881	 * permissions on underlying tables (see all_rows_selectable() and its
882	 * callers).  This is a little ugly, because it means that access
883	 * permissions for views will be checked twice, which is another reason
884	 * why it would be better to do all the ACL checks here.
885	 */
886	foreach(l, parse->rtable)
887	{
888		RangeTblEntry *rte = lfirst_node(RangeTblEntry, l);
889
890		if (rte->perminfoindex != 0 &&
891			rte->relkind == RELKIND_VIEW)
892		{
893			RTEPermissionInfo *perminfo;
894			bool		result;
895
896			perminfo = getRTEPermissionInfo(parse->rteperminfos, rte);
897			result = ExecCheckOneRelPerms(perminfo);
898			if (!result)
899				aclcheck_error(ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV, OBJECT_VIEW,
900							   get_rel_name(perminfo->relid));
901		}
902	}
903
904	/*
905	 * Preprocess RowMark information.  We need to do this after subquery
906	 * pullup, so that all base relations are present.
907	 */
908	preprocess_rowmarks(root);
909
910	/*
911	 * Set hasHavingQual to remember if HAVING clause is present.  Needed
912	 * because preprocess_expression will reduce a constant-true condition to
913	 * an empty qual list ... but "HAVING TRUE" is not a semantic no-op.
914	 */
915	root->hasHavingQual = (parse->havingQual != NULL);
916
917	/*
918	 * Do expression preprocessing on targetlist and quals, as well as other
919	 * random expressions in the querytree.  Note that we do not need to
920	 * handle sort/group expressions explicitly, because they are actually
921	 * part of the targetlist.
922	 */
923	parse->targetList = (List *)
924		preprocess_expression(root, (Node *) parse->targetList,
925							  EXPRKIND_TARGET);
926
927	newWithCheckOptions = NIL;
928	foreach(l, parse->withCheckOptions)
929	{
930		WithCheckOption *wco = lfirst_node(WithCheckOption, l);
931
932		wco->qual = preprocess_expression(root, wco->qual,
933										  EXPRKIND_QUAL);
934		if (wco->qual != NULL)
935			newWithCheckOptions = lappend(newWithCheckOptions, wco);
936	}
937	parse->withCheckOptions = newWithCheckOptions;
938
939	parse->returningList = (List *)
940		preprocess_expression(root, (Node *) parse->returningList,
941							  EXPRKIND_TARGET);
942
943	preprocess_qual_conditions(root, (Node *) parse->jointree);
944
945	parse->havingQual = preprocess_expression(root, parse->havingQual,
946											  EXPRKIND_QUAL);
947
948	foreach(l, parse->windowClause)
949	{
950		WindowClause *wc = lfirst_node(WindowClause, l);
951
952		/* partitionClause/orderClause are sort/group expressions */
953		wc->startOffset = preprocess_expression(root, wc->startOffset,
954												EXPRKIND_LIMIT);
955		wc->endOffset = preprocess_expression(root, wc->endOffset,
956											  EXPRKIND_LIMIT);
957	}
958
959	parse->limitOffset = preprocess_expression(root, parse->limitOffset,
960											   EXPRKIND_LIMIT);
961	parse->limitCount = preprocess_expression(root, parse->limitCount,
962											  EXPRKIND_LIMIT);
963
964	if (parse->onConflict)
965	{
966		parse->onConflict->arbiterElems = (List *)
967			preprocess_expression(root,
968								  (Node *) parse->onConflict->arbiterElems,
969								  EXPRKIND_ARBITER_ELEM);
970		parse->onConflict->arbiterWhere =
971			preprocess_expression(root,
972								  parse->onConflict->arbiterWhere,
973								  EXPRKIND_QUAL);
974		parse->onConflict->onConflictSet = (List *)
975			preprocess_expression(root,
976								  (Node *) parse->onConflict->onConflictSet,
977								  EXPRKIND_TARGET);
978		parse->onConflict->onConflictWhere =
979			preprocess_expression(root,
980								  parse->onConflict->onConflictWhere,
981								  EXPRKIND_QUAL);
982		/* exclRelTlist contains only Vars, so no preprocessing needed */
983	}
984
985	foreach(l, parse->mergeActionList)
986	{
987		MergeAction *action = (MergeAction *) lfirst(l);
988
989		action->targetList = (List *)
990			preprocess_expression(root,
991								  (Node *) action->targetList,
992								  EXPRKIND_TARGET);
993		action->qual =
994			preprocess_expression(root,
995								  (Node *) action->qual,
996								  EXPRKIND_QUAL);
997	}
998
999	parse->mergeJoinCondition =
1000		preprocess_expression(root, parse->mergeJoinCondition, EXPRKIND_QUAL);
1001
1002	root->append_rel_list = (List *)
1003		preprocess_expression(root, (Node *) root->append_rel_list,
1004							  EXPRKIND_APPINFO);
1005
1006	/* Also need to preprocess expressions within RTEs */
1007	foreach(l, parse->rtable)
1008	{
1009		RangeTblEntry *rte = lfirst_node(RangeTblEntry, l);
1010		int			kind;
1011		ListCell   *lcsq;
1012
1013		if (rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION)
1014		{
1015			if (rte->tablesample)
1016				rte->tablesample = (TableSampleClause *)
1017					preprocess_expression(root,
1018										  (Node *) rte->tablesample,
1019										  EXPRKIND_TABLESAMPLE);
1020		}
1021		else if (rte->rtekind == RTE_SUBQUERY)
1022		{
1023			/*
1024			 * We don't want to do all preprocessing yet on the subquery's
1025			 * expressions, since that will happen when we plan it.  But if it
1026			 * contains any join aliases of our level, those have to get
1027			 * expanded now, because planning of the subquery won't do it.
1028			 * That's only possible if the subquery is LATERAL.
1029			 */
1030			if (rte->lateral && root->hasJoinRTEs)
1031				rte->subquery = (Query *)
1032					flatten_join_alias_vars(root, root->parse,
1033											(Node *) rte->subquery);
1034		}
1035		else if (rte->rtekind == RTE_FUNCTION)
1036		{
1037			/* Preprocess the function expression(s) fully */
1038			kind = rte->lateral ? EXPRKIND_RTFUNC_LATERAL : EXPRKIND_RTFUNC;
1039			rte->functions = (List *)
1040				preprocess_expression(root, (Node *) rte->functions, kind);
1041		}
1042		else if (rte->rtekind == RTE_TABLEFUNC)
1043		{
1044			/* Preprocess the function expression(s) fully */
1045			kind = rte->lateral ? EXPRKIND_TABLEFUNC_LATERAL : EXPRKIND_TABLEFUNC;
1046			rte->tablefunc = (TableFunc *)
1047				preprocess_expression(root, (Node *) rte->tablefunc, kind);
1048		}
1049		else if (rte->rtekind == RTE_VALUES)
1050		{
1051			/* Preprocess the values lists fully */
1052			kind = rte->lateral ? EXPRKIND_VALUES_LATERAL : EXPRKIND_VALUES;
1053			rte->values_lists = (List *)
1054				preprocess_expression(root, (Node *) rte->values_lists, kind);
1055		}
1056		else if (rte->rtekind == RTE_GROUP)
1057		{
1058			/* Preprocess the groupexprs list fully */
1059			rte->groupexprs = (List *)
1060				preprocess_expression(root, (Node *) rte->groupexprs,
1061									  EXPRKIND_GROUPEXPR);
1062		}
1063
1064		/*
1065		 * Process each element of the securityQuals list as if it were a
1066		 * separate qual expression (as indeed it is).  We need to do it this
1067		 * way to get proper canonicalization of AND/OR structure.  Note that
1068		 * this converts each element into an implicit-AND sublist.
1069		 */
1070		foreach(lcsq, rte->securityQuals)
1071		{
1072			lfirst(lcsq) = preprocess_expression(root,
1073												 (Node *) lfirst(lcsq),
1074												 EXPRKIND_QUAL);
1075		}
1076	}
1077
1078	/*
1079	 * Now that we are done preprocessing expressions, and in particular done
1080	 * flattening join alias variables, get rid of the joinaliasvars lists.
1081	 * They no longer match what expressions in the rest of the tree look
1082	 * like, because we have not preprocessed expressions in those lists (and
1083	 * do not want to; for example, expanding a SubLink there would result in
1084	 * a useless unreferenced subplan).  Leaving them in place simply creates
1085	 * a hazard for later scans of the tree.  We could try to prevent that by
1086	 * using QTW_IGNORE_JOINALIASES in every tree scan done after this point,
1087	 * but that doesn't sound very reliable.
1088	 */
1089	if (root->hasJoinRTEs)
1090	{
1091		foreach(l, parse->rtable)
1092		{
1093			RangeTblEntry *rte = lfirst_node(RangeTblEntry, l);
1094
1095			rte->joinaliasvars = NIL;
1096		}
1097	}
1098
1099	/*
1100	 * Before we flatten GROUP Vars, check which HAVING clauses have collation
1101	 * conflicts.  When GROUP BY uses a nondeterministic collation, values
1102	 * that are "equal" for grouping may be distinguishable under a different
1103	 * collation.  If such a HAVING clause were moved to WHERE, it would
1104	 * filter individual rows before grouping, potentially eliminating some
1105	 * members of a group and thereby changing aggregate results.
1106	 *
1107	 * We do this check before flatten_group_exprs because we can easily
1108	 * identify grouping expressions by checking whether a Var references
1109	 * RTE_GROUP, and such Vars directly carry the GROUP BY collation as their
1110	 * varcollid.  After flattening, these Vars are replaced by the underlying
1111	 * expressions, and we would have to match expressions in the HAVING
1112	 * clause back to grouping expressions, which is much more complex.
1113	 */
1114	if (parse->hasGroupRTE)
1115		havingCollationConflicts =
1116			find_having_collation_conflicts(parse, root->group_rtindex);
1117	else
1118		havingCollationConflicts = NULL;
1119
1120	/*
1121	 * Replace any Vars in the subquery's targetlist and havingQual that
1122	 * reference GROUP outputs with the underlying grouping expressions.
1123	 *
1124	 * Note that we need to perform this replacement after we've preprocessed
1125	 * the grouping expressions.  This is to ensure that there is only one
1126	 * instance of SubPlan for each SubLink contained within the grouping
1127	 * expressions.
1128	 */
1129	if (parse->hasGroupRTE)
1130	{
1131		parse->targetList = (List *)
1132			flatten_group_exprs(root, root->parse, (Node *) parse->targetList);
1133		parse->havingQual =
1134			flatten_group_exprs(root, root->parse, parse->havingQual);
1135	}
1136
1137	/* Constant-folding might have removed all set-returning functions */
1138	if (parse->hasTargetSRFs)
1139		parse->hasTargetSRFs = expression_returns_set((Node *) parse->targetList);
1140
1141	/*
1142	 * If we have grouping sets, expand the groupingSets tree of this query to
1143	 * a flat list of grouping sets.  We need to do this before optimizing
1144	 * HAVING, since we can't easily tell if there's an empty grouping set
1145	 * until we have this representation.
1146	 */
1147	if (parse->groupingSets)
1148	{
1149		parse->groupingSets =
1150			expand_grouping_sets(parse->groupingSets, parse->groupDistinct, -1);
1151	}
1152
1153	/*
1154	 * In some cases we may want to transfer a HAVING clause into WHERE. We
1155	 * cannot do so if the HAVING clause contains aggregates (obviously) or
1156	 * volatile functions (since a HAVING clause is supposed to be executed
1157	 * only once per group).  We also can't do this if there are any grouping
1158	 * sets and the clause references any columns that are nullable by the
1159	 * grouping sets; the nulled values of those columns are not available
1160	 * before the grouping step.  (The test on groupClause might seem wrong,
1161	 * but it's okay: it's just an optimization to avoid running pull_varnos
1162	 * when there cannot be any Vars in the HAVING clause.)
1163	 *
1164	 * We also cannot do this if the HAVING clause uses a different collation
1165	 * than the GROUP BY for any grouping expression whose GROUP BY collation
1166	 * is nondeterministic.  This is detected before flatten_group_exprs (see
1167	 * find_having_collation_conflicts above) and recorded in the
1168	 * havingCollationConflicts bitmapset.  The bitmapset indexes remain valid
1169	 * here because flatten_group_exprs uses expression_tree_mutator, which
1170	 * preserves the list length and ordering of havingQual.
1171	 *
1172	 * Also, it may be that the clause is so expensive to execute that we're
1173	 * better off doing it only once per group, despite the loss of
1174	 * selectivity.  This is hard to estimate short of doing the entire
1175	 * planning process twice, so we use a heuristic: clauses containing
1176	 * subplans are left in HAVING.  Otherwise, we move or copy the HAVING
1177	 * clause into WHERE, in hopes of eliminating tuples before aggregation
1178	 * instead of after.
1179	 *
1180	 * If the query has no empty grouping set then we can simply move such a
1181	 * clause into WHERE; any group that fails the clause will not be in the
1182	 * output because none of its tuples will reach the grouping or
1183	 * aggregation stage.  Otherwise we have to keep the clause in HAVING to
1184	 * ensure that we don't emit a bogus aggregated row.  But then the HAVING
1185	 * clause must be degenerate (variable-free), so we can copy it into WHERE
1186	 * so that query_planner() can use it in a gating Result node. (This could
1187	 * be done better, but it seems not worth optimizing.)
1188	 *
1189	 * Note that a HAVING clause may contain expressions that are not fully
1190	 * preprocessed.  This can happen if these expressions are part of
1191	 * grouping items.  In such cases, they are replaced with GROUP Vars in
1192	 * the parser and then replaced back after we're done with expression
1193	 * preprocessing on havingQual.  This is not an issue if the clause
1194	 * remains in HAVING, because these expressions will be matched to lower
1195	 * target items in setrefs.c.  However, if the clause is moved or copied
1196	 * into WHERE, we need to ensure that these expressions are fully
1197	 * preprocessed.
1198	 *
1199	 * Note that both havingQual and parse->jointree->quals are in
1200	 * implicitly-ANDed-list form at this point, even though they are declared
1201	 * as Node *.
1202	 */
1203	newHaving = NIL;
1204	havingIdx = 0;
1205	foreach(l, (List *) parse->havingQual)
1206	{
1207		Node	   *havingclause = (Node *) lfirst(l);
1208
1209		if (contain_agg_clause(havingclause) ||
1210			contain_volatile_functions(havingclause) ||
1211			contain_subplans(havingclause) ||
1212			bms_is_member(havingIdx, havingCollationConflicts) ||
1213			(parse->groupClause && parse->groupingSets &&
1214			 bms_is_member(root->group_rtindex, pull_varnos(root, havingclause))))
1215		{
1216			/* keep it in HAVING */
1217			newHaving = lappend(newHaving, havingclause);
1218		}
1219		else if (parse->groupClause &&
1220				 (parse->groupingSets == NIL ||
1221				  (List *) linitial(parse->groupingSets) != NIL))
1222		{
1223			/* There is GROUP BY, but no empty grouping set */
1224			Node	   *whereclause;
1225
1226			/* Preprocess the HAVING clause fully */
1227			whereclause = preprocess_expression(root, havingclause,
1228												EXPRKIND_QUAL);
1229			/* ... and move it to WHERE */
1230			parse->jointree->quals = (Node *)
1231				list_concat((List *) parse->jointree->quals,
1232							(List *) whereclause);
1233		}
1234		else
1235		{
1236			/* There is an empty grouping set (perhaps implicitly) */
1237			Node	   *whereclause;
1238
1239			/* Preprocess the HAVING clause fully */
1240			whereclause = preprocess_expression(root, copyObject(havingclause),
1241												EXPRKIND_QUAL);
1242			/* ... and put a copy in WHERE */
1243			parse->jointree->quals = (Node *)
1244				list_concat((List *) parse->jointree->quals,
1245							(List *) whereclause);
1246			/* ... and also keep it in HAVING */
1247			newHaving = lappend(newHaving, havingclause);
1248		}
1249
1250		havingIdx++;
1251	}
1252	parse->havingQual = (Node *) newHaving;
1253
1254	/*
1255	 * If we have any outer joins, try to reduce them to plain inner joins.
1256	 * This step is most easily done after we've done expression
1257	 * preprocessing.
1258	 */
1259	if (hasOuterJoins)
1260		reduce_outer_joins(root);
1261
1262	/*
1263	 * If we have any RTE_RESULT relations, see if they can be deleted from
1264	 * the jointree.  We also rely on this processing to flatten single-child
1265	 * FromExprs underneath outer joins.  This step is most effectively done
1266	 * after we've done expression preprocessing and outer join reduction.
1267	 */
1268	if (hasResultRTEs || hasOuterJoins)
1269		remove_useless_result_rtes(root);
1270
1271	/*
1272	 * Do the main planning.
1273	 */
1274	grouping_planner(root, tuple_fraction, setops);
1275
1276	/*
1277	 * Capture the set of outer-level param IDs we have access to, for use in
1278	 * extParam/allParam calculations later.
1279	 */
1280	SS_identify_outer_params(root);
1281
1282	/*
1283	 * If any initPlans were created in this query level, adjust the surviving
1284	 * Paths' costs and parallel-safety flags to account for them.  The
1285	 * initPlans won't actually get attached to the plan tree till
1286	 * create_plan() runs, but we must include their effects now.
1287	 */
1288	final_rel = fetch_upper_rel(root, UPPERREL_FINAL, NULL);
1289	SS_charge_for_initplans(root, final_rel);
1290
1291	/*
1292	 * Make sure we've identified the cheapest Path for the final rel.  (By
1293	 * doing this here not in grouping_planner, we include initPlan costs in
1294	 * the decision, though it's unlikely that will change anything.)
1295	 */
1296	set_cheapest(final_rel);
1297
1298	return root;
1299}
1300
1301/*
1302 * preprocess_expression
1303 *		Do subquery_planner's preprocessing work for an expression,
1304 *		which can be a targetlist, a WHERE clause (including JOIN/ON
1305 *		conditions), a HAVING clause, or a few other things.
1306 */
1307static Node *
1308preprocess_expression(PlannerInfo *root, Node *expr, int kind)
1309{
1310	/*
1311	 * Fall out quickly if expression is empty.  This occurs often enough to
1312	 * be worth checking.  Note that null->null is the correct conversion for
1313	 * implicit-AND result format, too.
1314	 */
1315	if (expr == NULL)
1316		return NULL;
1317
1318	/*
1319	 * If the query has any join RTEs, replace join alias variables with
1320	 * base-relation variables.  We must do this first, since any expressions
1321	 * we may extract from the joinaliasvars lists have not been preprocessed.
1322	 * For example, if we did this after sublink processing, sublinks expanded
1323	 * out from join aliases would not get processed.  But we can skip this in
1324	 * non-lateral RTE functions, VALUES lists, and TABLESAMPLE clauses, since
1325	 * they can't contain any Vars of the current query level.
1326	 */
1327	if (root->hasJoinRTEs &&
1328		!(kind == EXPRKIND_RTFUNC ||
1329		  kind == EXPRKIND_VALUES ||
1330		  kind == EXPRKIND_TABLESAMPLE ||
1331		  kind == EXPRKIND_TABLEFUNC))
1332		expr = flatten_join_alias_vars(root, root->parse, expr);
1333
1334	/*
1335	 * Simplify constant expressions.  For function RTEs, this was already
1336	 * done by preprocess_function_rtes.  (But note we must do it again for
1337	 * EXPRKIND_RTFUNC_LATERAL, because those might by now contain
1338	 * un-simplified subexpressions inserted by flattening of subqueries or
1339	 * join alias variables.)
1340	 *
1341	 * Note: an essential effect of this is to convert named-argument function
1342	 * calls to positional notation and insert the current actual values of
1343	 * any default arguments for functions.  To ensure that happens, we *must*
1344	 * process all expressions here.  Previous PG versions sometimes skipped
1345	 * const-simplification if it didn't seem worth the trouble, but we can't
1346	 * do that anymore.
1347	 *
1348	 * Note: this also flattens nested AND and OR expressions into N-argument
1349	 * form.  All processing of a qual expression after this point must be
1350	 * careful to maintain AND/OR flatness --- that is, do not generate a tree
1351	 * with AND directly under AND, nor OR directly under OR.
1352	 */
1353	if (kind != EXPRKIND_RTFUNC)
1354		expr = eval_const_expressions(root, expr);
1355
1356	/*
1357	 * If it's a qual or havingQual, canonicalize it.
1358	 */
1359	if (kind == EXPRKIND_QUAL)
1360	{
1361		expr = (Node *) canonicalize_qual((Expr *) expr, false);
1362
1363#ifdef OPTIMIZER_DEBUG
1364		printf("After canonicalize_qual()\n");
1365		pprint(expr);
1366#endif
1367	}
1368
1369	/*
1370	 * Check for ANY ScalarArrayOpExpr with Const arrays and set the
1371	 * hashfuncid of any that might execute more quickly by using hash lookups
1372	 * instead of a linear search.
1373	 */
1374	if (kind == EXPRKIND_QUAL || kind == EXPRKIND_TARGET)
1375	{
1376		convert_saop_to_hashed_saop(expr);
1377	}
1378
1379	/* Expand SubLinks to SubPlans */
1380	if (root->parse->hasSubLinks)
1381		expr = SS_process_sublinks(root, expr, (kind == EXPRKIND_QUAL));
1382
1383	/*
1384	 * XXX do not insert anything here unless you have grokked the comments in
1385	 * SS_replace_correlation_vars ...
1386	 */
1387
1388	/* Replace uplevel vars with Param nodes (this IS possible in VALUES) */
1389	if (root->query_level > 1)
1390		expr = SS_replace_correlation_vars(root, expr);
1391
1392	/*
1393	 * If it's a qual or havingQual, convert it to implicit-AND format. (We
1394	 * don't want to do this before eval_const_expressions, since the latter
1395	 * would be unable to simplify a top-level AND correctly. Also,
1396	 * SS_process_sublinks expects explicit-AND format.)
1397	 */
1398	if (kind == EXPRKIND_QUAL)
1399		expr = (Node *) make_ands_implicit((Expr *) expr);
1400
1401	return expr;
1402}
1403
1404/*
1405 * preprocess_qual_conditions
1406 *		Recursively scan the query's jointree and do subquery_planner's
1407 *		preprocessing work on each qual condition found therein.
1408 */
1409static void
1410preprocess_qual_conditions(PlannerInfo *root, Node *jtnode)
1411{
1412	if (jtnode == NULL)
1413		return;
1414	if (IsA(jtnode, RangeTblRef))
1415	{
1416		/* nothing to do here */
1417	}
1418	else if (IsA(jtnode, FromExpr))
1419	{
1420		FromExpr   *f = (FromExpr *) jtnode;
1421		ListCell   *l;
1422
1423		foreach(l, f->fromlist)
1424			preprocess_qual_conditions(root, lfirst(l));
1425
1426		f->quals = preprocess_expression(root, f->quals, EXPRKIND_QUAL);
1427	}
1428	else if (IsA(jtnode, JoinExpr))
1429	{
1430		JoinExpr   *j = (JoinExpr *) jtnode;
1431
1432		preprocess_qual_conditions(root, j->larg);
1433		preprocess_qual_conditions(root, j->rarg);
1434
1435		j->quals = preprocess_expression(root, j->quals, EXPRKIND_QUAL);
1436	}
1437	else
1438		elog(ERROR, "unrecognized node type: %d",
1439			 (int) nodeTag(jtnode));
1440}
1441
1442/*
1443 * find_having_collation_conflicts
1444 *	  Identify HAVING clauses that must not be moved to WHERE due to collation
1445 *	  mismatches with GROUP BY.
1446 *
1447 * This must be called before flatten_group_exprs, while the HAVING clause
1448 * still contains GROUP Vars (Vars referencing RTE_GROUP).  These GROUP Vars
1449 * carry the GROUP BY collation as their varcollid.  A GROUP Var with a
1450 * nondeterministic varcollid conflicts whenever some collation-aware ancestor
1451 * on its path applies a different inputcollid: that operator would distinguish
1452 * values which the GROUP BY considers equal, so the clause is unsafe to push
1453 * to WHERE.
1454 *
1455 * Returns a Bitmapset of zero-based indexes into the havingQual list for
1456 * clauses that have collation conflicts and must stay in HAVING.
1457 */
1458static Bitmapset *
1459find_having_collation_conflicts(Query *parse, Index group_rtindex)
1460{
1461	Bitmapset  *result = NULL;
1462	having_collation_ctx ctx;
1463	int			idx;
1464
1465	if (parse->havingQual == NULL)
1466		return NULL;
1467
1468	ctx.group_rtindex = group_rtindex;
1469	ctx.ancestor_collids = NIL;
1470
1471	idx = 0;
1472	foreach_ptr(Node, clause, (List *) parse->havingQual)
1473	{
1474		if (having_collation_conflict_walker(clause, &ctx))
1475			result = bms_add_member(result, idx);
1476		idx++;
1477		Assert(ctx.ancestor_collids == NIL);
1478	}
1479
1480	return result;
1481}
1482
1483/*
1484 * Walker function for find_having_collation_conflicts.
1485 *
1486 * Walk the clause top-down, maintaining a stack of inputcollids contributed
1487 * by collation-aware ancestors.  At each GROUP Var with a nondeterministic
1488 * varcollid, the clause has a conflict if any ancestor's inputcollid differs
1489 * from the GROUP Var's varcollid.  Most collation-aware nodes expose their
1490 * inputcollid through exprInputCollation().  Two structural exceptions need
1491 * special handling:
1492 *
1493 * - RowCompareExpr carries one inputcollid per column in inputcollids[], so we
1494 *   descend into its (largs[i], rargs[i]) pairs explicitly with the matching
1495 *   collation pushed onto the stack.
1496 *
1497 * - A simple CASE (CaseExpr with a non-NULL arg) holds the arg outside the
1498 *   WHEN's OpExpr, even though the WHEN's OpExpr is the place where the
1499 *   comparison's inputcollid lives.  Parse analysis builds each WHEN as
1500 *   "OpExpr(CaseTestExpr op val)" -- the CaseTestExpr is a placeholder for
1501 *   the arg.  Before walking cexpr->arg we therefore push every WHEN's
1502 *   inputcollid onto the ancestor stack, so a GROUP Var at the arg is
1503 *   checked against the same collations the WHEN comparisons would apply.
1504 *   The WHEN bodies and defresult are then walked under the unchanged stack
1505 *   so their own collation contexts are picked up by the default path.
1506 */
1507static bool
1508having_collation_conflict_walker(Node *node, having_collation_ctx *ctx)
1509{
1510	Oid			this_collid;
1511	bool		result;
1512
1513	if (node == NULL)
1514		return false;
1515
1516	if (IsA(node, Var))
1517	{
1518		Var		   *var = (Var *) node;
1519
1520		/* We should not see any upper-level Vars here */
1521		Assert(var->varlevelsup == 0);
1522
1523		if (var->varno == ctx->group_rtindex &&
1524			OidIsValid(var->varcollid) &&
1525			!get_collation_isdeterministic(var->varcollid))
1526		{
1527			foreach_oid(collid, ctx->ancestor_collids)
1528			{
1529				if (collid != var->varcollid)
1530					return true;
1531			}
1532		}
1533		return false;
1534	}
1535
1536	if (IsA(node, RowCompareExpr))
1537	{
1538		RowCompareExpr *rcexpr = (RowCompareExpr *) node;
1539		ListCell   *lc_l;
1540		ListCell   *lc_r;
1541		ListCell   *lc_c;
1542
1543		/*
1544		 * Each column of a row comparison is compared under its own
1545		 * inputcollids[i].  Walk each (largs[i], rargs[i]) pair with that
1546		 * collation pushed, so a Var in column i is checked against the
1547		 * collation that actually applies to it.
1548		 */
1549		forthree(lc_l, rcexpr->largs,
1550				 lc_r, rcexpr->rargs,
1551				 lc_c, rcexpr->inputcollids)
1552		{
1553			Oid			collid = lfirst_oid(lc_c);
1554			bool		found;
1555
1556			if (OidIsValid(collid))
1557				ctx->ancestor_collids = lappend_oid(ctx->ancestor_collids,
1558													collid);
1559
1560			found = having_collation_conflict_walker((Node *) lfirst(lc_l),
1561													 ctx) ||
1562				having_collation_conflict_walker((Node *) lfirst(lc_r),
1563												 ctx);
1564
1565			if (OidIsValid(collid))
1566				ctx->ancestor_collids =
1567					list_delete_last(ctx->ancestor_collids);
1568
1569			if (found)
1570				return true;
1571		}
1572		return false;
1573	}
1574
1575	if (IsA(node, CaseExpr) && ((CaseExpr *) node)->arg != NULL)
1576	{
1577		CaseExpr   *cexpr = (CaseExpr *) node;
1578		int			saved_len = list_length(ctx->ancestor_collids);
1579		bool		found;
1580
1581		/*
1582		 * Push every WHEN's inputcollid before walking cexpr->arg, since each
1583		 * WHEN implicitly compares the arg under that inputcollid.
1584		 */
1585		foreach_node(CaseWhen, cw, cexpr->args)
1586		{
1587			Oid			collid = exprInputCollation((Node *) cw->expr);
1588
1589			if (OidIsValid(collid))
1590				ctx->ancestor_collids = lappend_oid(ctx->ancestor_collids,
1591													collid);
1592		}
1593
1594		found = having_collation_conflict_walker((Node *) cexpr->arg, ctx);
1595
1596		ctx->ancestor_collids = list_truncate(ctx->ancestor_collids,
1597											  saved_len);
1598
1599		if (found)
1600			return true;
1601
1602		/*
1603		 * Walk the WHEN bodies and defresult under the unchanged ancestor
1604		 * stack; any inputcollids inside them are picked up by the default
1605		 * path.
1606		 */
1607		foreach_node(CaseWhen, cw, cexpr->args)
1608		{
1609			if (having_collation_conflict_walker((Node *) cw->expr, ctx) ||
1610				having_collation_conflict_walker((Node *) cw->result, ctx))
1611				return true;
1612		}
1613		return having_collation_conflict_walker((Node *) cexpr->defresult,
1614												ctx);
1615	}
1616
1617	this_collid = exprInputCollation(node);
1618	if (OidIsValid(this_collid))
1619		ctx->ancestor_collids = lappend_oid(ctx->ancestor_collids,
1620											this_collid);
1621
1622	result = expression_tree_walker(node, having_collation_conflict_walker,
1623									ctx);
1624
1625	if (OidIsValid(this_collid))
1626		ctx->ancestor_collids = list_delete_last(ctx->ancestor_collids);
1627
1628	return result;
1629}
1630
1631/*
1632 * preprocess_phv_expression
1633 *	  Do preprocessing on a PlaceHolderVar expression that's been pulled up.
1634 *
1635 * If a LATERAL subquery references an output of another subquery, and that
1636 * output must be wrapped in a PlaceHolderVar because of an intermediate outer
1637 * join, then we'll push the PlaceHolderVar expression down into the subquery
1638 * and later pull it back up during find_lateral_references, which runs after
1639 * subquery_planner has preprocessed all the expressions that were in the
1640 * current query level to start with.  So we need to preprocess it then.
1641 */
1642Expr *
1643preprocess_phv_expression(PlannerInfo *root, Expr *expr)
1644{
1645	return (Expr *) preprocess_expression(root, (Node *) expr, EXPRKIND_PHV);
1646}
1647
1648/*--------------------
1649 * grouping_planner
1650 *	  Perform planning steps related to grouping, aggregation, etc.
1651 *
1652 * This function adds all required top-level processing to the scan/join
1653 * Path(s) produced by query_planner.
1654 *
1655 * tuple_fraction is the fraction of tuples we expect will be retrieved.
1656 * tuple_fraction is interpreted as follows:
1657 *	  0: expect all tuples to be retrieved (normal case)
1658 *	  0 < tuple_fraction < 1: expect the given fraction of tuples available
1659 *		from the plan to be retrieved
1660 *	  tuple_fraction >= 1: tuple_fraction is the absolute number of tuples
1661 *		expected to be retrieved (ie, a LIMIT specification).
1662 * setops is used for set operation subqueries to provide the subquery with
1663 * the context in which it's being used so that Paths correctly sorted for the
1664 * set operation can be generated.  NULL when not planning a set operation
1665 * child, or when a child of a set op that isn't interested in sorted input.
1666 *
1667 * Returns nothing; the useful output is in the Paths we attach to the
1668 * (UPPERREL_FINAL, NULL) upperrel in *root.  In addition,
1669 * root->processed_tlist contains the final processed targetlist.
1670 *
1671 * Note that we have not done set_cheapest() on the final rel; it's convenient
1672 * to leave this to the caller.
1673 *--------------------
1674 */
1675static void
1676grouping_planner(PlannerInfo *root, double tuple_fraction,
1677				 SetOperationStmt *setops)
1678{
1679	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
1680	int64		offset_est = 0;
1681	int64		count_est = 0;
1682	double		limit_tuples = -1.0;
1683	bool		have_postponed_srfs = false;
1684	PathTarget *final_target;
1685	List	   *final_targets;
1686	List	   *final_targets_contain_srfs;
1687	bool		final_target_parallel_safe;
1688	RelOptInfo *current_rel;
1689	RelOptInfo *final_rel;
1690	FinalPathExtraData extra;
1691	ListCell   *lc;
1692
1693	/* Tweak caller-supplied tuple_fraction if have LIMIT/OFFSET */
1694	if (parse->limitCount || parse->limitOffset)
1695	{
1696		tuple_fraction = preprocess_limit(root, tuple_fraction,
1697										  &offset_est, &count_est);
1698
1699		/*
1700		 * If we have a known LIMIT, and don't have an unknown OFFSET, we can
1701		 * estimate the effects of using a bounded sort.
1702		 */
1703		if (count_est > 0 && offset_est >= 0)
1704			limit_tuples = (double) count_est + (double) offset_est;
1705	}
1706
1707	/* Make tuple_fraction accessible to lower-level routines */
1708	root->tuple_fraction = tuple_fraction;
1709
1710	if (parse->setOperations)
1711	{
1712		/*
1713		 * Construct Paths for set operations.  The results will not need any
1714		 * work except perhaps a top-level sort and/or LIMIT.  Note that any
1715		 * special work for recursive unions is the responsibility of
1716		 * plan_set_operations.
1717		 */
1718		current_rel = plan_set_operations(root);
1719
1720		/*
1721		 * We should not need to call preprocess_targetlist, since we must be
1722		 * in a SELECT query node.  Instead, use the processed_tlist returned
1723		 * by plan_set_operations (since this tells whether it returned any
1724		 * resjunk columns!), and transfer any sort key information from the
1725		 * original tlist.
1726		 */
1727		Assert(parse->commandType == CMD_SELECT);
1728
1729		/* for safety, copy processed_tlist instead of modifying in-place */
1730		root->processed_tlist =
1731			postprocess_setop_tlist(copyObject(root->processed_tlist),
1732									parse->targetList);
1733
1734		/* Also extract the PathTarget form of the setop result tlist */
1735		final_target = current_rel->cheapest_total_path->pathtarget;
1736
1737		/* And check whether it's parallel safe */
1738		final_target_parallel_safe =
1739			is_parallel_safe(root, (Node *) final_target->exprs);
1740
1741		/* The setop result tlist couldn't contain any SRFs */
1742		Assert(!parse->hasTargetSRFs);
1743		final_targets = final_targets_contain_srfs = NIL;
1744
1745		/*
1746		 * Can't handle FOR [KEY] UPDATE/SHARE here (parser should have
1747		 * checked already, but let's make sure).
1748		 */
1749		if (parse->rowMarks)
1750			ereport(ERROR,
1751					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
1752			/*------
1753			  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
1754					 errmsg("%s is not allowed with UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT",
1755							LCS_asString(linitial_node(RowMarkClause,
1756													   parse->rowMarks)->strength))));
1757
1758		/*
1759		 * Calculate pathkeys that represent result ordering requirements
1760		 */
1761		Assert(parse->distinctClause == NIL);
1762		root->sort_pathkeys = make_pathkeys_for_sortclauses(root,
1763															parse->sortClause,
1764															root->processed_tlist);
1765	}
1766	else
1767	{
1768		/* No set operations, do regular planning */
1769		PathTarget *sort_input_target;
1770		List	   *sort_input_targets;
1771		List	   *sort_input_targets_contain_srfs;
1772		bool		sort_input_target_parallel_safe;
1773		PathTarget *grouping_target;
1774		List	   *grouping_targets;
1775		List	   *grouping_targets_contain_srfs;
1776		bool		grouping_target_parallel_safe;
1777		PathTarget *scanjoin_target;
1778		List	   *scanjoin_targets;
1779		List	   *scanjoin_targets_contain_srfs;
1780		bool		scanjoin_target_parallel_safe;
1781		bool		scanjoin_target_same_exprs;
1782		bool		have_grouping;
1783		WindowFuncLists *wflists = NULL;
1784		List	   *activeWindows = NIL;
1785		grouping_sets_data *gset_data = NULL;
1786		standard_qp_extra qp_extra;
1787
1788		/* A recursive query should always have setOperations */
1789		Assert(!root->hasRecursion);
1790
1791		/* Preprocess grouping sets and GROUP BY clause, if any */
1792		if (parse->groupingSets)
1793		{
1794			gset_data = preprocess_grouping_sets(root);
1795		}
1796		else if (parse->groupClause)
1797		{
1798			/* Preprocess regular GROUP BY clause, if any */
1799			root->processed_groupClause = preprocess_groupclause(root, NIL);
1800		}
1801
1802		/*
1803		 * Preprocess targetlist.  Note that much of the remaining planning
1804		 * work will be done with the PathTarget representation of tlists, but
1805		 * we must also maintain the full representation of the final tlist so
1806		 * that we can transfer its decoration (resnames etc) to the topmost
1807		 * tlist of the finished Plan.  This is kept in processed_tlist.
1808		 */
1809		preprocess_targetlist(root);
1810
1811		/*
1812		 * Mark all the aggregates with resolved aggtranstypes, and detect
1813		 * aggregates that are duplicates or can share transition state.  We
1814		 * must do this before slicing and dicing the tlist into various
1815		 * pathtargets, else some copies of the Aggref nodes might escape
1816		 * being marked.
1817		 */
1818		if (parse->hasAggs)
1819		{
1820			preprocess_aggrefs(root, (Node *) root->processed_tlist);
1821			preprocess_aggrefs(root, (Node *) parse->havingQual);
1822		}
1823
1824		/*
1825		 * Locate any window functions in the tlist.  (We don't need to look
1826		 * anywhere else, since expressions used in ORDER BY will be in there
1827		 * too.)  Note that they could all have been eliminated by constant
1828		 * folding, in which case we don't need to do any more work.
1829		 */
1830		if (parse->hasWindowFuncs)
1831		{
1832			wflists = find_window_functions((Node *) root->processed_tlist,
1833											list_length(parse->windowClause));
1834			if (wflists->numWindowFuncs > 0)
1835			{
1836				/*
1837				 * See if any modifications can be made to each WindowClause
1838				 * to allow the executor to execute the WindowFuncs more
1839				 * quickly.
1840				 */
1841				optimize_window_clauses(root, wflists);
1842
1843				/* Extract the list of windows actually in use. */
1844				activeWindows = select_active_windows(root, wflists);
1845
1846				/* Make sure they all have names, for EXPLAIN's use. */
1847				name_active_windows(activeWindows);
1848			}
1849			else
1850				parse->hasWindowFuncs = false;
1851		}
1852
1853		/*
1854		 * Preprocess MIN/MAX aggregates, if any.  Note: be careful about
1855		 * adding logic between here and the query_planner() call.  Anything
1856		 * that is needed in MIN/MAX-optimizable cases will have to be
1857		 * duplicated in planagg.c.
1858		 */
1859		if (parse->hasAggs)
1860			preprocess_minmax_aggregates(root);
1861
1862		/*
1863		 * Figure out whether there's a hard limit on the number of rows that
1864		 * query_planner's result subplan needs to return.  Even if we know a
1865		 * hard limit overall, it doesn't apply if the query has any
1866		 * grouping/aggregation operations, or SRFs in the tlist.
1867		 */
1868		if (parse->groupClause ||
1869			parse->groupingSets ||
1870			parse->distinctClause ||
1871			parse->hasAggs ||
1872			parse->hasWindowFuncs ||
1873			parse->hasTargetSRFs ||
1874			root->hasHavingQual)
1875			root->limit_tuples = -1.0;
1876		else
1877			root->limit_tuples = limit_tuples;
1878
1879		/* Set up data needed by standard_qp_callback */
1880		qp_extra.activeWindows = activeWindows;
1881		qp_extra.gset_data = gset_data;
1882
1883		/*
1884		 * If we're a subquery for a set operation, store the SetOperationStmt
1885		 * in qp_extra.
1886		 */
1887		qp_extra.setop = setops;
1888
1889		/*
1890		 * Generate the best unsorted and presorted paths for the scan/join
1891		 * portion of this Query, ie the processing represented by the
1892		 * FROM/WHERE clauses.  (Note there may not be any presorted paths.)
1893		 * We also generate (in standard_qp_callback) pathkey representations
1894		 * of the query's sort clause, distinct clause, etc.
1895		 */
1896		current_rel = query_planner(root, standard_qp_callback, &qp_extra);
1897
1898		/*
1899		 * Convert the query's result tlist into PathTarget format.
1900		 *
1901		 * Note: this cannot be done before query_planner() has performed
1902		 * appendrel expansion, because that might add resjunk entries to
1903		 * root->processed_tlist.  Waiting till afterwards is also helpful
1904		 * because the target width estimates can use per-Var width numbers
1905		 * that were obtained within query_planner().
1906		 */
1907		final_target = create_pathtarget(root, root->processed_tlist);
1908		final_target_parallel_safe =
1909			is_parallel_safe(root, (Node *) final_target->exprs);
1910
1911		/*
1912		 * If ORDER BY was given, consider whether we should use a post-sort
1913		 * projection, and compute the adjusted target for preceding steps if
1914		 * so.
1915		 */
1916		if (parse->sortClause)
1917		{
1918			sort_input_target = make_sort_input_target(root,
1919													   final_target,
1920													   &have_postponed_srfs);
1921			sort_input_target_parallel_safe =
1922				is_parallel_safe(root, (Node *) sort_input_target->exprs);
1923		}
1924		else
1925		{
1926			sort_input_target = final_target;
1927			sort_input_target_parallel_safe = final_target_parallel_safe;
1928		}
1929
1930		/*
1931		 * If we have window functions to deal with, the output from any
1932		 * grouping step needs to be what the window functions want;
1933		 * otherwise, it should be sort_input_target.
1934		 */
1935		if (activeWindows)
1936		{
1937			grouping_target = make_window_input_target(root,
1938													   final_target,
1939													   activeWindows);
1940			grouping_target_parallel_safe =
1941				is_parallel_safe(root, (Node *) grouping_target->exprs);
1942		}
1943		else
1944		{
1945			grouping_target = sort_input_target;
1946			grouping_target_parallel_safe = sort_input_target_parallel_safe;
1947		}
1948
1949		/*
1950		 * If we have grouping or aggregation to do, the topmost scan/join
1951		 * plan node must emit what the grouping step wants; otherwise, it
1952		 * should emit grouping_target.
1953		 */
1954		have_grouping = (parse->groupClause || parse->groupingSets ||
1955						 parse->hasAggs || root->hasHavingQual);
1956		if (have_grouping)
1957		{
1958			scanjoin_target = make_group_input_target(root, final_target);
1959			scanjoin_target_parallel_safe =
1960				is_parallel_safe(root, (Node *) scanjoin_target->exprs);
1961		}
1962		else
1963		{
1964			scanjoin_target = grouping_target;
1965			scanjoin_target_parallel_safe = grouping_target_parallel_safe;
1966		}
1967
1968		/*
1969		 * If there are any SRFs in the targetlist, we must separate each of
1970		 * these PathTargets into SRF-computing and SRF-free targets.  Replace
1971		 * each of the named targets with a SRF-free version, and remember the
1972		 * list of additional projection steps we need to add afterwards.
1973		 */
1974		if (parse->hasTargetSRFs)
1975		{
1976			/* final_target doesn't recompute any SRFs in sort_input_target */
1977			split_pathtarget_at_srfs(root, final_target, sort_input_target,
1978									 &final_targets,
1979									 &final_targets_contain_srfs);
1980			final_target = linitial_node(PathTarget, final_targets);
1981			Assert(!linitial_int(final_targets_contain_srfs));
1982			/* likewise for sort_input_target vs. grouping_target */
1983			split_pathtarget_at_srfs(root, sort_input_target, grouping_target,
1984									 &sort_input_targets,
1985									 &sort_input_targets_contain_srfs);
1986			sort_input_target = linitial_node(PathTarget, sort_input_targets);
1987			Assert(!linitial_int(sort_input_targets_contain_srfs));
1988			/* likewise for grouping_target vs. scanjoin_target */
1989			split_pathtarget_at_srfs_grouping(root,
1990											  grouping_target, scanjoin_target,
1991											  &grouping_targets,
1992											  &grouping_targets_contain_srfs);
1993			grouping_target = linitial_node(PathTarget, grouping_targets);
1994			Assert(!linitial_int(grouping_targets_contain_srfs));
1995			/* scanjoin_target will not have any SRFs precomputed for it */
1996			split_pathtarget_at_srfs(root, scanjoin_target, NULL,
1997									 &scanjoin_targets,
1998									 &scanjoin_targets_contain_srfs);
1999			scanjoin_target = linitial_node(PathTarget, scanjoin_targets);
2000			Assert(!linitial_int(scanjoin_targets_contain_srfs));
2001		}
2002		else
2003		{
2004			/* initialize lists; for most of these, dummy values are OK */
2005			final_targets = final_targets_contain_srfs = NIL;
2006			sort_input_targets = sort_input_targets_contain_srfs = NIL;
2007			grouping_targets = grouping_targets_contain_srfs = NIL;
2008			scanjoin_targets = list_make1(scanjoin_target);
2009			scanjoin_targets_contain_srfs = NIL;
2010		}
2011
2012		/* Apply scan/join target. */
2013		scanjoin_target_same_exprs = list_length(scanjoin_targets) == 1
2014			&& equal(scanjoin_target->exprs, current_rel->reltarget->exprs);
2015		apply_scanjoin_target_to_paths(root, current_rel, scanjoin_targets,
2016									   scanjoin_targets_contain_srfs,
2017									   scanjoin_target_parallel_safe,
2018									   scanjoin_target_same_exprs);
2019
2020		/*
2021		 * Save the various upper-rel PathTargets we just computed into
2022		 * root->upper_targets[].  The core code doesn't use this, but it
2023		 * provides a convenient place for extensions to get at the info.  For
2024		 * consistency, we save all the intermediate targets, even though some
2025		 * of the corresponding upperrels might not be needed for this query.
2026		 */
2027		root->upper_targets[UPPERREL_FINAL] = final_target;
2028		root->upper_targets[UPPERREL_ORDERED] = final_target;
2029		root->upper_targets[UPPERREL_DISTINCT] = sort_input_target;
2030		root->upper_targets[UPPERREL_PARTIAL_DISTINCT] = sort_input_target;
2031		root->upper_targets[UPPERREL_WINDOW] = sort_input_target;
2032		root->upper_targets[UPPERREL_GROUP_AGG] = grouping_target;
2033
2034		/*
2035		 * If we have grouping and/or aggregation, consider ways to implement
2036		 * that.  We build a new upperrel representing the output of this
2037		 * phase.
2038		 */
2039		if (have_grouping)
2040		{
2041			current_rel = create_grouping_paths(root,
2042												current_rel,
2043												grouping_target,
2044												grouping_target_parallel_safe,
2045												gset_data);
2046			/* Fix things up if grouping_target contains SRFs */
2047			if (parse->hasTargetSRFs)
2048				adjust_paths_for_srfs(root, current_rel,
2049									  grouping_targets,
2050									  grouping_targets_contain_srfs);
2051		}
2052
2053		/*
2054		 * If we have window functions, consider ways to implement those.  We
2055		 * build a new upperrel representing the output of this phase.
2056		 */
2057		if (activeWindows)
2058		{
2059			current_rel = create_window_paths(root,
2060											  current_rel,
2061											  grouping_target,
2062											  sort_input_target,
2063											  sort_input_target_parallel_safe,
2064											  wflists,
2065											  activeWindows);
2066			/* Fix things up if sort_input_target contains SRFs */
2067			if (parse->hasTargetSRFs)
2068				adjust_paths_for_srfs(root, current_rel,
2069									  sort_input_targets,
2070									  sort_input_targets_contain_srfs);
2071		}
2072
2073		/*
2074		 * If there is a DISTINCT clause, consider ways to implement that. We
2075		 * build a new upperrel representing the output of this phase.
2076		 */
2077		if (parse->distinctClause)
2078		{
2079			current_rel = create_distinct_paths(root,
2080												current_rel,
2081												sort_input_target);
2082		}
2083	}							/* end of if (setOperations) */
2084
2085	/*
2086	 * If ORDER BY was given, consider ways to implement that, and generate a
2087	 * new upperrel containing only paths that emit the correct ordering and
2088	 * project the correct final_target.  We can apply the original
2089	 * limit_tuples limit in sort costing here, but only if there are no
2090	 * postponed SRFs.
2091	 */
2092	if (parse->sortClause)
2093	{
2094		current_rel = create_ordered_paths(root,
2095										   current_rel,
2096										   final_target,
2097										   final_target_parallel_safe,
2098										   have_postponed_srfs ? -1.0 :
2099										   limit_tuples);
2100		/* Fix things up if final_target contains SRFs */
2101		if (parse->hasTargetSRFs)
2102			adjust_paths_for_srfs(root, current_rel,
2103								  final_targets,
2104								  final_targets_contain_srfs);
2105	}
2106
2107	/*
2108	 * Now we are prepared to build the final-output upperrel.
2109	 */
2110	final_rel = fetch_upper_rel(root, UPPERREL_FINAL, NULL);
2111
2112	/*
2113	 * If the input rel is marked consider_parallel and there's nothing that's
2114	 * not parallel-safe in the LIMIT clause, then the final_rel can be marked
2115	 * consider_parallel as well.  Note that if the query has rowMarks or is
2116	 * not a SELECT, consider_parallel will be false for every relation in the
2117	 * query.
2118	 */
2119	if (current_rel->consider_parallel &&
2120		is_parallel_safe(root, parse->limitOffset) &&
2121		is_parallel_safe(root, parse->limitCount))
2122		final_rel->consider_parallel = true;
2123
2124	/*
2125	 * If the current_rel belongs to a single FDW, so does the final_rel.
2126	 */
2127	final_rel->serverid = current_rel->serverid;
2128	final_rel->userid = current_rel->userid;
2129	final_rel->useridiscurrent = current_rel->useridiscurrent;
2130	final_rel->fdwroutine = current_rel->fdwroutine;
2131
2132	/*
2133	 * Generate paths for the final_rel.  Insert all surviving paths, with
2134	 * LockRows, Limit, and/or ModifyTable steps added if needed.
2135	 */
