2136	foreach(lc, current_rel->pathlist)
2137	{
2138		Path	   *path = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
2139
2140		/*
2141		 * If there is a FOR [KEY] UPDATE/SHARE clause, add the LockRows node.
2142		 * (Note: we intentionally test parse->rowMarks not root->rowMarks
2143		 * here.  If there are only non-locking rowmarks, they should be
2144		 * handled by the ModifyTable node instead.  However, root->rowMarks
2145		 * is what goes into the LockRows node.)
2146		 */
2147		if (parse->rowMarks)
2148		{
2149			path = (Path *) create_lockrows_path(root, final_rel, path,
2150												 root->rowMarks,
2151												 assign_special_exec_param(root));
2152		}
2153
2154		/*
2155		 * If there is a LIMIT/OFFSET clause, add the LIMIT node.
2156		 */
2157		if (limit_needed(parse))
2158		{
2159			path = (Path *) create_limit_path(root, final_rel, path,
2160											  parse->limitOffset,
2161											  parse->limitCount,
2162											  parse->limitOption,
2163											  offset_est, count_est);
2164		}
2165
2166		/*
2167		 * If this is an INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE, add the ModifyTable node.
2168		 */
2169		if (parse->commandType != CMD_SELECT)
2170		{
2171			Index		rootRelation;
2172			List	   *resultRelations = NIL;
2173			List	   *updateColnosLists = NIL;
2174			List	   *withCheckOptionLists = NIL;
2175			List	   *returningLists = NIL;
2176			List	   *mergeActionLists = NIL;
2177			List	   *mergeJoinConditions = NIL;
2178			List	   *rowMarks;
2179
2180			if (bms_membership(root->all_result_relids) == BMS_MULTIPLE)
2181			{
2182				/* Inherited UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE */
2183				RelOptInfo *top_result_rel = find_base_rel(root,
2184														   parse->resultRelation);
2185				int			resultRelation = -1;
2186
2187				/* Pass the root result rel forward to the executor. */
2188				rootRelation = parse->resultRelation;
2189
2190				/* Add only leaf children to ModifyTable. */
2191				while ((resultRelation = bms_next_member(root->leaf_result_relids,
2192														 resultRelation)) >= 0)
2193				{
2194					RelOptInfo *this_result_rel = find_base_rel(root,
2195																resultRelation);
2196
2197					/*
2198					 * Also exclude any leaf rels that have turned dummy since
2199					 * being added to the list, for example, by being excluded
2200					 * by constraint exclusion.
2201					 */
2202					if (IS_DUMMY_REL(this_result_rel))
2203						continue;
2204
2205					/* Build per-target-rel lists needed by ModifyTable */
2206					resultRelations = lappend_int(resultRelations,
2207												  resultRelation);
2208					if (parse->commandType == CMD_UPDATE)
2209					{
2210						List	   *update_colnos = root->update_colnos;
2211
2212						if (this_result_rel != top_result_rel)
2213							update_colnos =
2214								adjust_inherited_attnums_multilevel(root,
2215																	update_colnos,
2216																	this_result_rel->relid,
2217																	top_result_rel->relid);
2218						updateColnosLists = lappend(updateColnosLists,
2219													update_colnos);
2220					}
2221					if (parse->withCheckOptions)
2222					{
2223						List	   *withCheckOptions = parse->withCheckOptions;
2224
2225						if (this_result_rel != top_result_rel)
2226							withCheckOptions = (List *)
2227								adjust_appendrel_attrs_multilevel(root,
2228																  (Node *) withCheckOptions,
2229																  this_result_rel,
2230																  top_result_rel);
2231						withCheckOptionLists = lappend(withCheckOptionLists,
2232													   withCheckOptions);
2233					}
2234					if (parse->returningList)
2235					{
2236						List	   *returningList = parse->returningList;
2237
2238						if (this_result_rel != top_result_rel)
2239							returningList = (List *)
2240								adjust_appendrel_attrs_multilevel(root,
2241																  (Node *) returningList,
2242																  this_result_rel,
2243																  top_result_rel);
2244						returningLists = lappend(returningLists,
2245												 returningList);
2246					}
2247					if (parse->mergeActionList)
2248					{
2249						ListCell   *l;
2250						List	   *mergeActionList = NIL;
2251
2252						/*
2253						 * Copy MergeActions and translate stuff that
2254						 * references attribute numbers.
2255						 */
2256						foreach(l, parse->mergeActionList)
2257						{
2258							MergeAction *action = lfirst(l),
2259									   *leaf_action = copyObject(action);
2260
2261							leaf_action->qual =
2262								adjust_appendrel_attrs_multilevel(root,
2263																  (Node *) action->qual,
2264																  this_result_rel,
2265																  top_result_rel);
2266							leaf_action->targetList = (List *)
2267								adjust_appendrel_attrs_multilevel(root,
2268																  (Node *) action->targetList,
2269																  this_result_rel,
2270																  top_result_rel);
2271							if (leaf_action->commandType == CMD_UPDATE)
2272								leaf_action->updateColnos =
2273									adjust_inherited_attnums_multilevel(root,
2274																		action->updateColnos,
2275																		this_result_rel->relid,
2276																		top_result_rel->relid);
2277							mergeActionList = lappend(mergeActionList,
2278													  leaf_action);
2279						}
2280
2281						mergeActionLists = lappend(mergeActionLists,
2282												   mergeActionList);
2283					}
2284					if (parse->commandType == CMD_MERGE)
2285					{
2286						Node	   *mergeJoinCondition = parse->mergeJoinCondition;
2287
2288						if (this_result_rel != top_result_rel)
2289							mergeJoinCondition =
2290								adjust_appendrel_attrs_multilevel(root,
2291																  mergeJoinCondition,
2292																  this_result_rel,
2293																  top_result_rel);
2294						mergeJoinConditions = lappend(mergeJoinConditions,
2295													  mergeJoinCondition);
2296					}
2297				}
2298
2299				if (resultRelations == NIL)
2300				{
2301					/*
2302					 * We managed to exclude every child rel, so generate a
2303					 * dummy one-relation plan using info for the top target
2304					 * rel (even though that may not be a leaf target).
2305					 * Although it's clear that no data will be updated or
2306					 * deleted, we still need to have a ModifyTable node so
2307					 * that any statement triggers will be executed.  (This
2308					 * could be cleaner if we fixed nodeModifyTable.c to allow
2309					 * zero target relations, but that probably wouldn't be a
2310					 * net win.)
2311					 */
2312					resultRelations = list_make1_int(parse->resultRelation);
2313					if (parse->commandType == CMD_UPDATE)
2314						updateColnosLists = list_make1(root->update_colnos);
2315					if (parse->withCheckOptions)
2316						withCheckOptionLists = list_make1(parse->withCheckOptions);
2317					if (parse->returningList)
2318						returningLists = list_make1(parse->returningList);
2319					if (parse->mergeActionList)
2320						mergeActionLists = list_make1(parse->mergeActionList);
2321					if (parse->commandType == CMD_MERGE)
2322						mergeJoinConditions = list_make1(parse->mergeJoinCondition);
2323				}
2324			}
2325			else
2326			{
2327				/* Single-relation INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE. */
2328				rootRelation = 0;	/* there's no separate root rel */
2329				resultRelations = list_make1_int(parse->resultRelation);
2330				if (parse->commandType == CMD_UPDATE)
2331					updateColnosLists = list_make1(root->update_colnos);
2332				if (parse->withCheckOptions)
2333					withCheckOptionLists = list_make1(parse->withCheckOptions);
2334				if (parse->returningList)
2335					returningLists = list_make1(parse->returningList);
2336				if (parse->mergeActionList)
2337					mergeActionLists = list_make1(parse->mergeActionList);
2338				if (parse->commandType == CMD_MERGE)
2339					mergeJoinConditions = list_make1(parse->mergeJoinCondition);
2340			}
2341
2342			/*
2343			 * If there was a FOR [KEY] UPDATE/SHARE clause, the LockRows node
2344			 * will have dealt with fetching non-locked marked rows, else we
2345			 * need to have ModifyTable do that.
2346			 */
2347			if (parse->rowMarks)
2348				rowMarks = NIL;
2349			else
2350				rowMarks = root->rowMarks;
2351
2352			path = (Path *)
2353				create_modifytable_path(root, final_rel,
2354										path,
2355										parse->commandType,
2356										parse->canSetTag,
2357										parse->resultRelation,
2358										rootRelation,
2359										root->partColsUpdated,
2360										resultRelations,
2361										updateColnosLists,
2362										withCheckOptionLists,
2363										returningLists,
2364										rowMarks,
2365										parse->onConflict,
2366										mergeActionLists,
2367										mergeJoinConditions,
2368										assign_special_exec_param(root));
2369		}
2370
2371		/* And shove it into final_rel */
2372		add_path(final_rel, path);
2373	}
2374
2375	/*
2376	 * Generate partial paths for final_rel, too, if outer query levels might
2377	 * be able to make use of them.
2378	 */
2379	if (final_rel->consider_parallel && root->query_level > 1 &&
2380		!limit_needed(parse))
2381	{
2382		Assert(!parse->rowMarks && parse->commandType == CMD_SELECT);
2383		foreach(lc, current_rel->partial_pathlist)
2384		{
2385			Path	   *partial_path = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
2386
2387			add_partial_path(final_rel, partial_path);
2388		}
2389	}
2390
2391	extra.limit_needed = limit_needed(parse);
2392	extra.limit_tuples = limit_tuples;
2393	extra.count_est = count_est;
2394	extra.offset_est = offset_est;
2395
2396	/*
2397	 * If there is an FDW that's responsible for all baserels of the query,
2398	 * let it consider adding ForeignPaths.
2399	 */
2400	if (final_rel->fdwroutine &&
2401		final_rel->fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths)
2402		final_rel->fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths(root, UPPERREL_FINAL,
2403													current_rel, final_rel,
2404													&extra);
2405
2406	/* Let extensions possibly add some more paths */
2407	if (create_upper_paths_hook)
2408		(*create_upper_paths_hook) (root, UPPERREL_FINAL,
2409									current_rel, final_rel, &extra);
2410
2411	/* Note: currently, we leave it to callers to do set_cheapest() */
2412}
2413
2414/*
2415 * Do preprocessing for groupingSets clause and related data.
2416 *
2417 * We expect that parse->groupingSets has already been expanded into a flat
2418 * list of grouping sets (that is, just integer Lists of ressortgroupref
2419 * numbers) by expand_grouping_sets().  This function handles the preliminary
2420 * steps of organizing the grouping sets into lists of rollups, and preparing
2421 * annotations which will later be filled in with size estimates.
2422 */
2423static grouping_sets_data *
2424preprocess_grouping_sets(PlannerInfo *root)
2425{
2426	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
2427	List	   *sets;
2428	int			maxref = 0;
2429	ListCell   *lc_set;
2430	grouping_sets_data *gd = palloc0(sizeof(grouping_sets_data));
2431
2432	/*
2433	 * We don't currently make any attempt to optimize the groupClause when
2434	 * there are grouping sets, so just duplicate it in processed_groupClause.
2435	 */
2436	root->processed_groupClause = parse->groupClause;
2437
2438	/* Detect unhashable and unsortable grouping expressions */
2439	gd->any_hashable = false;
2440	gd->unhashable_refs = NULL;
2441	gd->unsortable_refs = NULL;
2442	gd->unsortable_sets = NIL;
2443
2444	if (parse->groupClause)
2445	{
2446		ListCell   *lc;
2447
2448		foreach(lc, parse->groupClause)
2449		{
2450			SortGroupClause *gc = lfirst_node(SortGroupClause, lc);
2451			Index		ref = gc->tleSortGroupRef;
2452
2453			if (ref > maxref)
2454				maxref = ref;
2455
2456			if (!gc->hashable)
2457				gd->unhashable_refs = bms_add_member(gd->unhashable_refs, ref);
2458
2459			if (!OidIsValid(gc->sortop))
2460				gd->unsortable_refs = bms_add_member(gd->unsortable_refs, ref);
2461		}
2462	}
2463
2464	/* Allocate workspace array for remapping */
2465	gd->tleref_to_colnum_map = (int *) palloc((maxref + 1) * sizeof(int));
2466
2467	/*
2468	 * If we have any unsortable sets, we must extract them before trying to
2469	 * prepare rollups. Unsortable sets don't go through
2470	 * reorder_grouping_sets, so we must apply the GroupingSetData annotation
2471	 * here.
2472	 */
2473	if (!bms_is_empty(gd->unsortable_refs))
2474	{
2475		List	   *sortable_sets = NIL;
2476		ListCell   *lc;
2477
2478		foreach(lc, parse->groupingSets)
2479		{
2480			List	   *gset = (List *) lfirst(lc);
2481
2482			if (bms_overlap_list(gd->unsortable_refs, gset))
2483			{
2484				GroupingSetData *gs = makeNode(GroupingSetData);
2485
2486				gs->set = gset;
2487				gd->unsortable_sets = lappend(gd->unsortable_sets, gs);
2488
2489				/*
2490				 * We must enforce here that an unsortable set is hashable;
2491				 * later code assumes this.  Parse analysis only checks that
2492				 * every individual column is either hashable or sortable.
2493				 *
2494				 * Note that passing this test doesn't guarantee we can
2495				 * generate a plan; there might be other showstoppers.
2496				 */
2497				if (bms_overlap_list(gd->unhashable_refs, gset))
2498					ereport(ERROR,
2499							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
2500							 errmsg("could not implement GROUP BY"),
2501							 errdetail("Some of the datatypes only support hashing, while others only support sorting.")));
2502			}
2503			else
2504				sortable_sets = lappend(sortable_sets, gset);
2505		}
2506
2507		if (sortable_sets)
2508			sets = extract_rollup_sets(sortable_sets);
2509		else
2510			sets = NIL;
2511	}
2512	else
2513		sets = extract_rollup_sets(parse->groupingSets);
2514
2515	foreach(lc_set, sets)
2516	{
2517		List	   *current_sets = (List *) lfirst(lc_set);
2518		RollupData *rollup = makeNode(RollupData);
2519		GroupingSetData *gs;
2520
2521		/*
2522		 * Reorder the current list of grouping sets into correct prefix
2523		 * order.  If only one aggregation pass is needed, try to make the
2524		 * list match the ORDER BY clause; if more than one pass is needed, we
2525		 * don't bother with that.
2526		 *
2527		 * Note that this reorders the sets from smallest-member-first to
2528		 * largest-member-first, and applies the GroupingSetData annotations,
2529		 * though the data will be filled in later.
2530		 */
2531		current_sets = reorder_grouping_sets(current_sets,
2532											 (list_length(sets) == 1
2533											  ? parse->sortClause
2534											  : NIL));
2535
2536		/*
2537		 * Get the initial (and therefore largest) grouping set.
2538		 */
2539		gs = linitial_node(GroupingSetData, current_sets);
2540
2541		/*
2542		 * Order the groupClause appropriately.  If the first grouping set is
2543		 * empty, then the groupClause must also be empty; otherwise we have
2544		 * to force the groupClause to match that grouping set's order.
2545		 *
2546		 * (The first grouping set can be empty even though parse->groupClause
2547		 * is not empty only if all non-empty grouping sets are unsortable.
2548		 * The groupClauses for hashed grouping sets are built later on.)
2549		 */
2550		if (gs->set)
2551			rollup->groupClause = preprocess_groupclause(root, gs->set);
2552		else
2553			rollup->groupClause = NIL;
2554
2555		/*
2556		 * Is it hashable? We pretend empty sets are hashable even though we
2557		 * actually force them not to be hashed later. But don't bother if
2558		 * there's nothing but empty sets (since in that case we can't hash
2559		 * anything).
2560		 */
2561		if (gs->set &&
2562			!bms_overlap_list(gd->unhashable_refs, gs->set))
2563		{
2564			rollup->hashable = true;
2565			gd->any_hashable = true;
2566		}
2567
2568		/*
2569		 * Now that we've pinned down an order for the groupClause for this
2570		 * list of grouping sets, we need to remap the entries in the grouping
2571		 * sets from sortgrouprefs to plain indices (0-based) into the
2572		 * groupClause for this collection of grouping sets. We keep the
2573		 * original form for later use, though.
2574		 */
2575		rollup->gsets = remap_to_groupclause_idx(rollup->groupClause,
2576												 current_sets,
2577												 gd->tleref_to_colnum_map);
2578		rollup->gsets_data = current_sets;
2579
2580		gd->rollups = lappend(gd->rollups, rollup);
2581	}
2582
2583	if (gd->unsortable_sets)
2584	{
2585		/*
2586		 * We have not yet pinned down a groupclause for this, but we will
2587		 * need index-based lists for estimation purposes. Construct
2588		 * hash_sets_idx based on the entire original groupclause for now.
2589		 */
2590		gd->hash_sets_idx = remap_to_groupclause_idx(parse->groupClause,
2591													 gd->unsortable_sets,
2592													 gd->tleref_to_colnum_map);
2593		gd->any_hashable = true;
2594	}
2595
2596	return gd;
2597}
2598
2599/*
2600 * Given a groupclause and a list of GroupingSetData, return equivalent sets
2601 * (without annotation) mapped to indexes into the given groupclause.
2602 */
2603static List *
2604remap_to_groupclause_idx(List *groupClause,
2605						 List *gsets,
2606						 int *tleref_to_colnum_map)
2607{
2608	int			ref = 0;
2609	List	   *result = NIL;
2610	ListCell   *lc;
2611
2612	foreach(lc, groupClause)
2613	{
2614		SortGroupClause *gc = lfirst_node(SortGroupClause, lc);
2615
2616		tleref_to_colnum_map[gc->tleSortGroupRef] = ref++;
2617	}
2618
2619	foreach(lc, gsets)
2620	{
2621		List	   *set = NIL;
2622		ListCell   *lc2;
2623		GroupingSetData *gs = lfirst_node(GroupingSetData, lc);
2624
2625		foreach(lc2, gs->set)
2626		{
2627			set = lappend_int(set, tleref_to_colnum_map[lfirst_int(lc2)]);
2628		}
2629
2630		result = lappend(result, set);
2631	}
2632
2633	return result;
2634}
2635
2636
2637/*
2638 * preprocess_rowmarks - set up PlanRowMarks if needed
2639 */
2640static void
2641preprocess_rowmarks(PlannerInfo *root)
2642{
2643	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
2644	Bitmapset  *rels;
2645	List	   *prowmarks;
2646	ListCell   *l;
2647	int			i;
2648
2649	if (parse->rowMarks)
2650	{
2651		/*
2652		 * We've got trouble if FOR [KEY] UPDATE/SHARE appears inside
2653		 * grouping, since grouping renders a reference to individual tuple
2654		 * CTIDs invalid.  This is also checked at parse time, but that's
2655		 * insufficient because of rule substitution, query pullup, etc.
2656		 */
2657		CheckSelectLocking(parse, linitial_node(RowMarkClause,
2658												parse->rowMarks)->strength);
2659	}
2660	else
2661	{
2662		/*
2663		 * We only need rowmarks for UPDATE, DELETE, MERGE, or FOR [KEY]
2664		 * UPDATE/SHARE.
2665		 */
2666		if (parse->commandType != CMD_UPDATE &&
2667			parse->commandType != CMD_DELETE &&
2668			parse->commandType != CMD_MERGE)
2669			return;
2670	}
2671
2672	/*
2673	 * We need to have rowmarks for all base relations except the target. We
2674	 * make a bitmapset of all base rels and then remove the items we don't
2675	 * need or have FOR [KEY] UPDATE/SHARE marks for.
2676	 */
2677	rels = get_relids_in_jointree((Node *) parse->jointree, false, false);
2678	if (parse->resultRelation)
2679		rels = bms_del_member(rels, parse->resultRelation);
2680
2681	/*
2682	 * Convert RowMarkClauses to PlanRowMark representation.
2683	 */
2684	prowmarks = NIL;
2685	foreach(l, parse->rowMarks)
2686	{
2687		RowMarkClause *rc = lfirst_node(RowMarkClause, l);
2688		RangeTblEntry *rte = rt_fetch(rc->rti, parse->rtable);
2689		PlanRowMark *newrc;
2690
2691		/*
2692		 * Currently, it is syntactically impossible to have FOR UPDATE et al
2693		 * applied to an update/delete target rel.  If that ever becomes
2694		 * possible, we should drop the target from the PlanRowMark list.
2695		 */
2696		Assert(rc->rti != parse->resultRelation);
2697
2698		/*
2699		 * Ignore RowMarkClauses for subqueries; they aren't real tables and
2700		 * can't support true locking.  Subqueries that got flattened into the
2701		 * main query should be ignored completely.  Any that didn't will get
2702		 * ROW_MARK_COPY items in the next loop.
2703		 */
2704		if (rte->rtekind != RTE_RELATION)
2705			continue;
2706
2707		rels = bms_del_member(rels, rc->rti);
2708
2709		newrc = makeNode(PlanRowMark);
2710		newrc->rti = newrc->prti = rc->rti;
2711		newrc->rowmarkId = ++(root->glob->lastRowMarkId);
2712		newrc->markType = select_rowmark_type(rte, rc->strength);
2713		newrc->allMarkTypes = (1 << newrc->markType);
2714		newrc->strength = rc->strength;
2715		newrc->waitPolicy = rc->waitPolicy;
2716		newrc->isParent = false;
2717
2718		prowmarks = lappend(prowmarks, newrc);
2719	}
2720
2721	/*
2722	 * Now, add rowmarks for any non-target, non-locked base relations.
2723	 */
2724	i = 0;
2725	foreach(l, parse->rtable)
2726	{
2727		RangeTblEntry *rte = lfirst_node(RangeTblEntry, l);
2728		PlanRowMark *newrc;
2729
2730		i++;
2731		if (!bms_is_member(i, rels))
2732			continue;
2733
2734		newrc = makeNode(PlanRowMark);
2735		newrc->rti = newrc->prti = i;
2736		newrc->rowmarkId = ++(root->glob->lastRowMarkId);
2737		newrc->markType = select_rowmark_type(rte, LCS_NONE);
2738		newrc->allMarkTypes = (1 << newrc->markType);
2739		newrc->strength = LCS_NONE;
2740		newrc->waitPolicy = LockWaitBlock;	/* doesn't matter */
2741		newrc->isParent = false;
2742
2743		prowmarks = lappend(prowmarks, newrc);
2744	}
2745
2746	root->rowMarks = prowmarks;
2747}
2748
2749/*
2750 * Select RowMarkType to use for a given table
2751 */
2752RowMarkType
2753select_rowmark_type(RangeTblEntry *rte, LockClauseStrength strength)
2754{
2755	if (rte->rtekind != RTE_RELATION)
2756	{
2757		/* If it's not a table at all, use ROW_MARK_COPY */
2758		return ROW_MARK_COPY;
2759	}
2760	else if (rte->relkind == RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE)
2761	{
2762		/* Let the FDW select the rowmark type, if it wants to */
2763		FdwRoutine *fdwroutine = GetFdwRoutineByRelId(rte->relid);
2764
2765		if (fdwroutine->GetForeignRowMarkType != NULL)
2766			return fdwroutine->GetForeignRowMarkType(rte, strength);
2767		/* Otherwise, use ROW_MARK_COPY by default */
2768		return ROW_MARK_COPY;
2769	}
2770	else
2771	{
2772		/* Regular table, apply the appropriate lock type */
2773		switch (strength)
2774		{
2775			case LCS_NONE:
2776
2777				/*
2778				 * We don't need a tuple lock, only the ability to re-fetch
2779				 * the row.
2780				 */
2781				return ROW_MARK_REFERENCE;
2782				break;
2783			case LCS_FORKEYSHARE:
2784				return ROW_MARK_KEYSHARE;
2785				break;
2786			case LCS_FORSHARE:
2787				return ROW_MARK_SHARE;
2788				break;
2789			case LCS_FORNOKEYUPDATE:
2790				return ROW_MARK_NOKEYEXCLUSIVE;
2791				break;
2792			case LCS_FORUPDATE:
2793				return ROW_MARK_EXCLUSIVE;
2794				break;
2795		}
2796		elog(ERROR, "unrecognized LockClauseStrength %d", (int) strength);
2797		return ROW_MARK_EXCLUSIVE;	/* keep compiler quiet */
2798	}
2799}
2800
2801/*
2802 * preprocess_limit - do pre-estimation for LIMIT and/or OFFSET clauses
2803 *
2804 * We try to estimate the values of the LIMIT/OFFSET clauses, and pass the
2805 * results back in *count_est and *offset_est.  These variables are set to
2806 * 0 if the corresponding clause is not present, and -1 if it's present
2807 * but we couldn't estimate the value for it.  (The "0" convention is OK
2808 * for OFFSET but a little bit bogus for LIMIT: effectively we estimate
2809 * LIMIT 0 as though it were LIMIT 1.  But this is in line with the planner's
2810 * usual practice of never estimating less than one row.)  These values will
2811 * be passed to create_limit_path, which see if you change this code.
2812 *
2813 * The return value is the suitably adjusted tuple_fraction to use for
2814 * planning the query.  This adjustment is not overridable, since it reflects
2815 * plan actions that grouping_planner() will certainly take, not assumptions
2816 * about context.
2817 */
2818static double
2819preprocess_limit(PlannerInfo *root, double tuple_fraction,
2820				 int64 *offset_est, int64 *count_est)
2821{
2822	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
2823	Node	   *est;
2824	double		limit_fraction;
2825
2826	/* Should not be called unless LIMIT or OFFSET */
2827	Assert(parse->limitCount || parse->limitOffset);
2828
2829	/*
2830	 * Try to obtain the clause values.  We use estimate_expression_value
2831	 * primarily because it can sometimes do something useful with Params.
2832	 */
2833	if (parse->limitCount)
2834	{
2835		est = estimate_expression_value(root, parse->limitCount);
2836		if (est && IsA(est, Const))
2837		{
2838			if (((Const *) est)->constisnull)
2839			{
2840				/* NULL indicates LIMIT ALL, ie, no limit */
2841				*count_est = 0; /* treat as not present */
2842			}
2843			else
2844			{
2845				*count_est = DatumGetInt64(((Const *) est)->constvalue);
2846				if (*count_est <= 0)
2847					*count_est = 1; /* force to at least 1 */
2848			}
2849		}
2850		else
2851			*count_est = -1;	/* can't estimate */
2852	}
2853	else
2854		*count_est = 0;			/* not present */
2855
2856	if (parse->limitOffset)
2857	{
2858		est = estimate_expression_value(root, parse->limitOffset);
2859		if (est && IsA(est, Const))
2860		{
2861			if (((Const *) est)->constisnull)
2862			{
2863				/* Treat NULL as no offset; the executor will too */
2864				*offset_est = 0;	/* treat as not present */
2865			}
2866			else
2867			{
2868				*offset_est = DatumGetInt64(((Const *) est)->constvalue);
2869				if (*offset_est < 0)
2870					*offset_est = 0;	/* treat as not present */
2871			}
2872		}
2873		else
2874			*offset_est = -1;	/* can't estimate */
2875	}
2876	else
2877		*offset_est = 0;		/* not present */
2878
2879	if (*count_est != 0)
2880	{
2881		/*
2882		 * A LIMIT clause limits the absolute number of tuples returned.
2883		 * However, if it's not a constant LIMIT then we have to guess; for
2884		 * lack of a better idea, assume 10% of the plan's result is wanted.
2885		 */
2886		if (*count_est < 0 || *offset_est < 0)
2887		{
2888			/* LIMIT or OFFSET is an expression ... punt ... */
2889			limit_fraction = 0.10;
2890		}
2891		else
2892		{
2893			/* LIMIT (plus OFFSET, if any) is max number of tuples needed */
2894			limit_fraction = (double) *count_est + (double) *offset_est;
2895		}
2896
2897		/*
2898		 * If we have absolute limits from both caller and LIMIT, use the
2899		 * smaller value; likewise if they are both fractional.  If one is
2900		 * fractional and the other absolute, we can't easily determine which
2901		 * is smaller, but we use the heuristic that the absolute will usually
2902		 * be smaller.
2903		 */
2904		if (tuple_fraction >= 1.0)
2905		{
2906			if (limit_fraction >= 1.0)
2907			{
2908				/* both absolute */
2909				tuple_fraction = Min(tuple_fraction, limit_fraction);
2910			}
2911			else
2912			{
2913				/* caller absolute, limit fractional; use caller's value */
2914			}
2915		}
2916		else if (tuple_fraction > 0.0)
2917		{
2918			if (limit_fraction >= 1.0)
2919			{
2920				/* caller fractional, limit absolute; use limit */
2921				tuple_fraction = limit_fraction;
2922			}
2923			else
2924			{
2925				/* both fractional */
2926				tuple_fraction = Min(tuple_fraction, limit_fraction);
2927			}
2928		}
2929		else
2930		{
2931			/* no info from caller, just use limit */
2932			tuple_fraction = limit_fraction;
2933		}
2934	}
2935	else if (*offset_est != 0 && tuple_fraction > 0.0)
2936	{
2937		/*
2938		 * We have an OFFSET but no LIMIT.  This acts entirely differently
2939		 * from the LIMIT case: here, we need to increase rather than decrease
2940		 * the caller's tuple_fraction, because the OFFSET acts to cause more
2941		 * tuples to be fetched instead of fewer.  This only matters if we got
2942		 * a tuple_fraction > 0, however.
2943		 *
2944		 * As above, use 10% if OFFSET is present but unestimatable.
2945		 */
2946		if (*offset_est < 0)
2947			limit_fraction = 0.10;
2948		else
2949			limit_fraction = (double) *offset_est;
2950
2951		/*
2952		 * If we have absolute counts from both caller and OFFSET, add them
2953		 * together; likewise if they are both fractional.  If one is
2954		 * fractional and the other absolute, we want to take the larger, and
2955		 * we heuristically assume that's the fractional one.
2956		 */
2957		if (tuple_fraction >= 1.0)
2958		{
2959			if (limit_fraction >= 1.0)
2960			{
2961				/* both absolute, so add them together */
2962				tuple_fraction += limit_fraction;
2963			}
2964			else
2965			{
2966				/* caller absolute, limit fractional; use limit */
2967				tuple_fraction = limit_fraction;
2968			}
2969		}
2970		else
2971		{
2972			if (limit_fraction >= 1.0)
2973			{
2974				/* caller fractional, limit absolute; use caller's value */
2975			}
2976			else
2977			{
2978				/* both fractional, so add them together */
2979				tuple_fraction += limit_fraction;
2980				if (tuple_fraction >= 1.0)
2981					tuple_fraction = 0.0;	/* assume fetch all */
2982			}
2983		}
2984	}
2985
2986	return tuple_fraction;
2987}
2988
2989/*
2990 * limit_needed - do we actually need a Limit plan node?
2991 *
2992 * If we have constant-zero OFFSET and constant-null LIMIT, we can skip adding
2993 * a Limit node.  This is worth checking for because "OFFSET 0" is a common
2994 * locution for an optimization fence.  (Because other places in the planner
2995 * merely check whether parse->limitOffset isn't NULL, it will still work as
2996 * an optimization fence --- we're just suppressing unnecessary run-time
2997 * overhead.)
2998 *
2999 * This might look like it could be merged into preprocess_limit, but there's
3000 * a key distinction: here we need hard constants in OFFSET/LIMIT, whereas
3001 * in preprocess_limit it's good enough to consider estimated values.
3002 */
3003bool
3004limit_needed(Query *parse)
3005{
3006	Node	   *node;
3007
3008	node = parse->limitCount;
3009	if (node)
3010	{
3011		if (IsA(node, Const))
3012		{
3013			/* NULL indicates LIMIT ALL, ie, no limit */
3014			if (!((Const *) node)->constisnull)
3015				return true;	/* LIMIT with a constant value */
3016		}
3017		else
3018			return true;		/* non-constant LIMIT */
3019	}
3020
3021	node = parse->limitOffset;
3022	if (node)
3023	{
3024		if (IsA(node, Const))
3025		{
3026			/* Treat NULL as no offset; the executor would too */
3027			if (!((Const *) node)->constisnull)
3028			{
3029				int64		offset = DatumGetInt64(((Const *) node)->constvalue);
3030
3031				if (offset != 0)
3032					return true;	/* OFFSET with a nonzero value */
3033			}
3034		}
3035		else
3036			return true;		/* non-constant OFFSET */
3037	}
3038
3039	return false;				/* don't need a Limit plan node */
3040}
3041
3042/*
3043 * preprocess_groupclause - do preparatory work on GROUP BY clause
3044 *
3045 * The idea here is to adjust the ordering of the GROUP BY elements
3046 * (which in itself is semantically insignificant) to match ORDER BY,
3047 * thereby allowing a single sort operation to both implement the ORDER BY
3048 * requirement and set up for a Unique step that implements GROUP BY.
3049 * We also consider partial match between GROUP BY and ORDER BY elements,
3050 * which could allow to implement ORDER BY using the incremental sort.
3051 *
3052 * We also consider other orderings of the GROUP BY elements, which could
3053 * match the sort ordering of other possible plans (eg an indexscan) and
3054 * thereby reduce cost.  This is implemented during the generation of grouping
3055 * paths.  See get_useful_group_keys_orderings() for details.
3056 *
3057 * Note: we need no comparable processing of the distinctClause because
3058 * the parser already enforced that that matches ORDER BY.
3059 *
3060 * Note: we return a fresh List, but its elements are the same
3061 * SortGroupClauses appearing in parse->groupClause.  This is important
3062 * because later processing may modify the processed_groupClause list.
3063 *
3064 * For grouping sets, the order of items is instead forced to agree with that
3065 * of the grouping set (and items not in the grouping set are skipped). The
3066 * work of sorting the order of grouping set elements to match the ORDER BY if
3067 * possible is done elsewhere.
3068 */
3069static List *
3070preprocess_groupclause(PlannerInfo *root, List *force)
3071{
3072	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
3073	List	   *new_groupclause = NIL;
3074	ListCell   *sl;
3075	ListCell   *gl;
3076
3077	/* For grouping sets, we need to force the ordering */
3078	if (force)
3079	{
3080		foreach(sl, force)
3081		{
3082			Index		ref = lfirst_int(sl);
3083			SortGroupClause *cl = get_sortgroupref_clause(ref, parse->groupClause);
3084
3085			new_groupclause = lappend(new_groupclause, cl);
3086		}
3087
3088		return new_groupclause;
3089	}
3090
3091	/* If no ORDER BY, nothing useful to do here */
3092	if (parse->sortClause == NIL)
3093		return list_copy(parse->groupClause);
3094
3095	/*
3096	 * Scan the ORDER BY clause and construct a list of matching GROUP BY
3097	 * items, but only as far as we can make a matching prefix.
3098	 *
3099	 * This code assumes that the sortClause contains no duplicate items.
3100	 */
3101	foreach(sl, parse->sortClause)
3102	{
3103		SortGroupClause *sc = lfirst_node(SortGroupClause, sl);
3104
3105		foreach(gl, parse->groupClause)
3106		{
3107			SortGroupClause *gc = lfirst_node(SortGroupClause, gl);
3108
3109			if (equal(gc, sc))
3110			{
3111				new_groupclause = lappend(new_groupclause, gc);
3112				break;
3113			}
3114		}
3115		if (gl == NULL)
3116			break;				/* no match, so stop scanning */
3117	}
3118
3119
3120	/* If no match at all, no point in reordering GROUP BY */
3121	if (new_groupclause == NIL)
3122		return list_copy(parse->groupClause);
3123
3124	/*
3125	 * Add any remaining GROUP BY items to the new list.  We don't require a
3126	 * complete match, because even partial match allows ORDER BY to be
3127	 * implemented using incremental sort.  Also, give up if there are any
3128	 * non-sortable GROUP BY items, since then there's no hope anyway.
3129	 */
3130	foreach(gl, parse->groupClause)
3131	{
3132		SortGroupClause *gc = lfirst_node(SortGroupClause, gl);
3133
3134		if (list_member_ptr(new_groupclause, gc))
3135			continue;			/* it matched an ORDER BY item */
3136		if (!OidIsValid(gc->sortop))	/* give up, GROUP BY can't be sorted */
3137			return list_copy(parse->groupClause);
3138		new_groupclause = lappend(new_groupclause, gc);
3139	}
3140
3141	/* Success --- install the rearranged GROUP BY list */
3142	Assert(list_length(parse->groupClause) == list_length(new_groupclause));
3143	return new_groupclause;
3144}
3145
3146/*
3147 * Extract lists of grouping sets that can be implemented using a single
3148 * rollup-type aggregate pass each. Returns a list of lists of grouping sets.
3149 *
3150 * Input must be sorted with smallest sets first. Result has each sublist
3151 * sorted with smallest sets first.
3152 *
3153 * We want to produce the absolute minimum possible number of lists here to
3154 * avoid excess sorts. Fortunately, there is an algorithm for this; the problem
3155 * of finding the minimal partition of a partially-ordered set into chains
3156 * (which is what we need, taking the list of grouping sets as a poset ordered
3157 * by set inclusion) can be mapped to the problem of finding the maximum
3158 * cardinality matching on a bipartite graph, which is solvable in polynomial
3159 * time with a worst case of no worse than O(n^2.5) and usually much
3160 * better. Since our N is at most 4096, we don't need to consider fallbacks to
3161 * heuristic or approximate methods.  (Planning time for a 12-d cube is under
3162 * half a second on my modest system even with optimization off and assertions
3163 * on.)
3164 */
3165static List *
3166extract_rollup_sets(List *groupingSets)
3167{
3168	int			num_sets_raw = list_length(groupingSets);
3169	int			num_empty = 0;
3170	int			num_sets = 0;	/* distinct sets */
3171	int			num_chains = 0;
3172	List	   *result = NIL;
3173	List	  **results;
3174	List	  **orig_sets;
3175	Bitmapset **set_masks;
3176	int		   *chains;
3177	short	  **adjacency;
3178	short	   *adjacency_buf;
3179	BipartiteMatchState *state;
3180	int			i;
3181	int			j;
3182	int			j_size;
3183	ListCell   *lc1 = list_head(groupingSets);
3184	ListCell   *lc;
3185
3186	/*
3187	 * Start by stripping out empty sets.  The algorithm doesn't require this,
3188	 * but the planner currently needs all empty sets to be returned in the
3189	 * first list, so we strip them here and add them back after.
3190	 */
3191	while (lc1 && lfirst(lc1) == NIL)
3192	{
3193		++num_empty;
3194		lc1 = lnext(groupingSets, lc1);
3195	}
3196
3197	/* bail out now if it turns out that all we had were empty sets. */
3198	if (!lc1)
3199		return list_make1(groupingSets);
3200
3201	/*----------
3202	 * We don't strictly need to remove duplicate sets here, but if we don't,
3203	 * they tend to become scattered through the result, which is a bit
3204	 * confusing (and irritating if we ever decide to optimize them out).
3205	 * So we remove them here and add them back after.
3206	 *
3207	 * For each non-duplicate set, we fill in the following:
3208	 *
3209	 * orig_sets[i] = list of the original set lists
3210	 * set_masks[i] = bitmapset for testing inclusion
3211	 * adjacency[i] = array [n, v1, v2, ... vn] of adjacency indices
3212	 *
3213	 * chains[i] will be the result group this set is assigned to.
3214	 *
3215	 * We index all of these from 1 rather than 0 because it is convenient
3216	 * to leave 0 free for the NIL node in the graph algorithm.
3217	 *----------
3218	 */
3219	orig_sets = palloc0((num_sets_raw + 1) * sizeof(List *));
3220	set_masks = palloc0((num_sets_raw + 1) * sizeof(Bitmapset *));
3221	adjacency = palloc0((num_sets_raw + 1) * sizeof(short *));
3222	adjacency_buf = palloc((num_sets_raw + 1) * sizeof(short));
3223
3224	j_size = 0;
3225	j = 0;
3226	i = 1;
3227
3228	for_each_cell(lc, groupingSets, lc1)
3229	{
3230		List	   *candidate = (List *) lfirst(lc);
3231		Bitmapset  *candidate_set = NULL;
3232		ListCell   *lc2;
3233		int			dup_of = 0;
3234
3235		foreach(lc2, candidate)
3236		{
3237			candidate_set = bms_add_member(candidate_set, lfirst_int(lc2));
3238		}
3239
3240		/* we can only be a dup if we're the same length as a previous set */
3241		if (j_size == list_length(candidate))
3242		{
3243			int			k;
3244
3245			for (k = j; k < i; ++k)
3246			{
3247				if (bms_equal(set_masks[k], candidate_set))
3248				{
3249					dup_of = k;
3250					break;
3251				}
3252			}
3253		}
3254		else if (j_size < list_length(candidate))
3255		{
3256			j_size = list_length(candidate);
3257			j = i;
3258		}
3259
3260		if (dup_of > 0)
3261		{
3262			orig_sets[dup_of] = lappend(orig_sets[dup_of], candidate);
3263			bms_free(candidate_set);
3264		}
3265		else
3266		{
3267			int			k;
3268			int			n_adj = 0;
3269
3270			orig_sets[i] = list_make1(candidate);
3271			set_masks[i] = candidate_set;
3272
3273			/* fill in adjacency list; no need to compare equal-size sets */
3274
3275			for (k = j - 1; k > 0; --k)
3276			{
3277				if (bms_is_subset(set_masks[k], candidate_set))
3278					adjacency_buf[++n_adj] = k;
3279			}
3280
3281			if (n_adj > 0)
3282			{
3283				adjacency_buf[0] = n_adj;
3284				adjacency[i] = palloc((n_adj + 1) * sizeof(short));
3285				memcpy(adjacency[i], adjacency_buf, (n_adj + 1) * sizeof(short));
3286			}
3287			else
3288				adjacency[i] = NULL;
3289
3290			++i;
3291		}
3292	}
3293
3294	num_sets = i - 1;
3295
3296	/*
3297	 * Apply the graph matching algorithm to do the work.
3298	 */
3299	state = BipartiteMatch(num_sets, num_sets, adjacency);
3300
3301	/*
3302	 * Now, the state->pair* fields have the info we need to assign sets to
3303	 * chains. Two sets (u,v) belong to the same chain if pair_uv[u] = v or
3304	 * pair_vu[v] = u (both will be true, but we check both so that we can do
3305	 * it in one pass)
3306	 */
3307	chains = palloc0((num_sets + 1) * sizeof(int));
3308
3309	for (i = 1; i <= num_sets; ++i)
3310	{
3311		int			u = state->pair_vu[i];
3312		int			v = state->pair_uv[i];
3313
3314		if (u > 0 && u < i)
3315			chains[i] = chains[u];
3316		else if (v > 0 && v < i)
3317			chains[i] = chains[v];
3318		else
3319			chains[i] = ++num_chains;
3320	}
3321
3322	/* build result lists. */
3323	results = palloc0((num_chains + 1) * sizeof(List *));
3324
3325	for (i = 1; i <= num_sets; ++i)
3326	{
3327		int			c = chains[i];
3328
3329		Assert(c > 0);
3330
3331		results[c] = list_concat(results[c], orig_sets[i]);
3332	}
3333
3334	/* push any empty sets back on the first list. */
3335	while (num_empty-- > 0)
3336		results[1] = lcons(NIL, results[1]);
3337
3338	/* make result list */
3339	for (i = 1; i <= num_chains; ++i)
3340		result = lappend(result, results[i]);
3341
3342	/*
3343	 * Free all the things.
3344	 *
3345	 * (This is over-fussy for small sets but for large sets we could have
3346	 * tied up a nontrivial amount of memory.)
3347	 */
3348	BipartiteMatchFree(state);
3349	pfree(results);
3350	pfree(chains);
3351	for (i = 1; i <= num_sets; ++i)
3352		if (adjacency[i])
3353			pfree(adjacency[i]);
3354	pfree(adjacency);
3355	pfree(adjacency_buf);
3356	pfree(orig_sets);
3357	for (i = 1; i <= num_sets; ++i)
3358		bms_free(set_masks[i]);
3359	pfree(set_masks);
3360
3361	return result;
3362}
3363
3364/*
3365 * Reorder the elements of a list of grouping sets such that they have correct
3366 * prefix relationships. Also inserts the GroupingSetData annotations.
3367 *
3368 * The input must be ordered with smallest sets first; the result is returned
3369 * with largest sets first.  Note that the result shares no list substructure
3370 * with the input, so it's safe for the caller to modify it later.
3371 *
3372 * If we're passed in a sortclause, we follow its order of columns to the
3373 * extent possible, to minimize the chance that we add unnecessary sorts.
3374 * (We're trying here to ensure that GROUPING SETS ((a,b,c),(c)) ORDER BY c,b,a
3375 * gets implemented in one pass.)
3376 */
3377static List *
3378reorder_grouping_sets(List *groupingSets, List *sortclause)
3379{
3380	ListCell   *lc;
3381	List	   *previous = NIL;
3382	List	   *result = NIL;
3383
3384	foreach(lc, groupingSets)
3385	{
3386		List	   *candidate = (List *) lfirst(lc);
3387		List	   *new_elems = list_difference_int(candidate, previous);
3388		GroupingSetData *gs = makeNode(GroupingSetData);
3389
3390		while (list_length(sortclause) > list_length(previous) &&
3391			   new_elems != NIL)
3392		{
3393			SortGroupClause *sc = list_nth(sortclause, list_length(previous));
3394			int			ref = sc->tleSortGroupRef;
3395
3396			if (list_member_int(new_elems, ref))
3397			{
3398				previous = lappend_int(previous, ref);
3399				new_elems = list_delete_int(new_elems, ref);
3400			}
3401			else
3402			{
3403				/* diverged from the sortclause; give up on it */
3404				sortclause = NIL;
3405				break;
3406			}
3407		}
3408
3409		previous = list_concat(previous, new_elems);
3410
3411		gs->set = list_copy(previous);
3412		result = lcons(gs, result);
3413	}
3414
3415	list_free(previous);
3416
3417	return result;
3418}
3419
3420/*
3421 * has_volatile_pathkey
3422 *		Returns true if any PathKey in 'keys' has an EquivalenceClass
3423 *		containing a volatile function.  Otherwise returns false.
3424 */
3425static bool
3426has_volatile_pathkey(List *keys)
3427{
3428	ListCell   *lc;
3429
3430	foreach(lc, keys)
3431	{
3432		PathKey    *pathkey = lfirst_node(PathKey, lc);
3433
3434		if (pathkey->pk_eclass->ec_has_volatile)
3435			return true;
3436	}
3437
3438	return false;
3439}
3440
3441/*
3442 * adjust_group_pathkeys_for_groupagg
3443 *		Add pathkeys to root->group_pathkeys to reflect the best set of
3444 *		pre-ordered input for ordered aggregates.
3445 *
3446 * We define "best" as the pathkeys that suit the largest number of
3447 * aggregate functions.  We find these by looking at the first ORDER BY /
3448 * DISTINCT aggregate and take the pathkeys for that before searching for
3449 * other aggregates that require the same or a more strict variation of the
3450 * same pathkeys.  We then repeat that process for any remaining aggregates
3451 * with different pathkeys and if we find another set of pathkeys that suits a
3452 * larger number of aggregates then we select those pathkeys instead.
3453 *
3454 * When the best pathkeys are found we also mark each Aggref that can use
3455 * those pathkeys as aggpresorted = true.
3456 *
3457 * Note: When an aggregate function's ORDER BY / DISTINCT clause contains any
3458 * volatile functions, we never make use of these pathkeys.  We want to ensure
3459 * that sorts using volatile functions are done independently in each Aggref
3460 * rather than once at the query level.  If we were to allow this then Aggrefs
3461 * with compatible sort orders would all transition their rows in the same
3462 * order if those pathkeys were deemed to be the best pathkeys to sort on.
3463 * Whereas, if some other set of Aggref's pathkeys happened to be deemed
3464 * better pathkeys to sort on, then the volatile function Aggrefs would be
3465 * left to perform their sorts individually.  To avoid this inconsistent
3466 * behavior which could make Aggref results depend on what other Aggrefs the
3467 * query contains, we always force Aggrefs with volatile functions to perform
3468 * their own sorts.
3469 */
3470static void
3471adjust_group_pathkeys_for_groupagg(PlannerInfo *root)
3472{
3473	List	   *grouppathkeys = root->group_pathkeys;
3474	List	   *bestpathkeys;
3475	Bitmapset  *bestaggs;
3476	Bitmapset  *unprocessed_aggs;
3477	ListCell   *lc;
3478	int			i;
3479
3480	/* Shouldn't be here if there are grouping sets */
3481	Assert(root->parse->groupingSets == NIL);
3482	/* Shouldn't be here unless there are some ordered aggregates */
3483	Assert(root->numOrderedAggs > 0);
3484
3485	/* Do nothing if disabled */
3486	if (!enable_presorted_aggregate)
3487		return;
3488
3489	/*
3490	 * Make a first pass over all AggInfos to collect a Bitmapset containing
3491	 * the indexes of all AggInfos to be processed below.
3492	 */
3493	unprocessed_aggs = NULL;
3494	foreach(lc, root->agginfos)
3495	{
3496		AggInfo    *agginfo = lfirst_node(AggInfo, lc);
3497		Aggref	   *aggref = linitial_node(Aggref, agginfo->aggrefs);
3498
3499		if (AGGKIND_IS_ORDERED_SET(aggref->aggkind))
3500			continue;
3501
3502		/* Skip unless there's a DISTINCT or ORDER BY clause */
3503		if (aggref->aggdistinct == NIL && aggref->aggorder == NIL)
3504			continue;
3505
3506		/* Additional safety checks are needed if there's a FILTER clause */
3507		if (aggref->aggfilter != NULL)
3508		{
3509			ListCell   *lc2;
3510			bool		allow_presort = true;
3511
3512			/*
3513			 * When the Aggref has a FILTER clause, it's possible that the
3514			 * filter removes rows that cannot be sorted because the
3515			 * expression to sort by results in an error during its
3516			 * evaluation.  This is a problem for presorting as that happens
3517			 * before the FILTER, whereas without presorting, the Aggregate
3518			 * node will apply the FILTER *before* sorting.  So that we never
3519			 * try to sort anything that might error, here we aim to skip over
3520			 * any Aggrefs with arguments with expressions which, when
3521			 * evaluated, could cause an ERROR.  Vars and Consts are ok. There
3522			 * may be more cases that should be allowed, but more thought
3523			 * needs to be given.  Err on the side of caution.
3524			 */
3525			foreach(lc2, aggref->args)
3526			{
3527				TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(lc2);
3528				Expr	   *expr = tle->expr;
3529
3530				while (IsA(expr, RelabelType))
3531					expr = (Expr *) (castNode(RelabelType, expr))->arg;
3532
3533				/* Common case, Vars and Consts are ok */
3534				if (IsA(expr, Var) || IsA(expr, Const))
3535					continue;
3536
3537				/* Unsupported.  Don't try to presort for this Aggref */
3538				allow_presort = false;
3539				break;
3540			}
3541
3542			/* Skip unsupported Aggrefs */
3543			if (!allow_presort)
3544				continue;
3545		}
3546
3547		unprocessed_aggs = bms_add_member(unprocessed_aggs,
3548										  foreach_current_index(lc));
3549	}
3550
3551	/*
3552	 * Now process all the unprocessed_aggs to find the best set of pathkeys
3553	 * for the given set of aggregates.
3554	 *
3555	 * On the first outer loop here 'bestaggs' will be empty.   We'll populate
3556	 * this during the first loop using the pathkeys for the very first
3557	 * AggInfo then taking any stronger pathkeys from any other AggInfos with
3558	 * a more strict set of compatible pathkeys.  Once the outer loop is
3559	 * complete, we mark off all the aggregates with compatible pathkeys then
3560	 * remove those from the unprocessed_aggs and repeat the process to try to
3561	 * find another set of pathkeys that are suitable for a larger number of
3562	 * aggregates.  The outer loop will stop when there are not enough
3563	 * unprocessed aggregates for it to be possible to find a set of pathkeys
3564	 * to suit a larger number of aggregates.
3565	 */
3566	bestpathkeys = NIL;
3567	bestaggs = NULL;
3568	while (bms_num_members(unprocessed_aggs) > bms_num_members(bestaggs))
3569	{
3570		Bitmapset  *aggindexes = NULL;
3571		List	   *currpathkeys = NIL;
3572
3573		i = -1;
3574		while ((i = bms_next_member(unprocessed_aggs, i)) >= 0)
3575		{
3576			AggInfo    *agginfo = list_nth_node(AggInfo, root->agginfos, i);
3577			Aggref	   *aggref = linitial_node(Aggref, agginfo->aggrefs);
3578			List	   *sortlist;
3579			List	   *pathkeys;
3580
3581			if (aggref->aggdistinct != NIL)
3582				sortlist = aggref->aggdistinct;
3583			else
3584				sortlist = aggref->aggorder;
3585
3586			pathkeys = make_pathkeys_for_sortclauses(root, sortlist,
3587													 aggref->args);
3588
3589			/*
3590			 * Ignore Aggrefs which have volatile functions in their ORDER BY
3591			 * or DISTINCT clause.
3592			 */
3593			if (has_volatile_pathkey(pathkeys))
3594			{
3595				unprocessed_aggs = bms_del_member(unprocessed_aggs, i);
3596				continue;
3597			}
3598
3599			/*
3600			 * When not set yet, take the pathkeys from the first unprocessed
3601			 * aggregate.
3602			 */
3603			if (currpathkeys == NIL)
3604			{
3605				currpathkeys = pathkeys;
3606
3607				/* include the GROUP BY pathkeys, if they exist */
3608				if (grouppathkeys != NIL)
3609					currpathkeys = append_pathkeys(list_copy(grouppathkeys),
3610												   currpathkeys);
3611
3612				/* record that we found pathkeys for this aggregate */
3613				aggindexes = bms_add_member(aggindexes, i);
3614			}
3615			else
3616			{
3617				/* now look for a stronger set of matching pathkeys */
3618
3619				/* include the GROUP BY pathkeys, if they exist */
3620				if (grouppathkeys != NIL)
3621					pathkeys = append_pathkeys(list_copy(grouppathkeys),
3622											   pathkeys);
3623
3624				/* are 'pathkeys' compatible or better than 'currpathkeys'? */
3625				switch (compare_pathkeys(currpathkeys, pathkeys))
3626				{
3627					case PATHKEYS_BETTER2:
3628						/* 'pathkeys' are stronger, use these ones instead */
3629						currpathkeys = pathkeys;
3630						/* FALLTHROUGH */
3631
3632					case PATHKEYS_BETTER1:
3633						/* 'pathkeys' are less strict */
3634						/* FALLTHROUGH */
3635
3636					case PATHKEYS_EQUAL:
3637						/* mark this aggregate as covered by 'currpathkeys' */
3638						aggindexes = bms_add_member(aggindexes, i);
3639						break;
3640
3641					case PATHKEYS_DIFFERENT:
3642						break;
3643				}
3644			}
3645		}
3646
3647		/* remove the aggregates that we've just processed */
3648		unprocessed_aggs = bms_del_members(unprocessed_aggs, aggindexes);
3649
3650		/*
3651		 * If this pass included more aggregates than the previous best then
3652		 * use these ones as the best set.
3653		 */
3654		if (bms_num_members(aggindexes) > bms_num_members(bestaggs))
3655		{
3656			bestaggs = aggindexes;
3657			bestpathkeys = currpathkeys;
3658		}
3659	}
3660
3661	/*
3662	 * If we found any ordered aggregates, update root->group_pathkeys to add
3663	 * the best set of aggregate pathkeys.  Note that bestpathkeys includes
3664	 * the original GROUP BY pathkeys already.
3665	 */
3666	if (bestpathkeys != NIL)
3667		root->group_pathkeys = bestpathkeys;
3668
3669	/*
3670	 * Now that we've found the best set of aggregates we can set the
3671	 * presorted flag to indicate to the executor that it needn't bother
3672	 * performing a sort for these Aggrefs.  We're able to do this now as
3673	 * there's no chance of a Hash Aggregate plan as create_grouping_paths
3674	 * will not mark the GROUP BY as GROUPING_CAN_USE_HASH due to the presence
3675	 * of ordered aggregates.
3676	 */
3677	i = -1;
3678	while ((i = bms_next_member(bestaggs, i)) >= 0)
3679	{
3680		AggInfo    *agginfo = list_nth_node(AggInfo, root->agginfos, i);
3681
3682		foreach(lc, agginfo->aggrefs)
3683		{
3684			Aggref	   *aggref = lfirst_node(Aggref, lc);
3685
3686			aggref->aggpresorted = true;
3687		}
3688	}
3689}
3690
3691/*
3692 * Compute query_pathkeys and other pathkeys during plan generation
3693 */
3694static void
3695standard_qp_callback(PlannerInfo *root, void *extra)
3696{
3697	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
3698	standard_qp_extra *qp_extra = (standard_qp_extra *) extra;
3699	List	   *tlist = root->processed_tlist;
3700	List	   *activeWindows = qp_extra->activeWindows;
3701
3702	/*
3703	 * Calculate pathkeys that represent grouping/ordering and/or ordered
3704	 * aggregate requirements.
3705	 */
3706	if (qp_extra->gset_data)
3707	{
3708		/*
3709		 * With grouping sets, just use the first RollupData's groupClause. We
3710		 * don't make any effort to optimize grouping clauses when there are
3711		 * grouping sets, nor can we combine aggregate ordering keys with
3712		 * grouping.
3713		 */
3714		List	   *rollups = qp_extra->gset_data->rollups;
3715		List	   *groupClause = (rollups ? linitial_node(RollupData, rollups)->groupClause : NIL);
3716
3717		if (grouping_is_sortable(groupClause))
3718		{
3719			bool		sortable;
3720
3721			/*
3722			 * The groupClause is logically below the grouping step.  So if
3723			 * there is an RTE entry for the grouping step, we need to remove
3724			 * its RT index from the sort expressions before we make PathKeys
3725			 * for them.
3726			 */
3727			root->group_pathkeys =
3728				make_pathkeys_for_sortclauses_extended(root,
3729													   &groupClause,
3730													   tlist,
3731													   false,
3732													   parse->hasGroupRTE,
3733													   &sortable,
3734													   false);
3735			Assert(sortable);
3736			root->num_groupby_pathkeys = list_length(root->group_pathkeys);
3737		}
3738		else
3739		{
3740			root->group_pathkeys = NIL;
3741			root->num_groupby_pathkeys = 0;
3742		}
3743	}
3744	else if (parse->groupClause || root->numOrderedAggs > 0)
3745	{
3746		/*
3747		 * With a plain GROUP BY list, we can remove any grouping items that
3748		 * are proven redundant by EquivalenceClass processing.  For example,
3749		 * we can remove y given "WHERE x = y GROUP BY x, y".  These aren't
3750		 * especially common cases, but they're nearly free to detect.  Note
3751		 * that we remove redundant items from processed_groupClause but not
3752		 * the original parse->groupClause.
3753		 */
3754		bool		sortable;
3755
3756		/*
3757		 * Convert group clauses into pathkeys.  Set the ec_sortref field of
3758		 * EquivalenceClass'es if it's not set yet.
3759		 */
3760		root->group_pathkeys =
3761			make_pathkeys_for_sortclauses_extended(root,
3762												   &root->processed_groupClause,
3763												   tlist,
3764												   true,
3765												   false,
3766												   &sortable,
3767												   true);
3768		if (!sortable)
3769		{
3770			/* Can't sort; no point in considering aggregate ordering either */
3771			root->group_pathkeys = NIL;
3772			root->num_groupby_pathkeys = 0;
3773		}
3774		else
3775		{
3776			root->num_groupby_pathkeys = list_length(root->group_pathkeys);
3777			/* If we have ordered aggs, consider adding onto group_pathkeys */
3778			if (root->numOrderedAggs > 0)
3779				adjust_group_pathkeys_for_groupagg(root);
3780		}
3781	}
3782	else
3783	{
3784		root->group_pathkeys = NIL;
3785		root->num_groupby_pathkeys = 0;
3786	}
3787
3788	/* We consider only the first (bottom) window in pathkeys logic */
3789	if (activeWindows != NIL)
3790	{
3791		WindowClause *wc = linitial_node(WindowClause, activeWindows);
3792
3793		root->window_pathkeys = make_pathkeys_for_window(root,
3794														 wc,
3795														 tlist);
3796	}
3797	else
3798		root->window_pathkeys = NIL;
3799
3800	/*
3801	 * As with GROUP BY, we can discard any DISTINCT items that are proven
3802	 * redundant by EquivalenceClass processing.  The non-redundant list is
3803	 * kept in root->processed_distinctClause, leaving the original
3804	 * parse->distinctClause alone.
3805	 */
3806	if (parse->distinctClause)
3807	{
3808		bool		sortable;
3809
3810		/* Make a copy since pathkey processing can modify the list */
3811		root->processed_distinctClause = list_copy(parse->distinctClause);
3812		root->distinct_pathkeys =
3813			make_pathkeys_for_sortclauses_extended(root,
3814												   &root->processed_distinctClause,
3815												   tlist,
3816												   true,
3817												   false,
3818												   &sortable,
3819												   false);
3820		if (!sortable)
3821			root->distinct_pathkeys = NIL;
3822	}
3823	else
3824		root->distinct_pathkeys = NIL;
3825
3826	root->sort_pathkeys =
3827		make_pathkeys_for_sortclauses(root,
3828									  parse->sortClause,
3829									  tlist);
3830
3831	/* setting setop_pathkeys might be useful to the union planner */
3832	if (qp_extra->setop != NULL)
3833	{
3834		List	   *groupClauses;
3835		bool		sortable;
3836
3837		groupClauses = generate_setop_child_grouplist(qp_extra->setop, tlist);
3838
3839		root->setop_pathkeys =
3840			make_pathkeys_for_sortclauses_extended(root,
3841												   &groupClauses,
3842												   tlist,
3843												   false,
3844												   false,
3845												   &sortable,
3846												   false);
3847		if (!sortable)
3848			root->setop_pathkeys = NIL;
3849	}
3850	else
3851		root->setop_pathkeys = NIL;
3852
3853	/*
3854	 * Figure out whether we want a sorted result from query_planner.
3855	 *
3856	 * If we have a sortable GROUP BY clause, then we want a result sorted
3857	 * properly for grouping.  Otherwise, if we have window functions to
3858	 * evaluate, we try to sort for the first window.  Otherwise, if there's a
3859	 * sortable DISTINCT clause that's more rigorous than the ORDER BY clause,
3860	 * we try to produce output that's sufficiently well sorted for the
3861	 * DISTINCT.  Otherwise, if there is an ORDER BY clause, we want to sort
3862	 * by the ORDER BY clause.  Otherwise, if we're a subquery being planned
3863	 * for a set operation which can benefit from presorted results and have a
3864	 * sortable targetlist, we want to sort by the target list.
3865	 *
3866	 * Note: if we have both ORDER BY and GROUP BY, and ORDER BY is a superset
3867	 * of GROUP BY, it would be tempting to request sort by ORDER BY --- but
3868	 * that might just leave us failing to exploit an available sort order at
3869	 * all.  Needs more thought.  The choice for DISTINCT versus ORDER BY is
3870	 * much easier, since we know that the parser ensured that one is a
3871	 * superset of the other.
3872	 */
3873	if (root->group_pathkeys)
3874		root->query_pathkeys = root->group_pathkeys;
3875	else if (root->window_pathkeys)
3876		root->query_pathkeys = root->window_pathkeys;
3877	else if (list_length(root->distinct_pathkeys) >
3878			 list_length(root->sort_pathkeys))
3879		root->query_pathkeys = root->distinct_pathkeys;
3880	else if (root->sort_pathkeys)
3881		root->query_pathkeys = root->sort_pathkeys;
3882	else if (root->setop_pathkeys != NIL)
3883		root->query_pathkeys = root->setop_pathkeys;
3884	else
3885		root->query_pathkeys = NIL;
3886}
3887
3888/*
3889 * Estimate number of groups produced by grouping clauses (1 if not grouping)
3890 *
3891 * path_rows: number of output rows from scan/join step
3892 * gd: grouping sets data including list of grouping sets and their clauses
3893 * target_list: target list containing group clause references
3894 *
3895 * If doing grouping sets, we also annotate the gsets data with the estimates
3896 * for each set and each individual rollup list, with a view to later
3897 * determining whether some combination of them could be hashed instead.
3898 */
3899static double
3900get_number_of_groups(PlannerInfo *root,
3901					 double path_rows,
3902					 grouping_sets_data *gd,
3903					 List *target_list)
3904{
3905	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
3906	double		dNumGroups;
3907
3908	if (parse->groupClause)
3909	{
3910		List	   *groupExprs;
3911
3912		if (parse->groupingSets)
3913		{
3914			/* Add up the estimates for each grouping set */
3915			ListCell   *lc;
3916
3917			Assert(gd);			/* keep Coverity happy */
3918
3919			dNumGroups = 0;
3920
3921			foreach(lc, gd->rollups)
3922			{
3923				RollupData *rollup = lfirst_node(RollupData, lc);
3924				ListCell   *lc2;
3925				ListCell   *lc3;
3926
3927				groupExprs = get_sortgrouplist_exprs(rollup->groupClause,
3928													 target_list);
3929
3930				rollup->numGroups = 0.0;
3931
3932				forboth(lc2, rollup->gsets, lc3, rollup->gsets_data)
3933				{
3934					List	   *gset = (List *) lfirst(lc2);
3935					GroupingSetData *gs = lfirst_node(GroupingSetData, lc3);
3936					double		numGroups = estimate_num_groups(root,
3937																groupExprs,
3938																path_rows,
3939																&gset,
3940																NULL);
3941
3942					gs->numGroups = numGroups;
3943					rollup->numGroups += numGroups;
3944				}
3945
3946				dNumGroups += rollup->numGroups;
3947			}
3948
3949			if (gd->hash_sets_idx)
3950			{
3951				ListCell   *lc2;
3952
3953				gd->dNumHashGroups = 0;
3954
3955				groupExprs = get_sortgrouplist_exprs(parse->groupClause,
3956													 target_list);
3957
3958				forboth(lc, gd->hash_sets_idx, lc2, gd->unsortable_sets)
3959				{
3960					List	   *gset = (List *) lfirst(lc);
3961					GroupingSetData *gs = lfirst_node(GroupingSetData, lc2);
3962					double		numGroups = estimate_num_groups(root,
3963																groupExprs,
3964																path_rows,
3965																&gset,
3966																NULL);
3967
3968					gs->numGroups = numGroups;
3969					gd->dNumHashGroups += numGroups;
3970				}
3971
3972				dNumGroups += gd->dNumHashGroups;
3973			}
3974		}
3975		else
3976		{
3977			/* Plain GROUP BY -- estimate based on optimized groupClause */
3978			groupExprs = get_sortgrouplist_exprs(root->processed_groupClause,
3979												 target_list);
3980
3981			dNumGroups = estimate_num_groups(root, groupExprs, path_rows,
3982											 NULL, NULL);
3983		}
3984	}
3985	else if (parse->groupingSets)
3986	{
3987		/* Empty grouping sets ... one result row for each one */
3988		dNumGroups = list_length(parse->groupingSets);
3989	}
3990	else if (parse->hasAggs || root->hasHavingQual)
3991	{
3992		/* Plain aggregation, one result row */
3993		dNumGroups = 1;
3994	}
3995	else
3996	{
3997		/* Not grouping */
3998		dNumGroups = 1;
3999	}
4000
4001	return dNumGroups;
4002}
4003
4004/*
4005 * create_grouping_paths
4006 *
4007 * Build a new upperrel containing Paths for grouping and/or aggregation.
4008 * Along the way, we also build an upperrel for Paths which are partially
4009 * grouped and/or aggregated.  A partially grouped and/or aggregated path
4010 * needs a FinalizeAggregate node to complete the aggregation.  Currently,
4011 * the only partially grouped paths we build are also partial paths; that
4012 * is, they need a Gather and then a FinalizeAggregate.
4013 *
4014 * input_rel: contains the source-data Paths
4015 * target: the pathtarget for the result Paths to compute
4016 * gd: grouping sets data including list of grouping sets and their clauses
4017 *
4018 * Note: all Paths in input_rel are expected to return the target computed
4019 * by make_group_input_target.
4020 */
4021static RelOptInfo *
4022create_grouping_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
4023					  RelOptInfo *input_rel,
4024					  PathTarget *target,
4025					  bool target_parallel_safe,
4026					  grouping_sets_data *gd)
4027{
4028	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
4029	RelOptInfo *grouped_rel;
4030	RelOptInfo *partially_grouped_rel;
4031	AggClauseCosts agg_costs;
4032
4033	MemSet(&agg_costs, 0, sizeof(AggClauseCosts));
4034	get_agg_clause_costs(root, AGGSPLIT_SIMPLE, &agg_costs);
4035
4036	/*
4037	 * Create grouping relation to hold fully aggregated grouping and/or
4038	 * aggregation paths.
4039	 */
4040	grouped_rel = make_grouping_rel(root, input_rel, target,
4041									target_parallel_safe, parse->havingQual);
4042
4043	/*
4044	 * Create either paths for a degenerate grouping or paths for ordinary
4045	 * grouping, as appropriate.
4046	 */
4047	if (is_degenerate_grouping(root))
4048		create_degenerate_grouping_paths(root, input_rel, grouped_rel);
4049	else
4050	{
4051		int			flags = 0;
4052		GroupPathExtraData extra;
4053
4054		/*
4055		 * Determine whether it's possible to perform sort-based
4056		 * implementations of grouping.  (Note that if processed_groupClause
4057		 * is empty, grouping_is_sortable() is trivially true, and all the
4058		 * pathkeys_contained_in() tests will succeed too, so that we'll
4059		 * consider every surviving input path.)
4060		 *
4061		 * If we have grouping sets, we might be able to sort some but not all
4062		 * of them; in this case, we need can_sort to be true as long as we
4063		 * must consider any sorted-input plan.
4064		 */
4065		if ((gd && gd->rollups != NIL)
4066			|| grouping_is_sortable(root->processed_groupClause))
4067			flags |= GROUPING_CAN_USE_SORT;
4068
4069		/*
4070		 * Determine whether we should consider hash-based implementations of
4071		 * grouping.
4072		 *
4073		 * Hashed aggregation only applies if we're grouping. If we have
4074		 * grouping sets, some groups might be hashable but others not; in
4075		 * this case we set can_hash true as long as there is nothing globally
4076		 * preventing us from hashing (and we should therefore consider plans
4077		 * with hashes).
4078		 *
4079		 * Executor doesn't support hashed aggregation with DISTINCT or ORDER
4080		 * BY aggregates.  (Doing so would imply storing *all* the input
4081		 * values in the hash table, and/or running many sorts in parallel,
4082		 * either of which seems like a certain loser.)  We similarly don't
4083		 * support ordered-set aggregates in hashed aggregation, but that case
4084		 * is also included in the numOrderedAggs count.
4085		 *
4086		 * Note: grouping_is_hashable() is much more expensive to check than
4087		 * the other gating conditions, so we want to do it last.
4088		 */
4089		if ((parse->groupClause != NIL &&
4090			 root->numOrderedAggs == 0 &&
4091			 (gd ? gd->any_hashable : grouping_is_hashable(root->processed_groupClause))))
4092			flags |= GROUPING_CAN_USE_HASH;
4093
4094		/*
4095		 * Determine whether partial aggregation is possible.
4096		 */
4097		if (can_partial_agg(root))
4098			flags |= GROUPING_CAN_PARTIAL_AGG;
4099
4100		extra.flags = flags;
4101		extra.target_parallel_safe = target_parallel_safe;
4102		extra.havingQual = parse->havingQual;
4103		extra.targetList = parse->targetList;
4104		extra.partial_costs_set = false;
4105
4106		/*
4107		 * Determine whether partitionwise aggregation is in theory possible.
4108		 * It can be disabled by the user, and for now, we don't try to
4109		 * support grouping sets.  create_ordinary_grouping_paths() will check
4110		 * additional conditions, such as whether input_rel is partitioned.
4111		 */
4112		if (enable_partitionwise_aggregate && !parse->groupingSets)
4113			extra.patype = PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_FULL;
4114		else
4115			extra.patype = PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_NONE;
4116
4117		create_ordinary_grouping_paths(root, input_rel, grouped_rel,
4118									   &agg_costs, gd, &extra,
4119									   &partially_grouped_rel);
4120	}
4121
4122	set_cheapest(grouped_rel);
4123	return grouped_rel;
4124}
4125
4126/*
4127 * make_grouping_rel
4128 *
4129 * Create a new grouping rel and set basic properties.
4130 *
4131 * input_rel represents the underlying scan/join relation.
4132 * target is the output expected from the grouping relation.
4133 */
4134static RelOptInfo *
4135make_grouping_rel(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *input_rel,
4136				  PathTarget *target, bool target_parallel_safe,
4137				  Node *havingQual)
4138{
4139	RelOptInfo *grouped_rel;
4140
4141	if (IS_OTHER_REL(input_rel))
4142	{
4143		grouped_rel = fetch_upper_rel(root, UPPERREL_GROUP_AGG,
4144									  input_rel->relids);
4145		grouped_rel->reloptkind = RELOPT_OTHER_UPPER_REL;
4146	}
4147	else
4148	{
4149		/*
4150		 * By tradition, the relids set for the main grouping relation is
4151		 * NULL.  (This could be changed, but might require adjustments
4152		 * elsewhere.)
4153		 */
4154		grouped_rel = fetch_upper_rel(root, UPPERREL_GROUP_AGG, NULL);
4155	}
4156
4157	/* Set target. */
4158	grouped_rel->reltarget = target;
4159
4160	/*
4161	 * If the input relation is not parallel-safe, then the grouped relation
4162	 * can't be parallel-safe, either.  Otherwise, it's parallel-safe if the
4163	 * target list and HAVING quals are parallel-safe.
4164	 */
4165	if (input_rel->consider_parallel && target_parallel_safe &&
4166		is_parallel_safe(root, (Node *) havingQual))
4167		grouped_rel->consider_parallel = true;
4168
4169	/*
4170	 * If the input rel belongs to a single FDW, so does the grouped rel.
4171	 */
4172	grouped_rel->serverid = input_rel->serverid;
4173	grouped_rel->userid = input_rel->userid;
4174	grouped_rel->useridiscurrent = input_rel->useridiscurrent;
4175	grouped_rel->fdwroutine = input_rel->fdwroutine;
4176
4177	return grouped_rel;
4178}
4179
4180/*
4181 * is_degenerate_grouping
4182 *
4183 * A degenerate grouping is one in which the query has a HAVING qual and/or
4184 * grouping sets, but no aggregates and no GROUP BY (which implies that the
4185 * grouping sets are all empty).
4186 */
4187static bool
4188is_degenerate_grouping(PlannerInfo *root)
4189{
4190	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
4191
4192	return (root->hasHavingQual || parse->groupingSets) &&
4193		!parse->hasAggs && parse->groupClause == NIL;
4194}
4195
4196/*
4197 * create_degenerate_grouping_paths
4198 *
4199 * When the grouping is degenerate (see is_degenerate_grouping), we are
4200 * supposed to emit either zero or one row for each grouping set depending on
4201 * whether HAVING succeeds.  Furthermore, there cannot be any variables in
4202 * either HAVING or the targetlist, so we actually do not need the FROM table
4203 * at all! We can just throw away the plan-so-far and generate a Result node.
4204 * This is a sufficiently unusual corner case that it's not worth contorting
4205 * the structure of this module to avoid having to generate the earlier paths
4206 * in the first place.
4207 */
4208static void
4209create_degenerate_grouping_paths(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *input_rel,
4210								 RelOptInfo *grouped_rel)
4211{
4212	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
4213	int			nrows;
4214	Path	   *path;
4215
4216	nrows = list_length(parse->groupingSets);
4217	if (nrows > 1)
4218	{
4219		/*
4220		 * Doesn't seem worthwhile writing code to cons up a generate_series
4221		 * or a values scan to emit multiple rows. Instead just make N clones
4222		 * and append them.  (With a volatile HAVING clause, this means you
4223		 * might get between 0 and N output rows. Offhand I think that's
4224		 * desired.)
4225		 */
4226		List	   *paths = NIL;
4227
4228		while (--nrows >= 0)
4229		{
4230			path = (Path *)
4231				create_group_result_path(root, grouped_rel,
4232										 grouped_rel->reltarget,
4233										 (List *) parse->havingQual);
4234			paths = lappend(paths, path);
4235		}
4236		path = (Path *)
4237			create_append_path(root,
4238							   grouped_rel,
4239							   paths,
4240							   NIL,
4241							   NIL,
4242							   NULL,
4243							   0,
4244							   false,
4245							   -1);
4246	}
4247	else
4248	{
4249		/* No grouping sets, or just one, so one output row */
4250		path = (Path *)
4251			create_group_result_path(root, grouped_rel,
4252									 grouped_rel->reltarget,
4253									 (List *) parse->havingQual);
4254	}
4255
4256	add_path(grouped_rel, path);
4257}
4258
4259/*
4260 * create_ordinary_grouping_paths
4261 *
4262 * Create grouping paths for the ordinary (that is, non-degenerate) case.
4263 *
4264 * We need to consider sorted and hashed aggregation in the same function,
4265 * because otherwise (1) it would be harder to throw an appropriate error
4266 * message if neither way works, and (2) we should not allow hashtable size
4267 * considerations to dissuade us from using hashing if sorting is not possible.
4268 *
4269 * *partially_grouped_rel_p will be set to the partially grouped rel which this
4270 * function creates, or to NULL if it doesn't create one.
4271 */
4272static void
4273create_ordinary_grouping_paths(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *input_rel,
4274							   RelOptInfo *grouped_rel,
4275							   const AggClauseCosts *agg_costs,
4276							   grouping_sets_data *gd,
4277							   GroupPathExtraData *extra,
4278							   RelOptInfo **partially_grouped_rel_p)
4279{
4280	Path	   *cheapest_path = input_rel->cheapest_total_path;
4281	RelOptInfo *partially_grouped_rel = NULL;
4282	double		dNumGroups;
4283	PartitionwiseAggregateType patype = PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_NONE;
4284
4285	/*
4286	 * If this is the topmost grouping relation or if the parent relation is
4287	 * doing some form of partitionwise aggregation, then we may be able to do
4288	 * it at this level also.  However, if the input relation is not
4289	 * partitioned, partitionwise aggregate is impossible.
4290	 */
4291	if (extra->patype != PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_NONE &&
4292		IS_PARTITIONED_REL(input_rel))
4293	{
4294		/*
4295		 * If this is the topmost relation or if the parent relation is doing
4296		 * full partitionwise aggregation, then we can do full partitionwise
4297		 * aggregation provided that the GROUP BY clause contains all of the
4298		 * partitioning columns at this level and the collation used by GROUP
4299		 * BY matches the partitioning collation.  Otherwise, we can do at
4300		 * most partial partitionwise aggregation.  But if partial aggregation
4301		 * is not supported in general then we can't use it for partitionwise
4302		 * aggregation either.
4303		 *
4304		 * Check parse->groupClause not processed_groupClause, because it's
4305		 * okay if some of the partitioning columns were proved redundant.
4306		 */
4307		if (extra->patype == PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_FULL &&
4308			group_by_has_partkey(input_rel, extra->targetList,
4309								 root->parse->groupClause))
4310			patype = PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_FULL;
4311		else if ((extra->flags & GROUPING_CAN_PARTIAL_AGG) != 0)
4312			patype = PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_PARTIAL;
4313		else
4314			patype = PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_NONE;
4315	}
4316
4317	/*
4318	 * Before generating paths for grouped_rel, we first generate any possible
4319	 * partially grouped paths; that way, later code can easily consider both
4320	 * parallel and non-parallel approaches to grouping.
4321	 */
4322	if ((extra->flags & GROUPING_CAN_PARTIAL_AGG) != 0)
4323	{
4324		bool		force_rel_creation;
4325
4326		/*
4327		 * If we're doing partitionwise aggregation at this level, force
4328		 * creation of a partially_grouped_rel so we can add partitionwise
4329		 * paths to it.
4330		 */
4331		force_rel_creation = (patype == PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_PARTIAL);
4332
4333		partially_grouped_rel =
4334			create_partial_grouping_paths(root,
4335										  grouped_rel,
4336										  input_rel,
4337										  gd,
4338										  extra,
4339										  force_rel_creation);
4340	}
4341
4342	/* Set out parameter. */
4343	*partially_grouped_rel_p = partially_grouped_rel;
4344
4345	/* Apply partitionwise aggregation technique, if possible. */
4346	if (patype != PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_NONE)
4347		create_partitionwise_grouping_paths(root, input_rel, grouped_rel,
4348											partially_grouped_rel, agg_costs,
4349											gd, patype, extra);
4350
4351	/* If we are doing partial aggregation only, return. */
4352	if (extra->patype == PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_PARTIAL)
4353	{
4354		Assert(partially_grouped_rel);
4355
4356		if (partially_grouped_rel->pathlist)
4357			set_cheapest(partially_grouped_rel);
4358
4359		return;
4360	}
4361
4362	/* Gather any partially grouped partial paths. */
4363	if (partially_grouped_rel && partially_grouped_rel->partial_pathlist)
4364	{
4365		gather_grouping_paths(root, partially_grouped_rel);
4366		set_cheapest(partially_grouped_rel);
4367	}
4368
4369	/*
4370	 * Estimate number of groups.
4371	 */
4372	dNumGroups = get_number_of_groups(root,
4373									  cheapest_path->rows,
4374									  gd,
4375									  extra->targetList);
4376
4377	/* Build final grouping paths */
4378	add_paths_to_grouping_rel(root, input_rel, grouped_rel,
4379							  partially_grouped_rel, agg_costs, gd,
4380							  dNumGroups, extra);
4381
4382	/* Give a helpful error if we failed to find any implementation */
4383	if (grouped_rel->pathlist == NIL)
4384		ereport(ERROR,
4385				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
4386				 errmsg("could not implement GROUP BY"),
4387				 errdetail("Some of the datatypes only support hashing, while others only support sorting.")));
4388
4389	/*
4390	 * If there is an FDW that's responsible for all baserels of the query,
4391	 * let it consider adding ForeignPaths.
4392	 */
4393	if (grouped_rel->fdwroutine &&
4394		grouped_rel->fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths)
4395		grouped_rel->fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths(root, UPPERREL_GROUP_AGG,
4396													  input_rel, grouped_rel,
4397													  extra);
4398
4399	/* Let extensions possibly add some more paths */
4400	if (create_upper_paths_hook)
4401		(*create_upper_paths_hook) (root, UPPERREL_GROUP_AGG,
4402									input_rel, grouped_rel,
4403									extra);
4404}
4405
4406/*
4407 * For a given input path, consider the possible ways of doing grouping sets on
4408 * it, by combinations of hashing and sorting.  This can be called multiple
4409 * times, so it's important that it not scribble on input.  No result is
4410 * returned, but any generated paths are added to grouped_rel.
4411 */
4412static void
4413consider_groupingsets_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
4414							RelOptInfo *grouped_rel,
4415							Path *path,
4416							bool is_sorted,
4417							bool can_hash,
4418							grouping_sets_data *gd,
4419							const AggClauseCosts *agg_costs,
4420							double dNumGroups)
4421{
4422	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
4423	Size		hash_mem_limit = get_hash_memory_limit();
4424
4425	/*
4426	 * If we're not being offered sorted input, then only consider plans that
4427	 * can be done entirely by hashing.
4428	 *
4429	 * We can hash everything if it looks like it'll fit in hash_mem. But if
4430	 * the input is actually sorted despite not being advertised as such, we
4431	 * prefer to make use of that in order to use less memory.
4432	 *
4433	 * If none of the grouping sets are sortable, then ignore the hash_mem
4434	 * limit and generate a path anyway, since otherwise we'll just fail.
4435	 */
4436	if (!is_sorted)
4437	{
4438		List	   *new_rollups = NIL;
4439		RollupData *unhashed_rollup = NULL;
4440		List	   *sets_data;
4441		List	   *empty_sets_data = NIL;
4442		List	   *empty_sets = NIL;
4443		ListCell   *lc;
4444		ListCell   *l_start = list_head(gd->rollups);
4445		AggStrategy strat = AGG_HASHED;
4446		double		hashsize;
4447		double		exclude_groups = 0.0;
4448
4449		Assert(can_hash);
4450
4451		/*
4452		 * If the input is coincidentally sorted usefully (which can happen
4453		 * even if is_sorted is false, since that only means that our caller
4454		 * has set up the sorting for us), then save some hashtable space by
4455		 * making use of that. But we need to watch out for degenerate cases:
4456		 *
4457		 * 1) If there are any empty grouping sets, then group_pathkeys might
4458		 * be NIL if all non-empty grouping sets are unsortable. In this case,
4459		 * there will be a rollup containing only empty groups, and the
4460		 * pathkeys_contained_in test is vacuously true; this is ok.
4461		 *
4462		 * XXX: the above relies on the fact that group_pathkeys is generated
