4463		 * from the first rollup. If we add the ability to consider multiple
4464		 * sort orders for grouping input, this assumption might fail.
4465		 *
4466		 * 2) If there are no empty sets and only unsortable sets, then the
4467		 * rollups list will be empty (and thus l_start == NULL), and
4468		 * group_pathkeys will be NIL; we must ensure that the vacuously-true
4469		 * pathkeys_contained_in test doesn't cause us to crash.
4470		 */
4471		if (l_start != NULL &&
4472			pathkeys_contained_in(root->group_pathkeys, path->pathkeys))
4473		{
4474			unhashed_rollup = lfirst_node(RollupData, l_start);
4475			exclude_groups = unhashed_rollup->numGroups;
4476			l_start = lnext(gd->rollups, l_start);
4477		}
4478
4479		hashsize = estimate_hashagg_tablesize(root,
4480											  path,
4481											  agg_costs,
4482											  dNumGroups - exclude_groups);
4483
4484		/*
4485		 * gd->rollups is empty if we have only unsortable columns to work
4486		 * with.  Override hash_mem in that case; otherwise, we'll rely on the
4487		 * sorted-input case to generate usable mixed paths.
4488		 */
4489		if (hashsize > hash_mem_limit && gd->rollups)
4490			return;				/* nope, won't fit */
4491
4492		/*
4493		 * We need to burst the existing rollups list into individual grouping
4494		 * sets and recompute a groupClause for each set.
4495		 */
4496		sets_data = list_copy(gd->unsortable_sets);
4497
4498		for_each_cell(lc, gd->rollups, l_start)
4499		{
4500			RollupData *rollup = lfirst_node(RollupData, lc);
4501
4502			/*
4503			 * If we find an unhashable rollup that's not been skipped by the
4504			 * "actually sorted" check above, we can't cope; we'd need sorted
4505			 * input (with a different sort order) but we can't get that here.
4506			 * So bail out; we'll get a valid path from the is_sorted case
4507			 * instead.
4508			 *
4509			 * The mere presence of empty grouping sets doesn't make a rollup
4510			 * unhashable (see preprocess_grouping_sets), we handle those
4511			 * specially below.
4512			 */
4513			if (!rollup->hashable)
4514				return;
4515
4516			sets_data = list_concat(sets_data, rollup->gsets_data);
4517		}
4518		foreach(lc, sets_data)
4519		{
4520			GroupingSetData *gs = lfirst_node(GroupingSetData, lc);
4521			List	   *gset = gs->set;
4522			RollupData *rollup;
4523
4524			if (gset == NIL)
4525			{
4526				/* Empty grouping sets can't be hashed. */
4527				empty_sets_data = lappend(empty_sets_data, gs);
4528				empty_sets = lappend(empty_sets, NIL);
4529			}
4530			else
4531			{
4532				rollup = makeNode(RollupData);
4533
4534				rollup->groupClause = preprocess_groupclause(root, gset);
4535				rollup->gsets_data = list_make1(gs);
4536				rollup->gsets = remap_to_groupclause_idx(rollup->groupClause,
4537														 rollup->gsets_data,
4538														 gd->tleref_to_colnum_map);
4539				rollup->numGroups = gs->numGroups;
4540				rollup->hashable = true;
4541				rollup->is_hashed = true;
4542				new_rollups = lappend(new_rollups, rollup);
4543			}
4544		}
4545
4546		/*
4547		 * If we didn't find anything nonempty to hash, then bail.  We'll
4548		 * generate a path from the is_sorted case.
4549		 */
4550		if (new_rollups == NIL)
4551			return;
4552
4553		/*
4554		 * If there were empty grouping sets they should have been in the
4555		 * first rollup.
4556		 */
4557		Assert(!unhashed_rollup || !empty_sets);
4558
4559		if (unhashed_rollup)
4560		{
4561			new_rollups = lappend(new_rollups, unhashed_rollup);
4562			strat = AGG_MIXED;
4563		}
4564		else if (empty_sets)
4565		{
4566			RollupData *rollup = makeNode(RollupData);
4567
4568			rollup->groupClause = NIL;
4569			rollup->gsets_data = empty_sets_data;
4570			rollup->gsets = empty_sets;
4571			rollup->numGroups = list_length(empty_sets);
4572			rollup->hashable = false;
4573			rollup->is_hashed = false;
4574			new_rollups = lappend(new_rollups, rollup);
4575			strat = AGG_MIXED;
4576		}
4577
4578		add_path(grouped_rel, (Path *)
4579				 create_groupingsets_path(root,
4580										  grouped_rel,
4581										  path,
4582										  (List *) parse->havingQual,
4583										  strat,
4584										  new_rollups,
4585										  agg_costs));
4586		return;
4587	}
4588
4589	/*
4590	 * If we have sorted input but nothing we can do with it, bail.
4591	 */
4592	if (gd->rollups == NIL)
4593		return;
4594
4595	/*
4596	 * Given sorted input, we try and make two paths: one sorted and one mixed
4597	 * sort/hash. (We need to try both because hashagg might be disabled, or
4598	 * some columns might not be sortable.)
4599	 *
4600	 * can_hash is passed in as false if some obstacle elsewhere (such as
4601	 * ordered aggs) means that we shouldn't consider hashing at all.
4602	 */
4603	if (can_hash && gd->any_hashable)
4604	{
4605		List	   *rollups = NIL;
4606		List	   *hash_sets = list_copy(gd->unsortable_sets);
4607		double		availspace = hash_mem_limit;
4608		ListCell   *lc;
4609
4610		/*
4611		 * Account first for space needed for groups we can't sort at all.
4612		 */
4613		availspace -= estimate_hashagg_tablesize(root,
4614												 path,
4615												 agg_costs,
4616												 gd->dNumHashGroups);
4617
4618		if (availspace > 0 && list_length(gd->rollups) > 1)
4619		{
4620			double		scale;
4621			int			num_rollups = list_length(gd->rollups);
4622			int			k_capacity;
4623			int		   *k_weights = palloc(num_rollups * sizeof(int));
4624			Bitmapset  *hash_items = NULL;
4625			int			i;
4626
4627			/*
4628			 * We treat this as a knapsack problem: the knapsack capacity
4629			 * represents hash_mem, the item weights are the estimated memory
4630			 * usage of the hashtables needed to implement a single rollup,
4631			 * and we really ought to use the cost saving as the item value;
4632			 * however, currently the costs assigned to sort nodes don't
4633			 * reflect the comparison costs well, and so we treat all items as
4634			 * of equal value (each rollup we hash instead saves us one sort).
4635			 *
4636			 * To use the discrete knapsack, we need to scale the values to a
4637			 * reasonably small bounded range.  We choose to allow a 5% error
4638			 * margin; we have no more than 4096 rollups in the worst possible
4639			 * case, which with a 5% error margin will require a bit over 42MB
4640			 * of workspace. (Anyone wanting to plan queries that complex had
4641			 * better have the memory for it.  In more reasonable cases, with
4642			 * no more than a couple of dozen rollups, the memory usage will
4643			 * be negligible.)
4644			 *
4645			 * k_capacity is naturally bounded, but we clamp the values for
4646			 * scale and weight (below) to avoid overflows or underflows (or
4647			 * uselessly trying to use a scale factor less than 1 byte).
4648			 */
4649			scale = Max(availspace / (20.0 * num_rollups), 1.0);
4650			k_capacity = (int) floor(availspace / scale);
4651
4652			/*
4653			 * We leave the first rollup out of consideration since it's the
4654			 * one that matches the input sort order.  We assign indexes "i"
4655			 * to only those entries considered for hashing; the second loop,
4656			 * below, must use the same condition.
4657			 */
4658			i = 0;
4659			for_each_from(lc, gd->rollups, 1)
4660			{
4661				RollupData *rollup = lfirst_node(RollupData, lc);
4662
4663				if (rollup->hashable)
4664				{
4665					double		sz = estimate_hashagg_tablesize(root,
4666																path,
4667																agg_costs,
4668																rollup->numGroups);
4669
4670					/*
4671					 * If sz is enormous, but hash_mem (and hence scale) is
4672					 * small, avoid integer overflow here.
4673					 */
4674					k_weights[i] = (int) Min(floor(sz / scale),
4675											 k_capacity + 1.0);
4676					++i;
4677				}
4678			}
4679
4680			/*
4681			 * Apply knapsack algorithm; compute the set of items which
4682			 * maximizes the value stored (in this case the number of sorts
4683			 * saved) while keeping the total size (approximately) within
4684			 * capacity.
4685			 */
4686			if (i > 0)
4687				hash_items = DiscreteKnapsack(k_capacity, i, k_weights, NULL);
4688
4689			if (!bms_is_empty(hash_items))
4690			{
4691				rollups = list_make1(linitial(gd->rollups));
4692
4693				i = 0;
4694				for_each_from(lc, gd->rollups, 1)
4695				{
4696					RollupData *rollup = lfirst_node(RollupData, lc);
4697
4698					if (rollup->hashable)
4699					{
4700						if (bms_is_member(i, hash_items))
4701							hash_sets = list_concat(hash_sets,
4702													rollup->gsets_data);
4703						else
4704							rollups = lappend(rollups, rollup);
4705						++i;
4706					}
4707					else
4708						rollups = lappend(rollups, rollup);
4709				}
4710			}
4711		}
4712
4713		if (!rollups && hash_sets)
4714			rollups = list_copy(gd->rollups);
4715
4716		foreach(lc, hash_sets)
4717		{
4718			GroupingSetData *gs = lfirst_node(GroupingSetData, lc);
4719			RollupData *rollup = makeNode(RollupData);
4720
4721			Assert(gs->set != NIL);
4722
4723			rollup->groupClause = preprocess_groupclause(root, gs->set);
4724			rollup->gsets_data = list_make1(gs);
4725			rollup->gsets = remap_to_groupclause_idx(rollup->groupClause,
4726													 rollup->gsets_data,
4727													 gd->tleref_to_colnum_map);
4728			rollup->numGroups = gs->numGroups;
4729			rollup->hashable = true;
4730			rollup->is_hashed = true;
4731			rollups = lcons(rollup, rollups);
4732		}
4733
4734		if (rollups)
4735		{
4736			add_path(grouped_rel, (Path *)
4737					 create_groupingsets_path(root,
4738											  grouped_rel,
4739											  path,
4740											  (List *) parse->havingQual,
4741											  AGG_MIXED,
4742											  rollups,
4743											  agg_costs));
4744		}
4745	}
4746
4747	/*
4748	 * Now try the simple sorted case.
4749	 */
4750	if (!gd->unsortable_sets)
4751		add_path(grouped_rel, (Path *)
4752				 create_groupingsets_path(root,
4753										  grouped_rel,
4754										  path,
4755										  (List *) parse->havingQual,
4756										  AGG_SORTED,
4757										  gd->rollups,
4758										  agg_costs));
4759}
4760
4761/*
4762 * create_window_paths
4763 *
4764 * Build a new upperrel containing Paths for window-function evaluation.
4765 *
4766 * input_rel: contains the source-data Paths
4767 * input_target: result of make_window_input_target
4768 * output_target: what the topmost WindowAggPath should return
4769 * wflists: result of find_window_functions
4770 * activeWindows: result of select_active_windows
4771 *
4772 * Note: all Paths in input_rel are expected to return input_target.
4773 */
4774static RelOptInfo *
4775create_window_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
4776					RelOptInfo *input_rel,
4777					PathTarget *input_target,
4778					PathTarget *output_target,
4779					bool output_target_parallel_safe,
4780					WindowFuncLists *wflists,
4781					List *activeWindows)
4782{
4783	RelOptInfo *window_rel;
4784	ListCell   *lc;
4785
4786	/* For now, do all work in the (WINDOW, NULL) upperrel */
4787	window_rel = fetch_upper_rel(root, UPPERREL_WINDOW, NULL);
4788
4789	/*
4790	 * If the input relation is not parallel-safe, then the window relation
4791	 * can't be parallel-safe, either.  Otherwise, we need to examine the
4792	 * target list and active windows for non-parallel-safe constructs.
4793	 */
4794	if (input_rel->consider_parallel && output_target_parallel_safe &&
4795		is_parallel_safe(root, (Node *) activeWindows))
4796		window_rel->consider_parallel = true;
4797
4798	/*
4799	 * If the input rel belongs to a single FDW, so does the window rel.
4800	 */
4801	window_rel->serverid = input_rel->serverid;
4802	window_rel->userid = input_rel->userid;
4803	window_rel->useridiscurrent = input_rel->useridiscurrent;
4804	window_rel->fdwroutine = input_rel->fdwroutine;
4805
4806	/*
4807	 * Consider computing window functions starting from the existing
4808	 * cheapest-total path (which will likely require a sort) as well as any
4809	 * existing paths that satisfy or partially satisfy root->window_pathkeys.
4810	 */
4811	foreach(lc, input_rel->pathlist)
4812	{
4813		Path	   *path = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
4814		int			presorted_keys;
4815
4816		if (path == input_rel->cheapest_total_path ||
4817			pathkeys_count_contained_in(root->window_pathkeys, path->pathkeys,
4818										&presorted_keys) ||
4819			presorted_keys > 0)
4820			create_one_window_path(root,
4821								   window_rel,
4822								   path,
4823								   input_target,
4824								   output_target,
4825								   wflists,
4826								   activeWindows);
4827	}
4828
4829	/*
4830	 * If there is an FDW that's responsible for all baserels of the query,
4831	 * let it consider adding ForeignPaths.
4832	 */
4833	if (window_rel->fdwroutine &&
4834		window_rel->fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths)
4835		window_rel->fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths(root, UPPERREL_WINDOW,
4836													 input_rel, window_rel,
4837													 NULL);
4838
4839	/* Let extensions possibly add some more paths */
4840	if (create_upper_paths_hook)
4841		(*create_upper_paths_hook) (root, UPPERREL_WINDOW,
4842									input_rel, window_rel, NULL);
4843
4844	/* Now choose the best path(s) */
4845	set_cheapest(window_rel);
4846
4847	return window_rel;
4848}
4849
4850/*
4851 * Stack window-function implementation steps atop the given Path, and
4852 * add the result to window_rel.
4853 *
4854 * window_rel: upperrel to contain result
4855 * path: input Path to use (must return input_target)
4856 * input_target: result of make_window_input_target
4857 * output_target: what the topmost WindowAggPath should return
4858 * wflists: result of find_window_functions
4859 * activeWindows: result of select_active_windows
4860 */
4861static void
4862create_one_window_path(PlannerInfo *root,
4863					   RelOptInfo *window_rel,
4864					   Path *path,
4865					   PathTarget *input_target,
4866					   PathTarget *output_target,
4867					   WindowFuncLists *wflists,
4868					   List *activeWindows)
4869{
4870	PathTarget *window_target;
4871	ListCell   *l;
4872	List	   *topqual = NIL;
4873
4874	/*
4875	 * Since each window clause could require a different sort order, we stack
4876	 * up a WindowAgg node for each clause, with sort steps between them as
4877	 * needed.  (We assume that select_active_windows chose a good order for
4878	 * executing the clauses in.)
4879	 *
4880	 * input_target should contain all Vars and Aggs needed for the result.
4881	 * (In some cases we wouldn't need to propagate all of these all the way
4882	 * to the top, since they might only be needed as inputs to WindowFuncs.
4883	 * It's probably not worth trying to optimize that though.)  It must also
4884	 * contain all window partitioning and sorting expressions, to ensure
4885	 * they're computed only once at the bottom of the stack (that's critical
4886	 * for volatile functions).  As we climb up the stack, we'll add outputs
4887	 * for the WindowFuncs computed at each level.
4888	 */
4889	window_target = input_target;
4890
4891	foreach(l, activeWindows)
4892	{
4893		WindowClause *wc = lfirst_node(WindowClause, l);
4894		List	   *window_pathkeys;
4895		List	   *runcondition = NIL;
4896		int			presorted_keys;
4897		bool		is_sorted;
4898		bool		topwindow;
4899		ListCell   *lc2;
4900
4901		window_pathkeys = make_pathkeys_for_window(root,
4902												   wc,
4903												   root->processed_tlist);
4904
4905		is_sorted = pathkeys_count_contained_in(window_pathkeys,
4906												path->pathkeys,
4907												&presorted_keys);
4908
4909		/* Sort if necessary */
4910		if (!is_sorted)
4911		{
4912			/*
4913			 * No presorted keys or incremental sort disabled, just perform a
4914			 * complete sort.
4915			 */
4916			if (presorted_keys == 0 || !enable_incremental_sort)
4917				path = (Path *) create_sort_path(root, window_rel,
4918												 path,
4919												 window_pathkeys,
4920												 -1.0);
4921			else
4922			{
4923				/*
4924				 * Since we have presorted keys and incremental sort is
4925				 * enabled, just use incremental sort.
4926				 */
4927				path = (Path *) create_incremental_sort_path(root,
4928															 window_rel,
4929															 path,
4930															 window_pathkeys,
4931															 presorted_keys,
4932															 -1.0);
4933			}
4934		}
4935
4936		if (lnext(activeWindows, l))
4937		{
4938			/*
4939			 * Add the current WindowFuncs to the output target for this
4940			 * intermediate WindowAggPath.  We must copy window_target to
4941			 * avoid changing the previous path's target.
4942			 *
4943			 * Note: a WindowFunc adds nothing to the target's eval costs; but
4944			 * we do need to account for the increase in tlist width.
4945			 */
4946			int64		tuple_width = window_target->width;
4947
4948			window_target = copy_pathtarget(window_target);
4949			foreach(lc2, wflists->windowFuncs[wc->winref])
4950			{
4951				WindowFunc *wfunc = lfirst_node(WindowFunc, lc2);
4952
4953				add_column_to_pathtarget(window_target, (Expr *) wfunc, 0);
4954				tuple_width += get_typavgwidth(wfunc->wintype, -1);
4955			}
4956			window_target->width = clamp_width_est(tuple_width);
4957		}
4958		else
4959		{
4960			/* Install the goal target in the topmost WindowAgg */
4961			window_target = output_target;
4962		}
4963
4964		/* mark the final item in the list as the top-level window */
4965		topwindow = foreach_current_index(l) == list_length(activeWindows) - 1;
4966
4967		/*
4968		 * Collect the WindowFuncRunConditions from each WindowFunc and
4969		 * convert them into OpExprs
4970		 */
4971		foreach(lc2, wflists->windowFuncs[wc->winref])
4972		{
4973			ListCell   *lc3;
4974			WindowFunc *wfunc = lfirst_node(WindowFunc, lc2);
4975
4976			foreach(lc3, wfunc->runCondition)
4977			{
4978				WindowFuncRunCondition *wfuncrc =
4979					lfirst_node(WindowFuncRunCondition, lc3);
4980				Expr	   *opexpr;
4981				Expr	   *leftop;
4982				Expr	   *rightop;
4983
4984				if (wfuncrc->wfunc_left)
4985				{
4986					leftop = (Expr *) copyObject(wfunc);
4987					rightop = copyObject(wfuncrc->arg);
4988				}
4989				else
4990				{
4991					leftop = copyObject(wfuncrc->arg);
4992					rightop = (Expr *) copyObject(wfunc);
4993				}
4994
4995				opexpr = make_opclause(wfuncrc->opno,
4996									   BOOLOID,
4997									   false,
4998									   leftop,
4999									   rightop,
5000									   InvalidOid,
5001									   wfuncrc->inputcollid);
5002
5003				runcondition = lappend(runcondition, opexpr);
5004
5005				if (!topwindow)
5006					topqual = lappend(topqual, opexpr);
5007			}
5008		}
5009
5010		path = (Path *)
5011			create_windowagg_path(root, window_rel, path, window_target,
5012								  wflists->windowFuncs[wc->winref],
5013								  runcondition, wc,
5014								  topwindow ? topqual : NIL, topwindow);
5015	}
5016
5017	add_path(window_rel, path);
5018}
5019
5020/*
5021 * create_distinct_paths
5022 *
5023 * Build a new upperrel containing Paths for SELECT DISTINCT evaluation.
5024 *
5025 * input_rel: contains the source-data Paths
5026 * target: the pathtarget for the result Paths to compute
5027 *
5028 * Note: input paths should already compute the desired pathtarget, since
5029 * Sort/Unique won't project anything.
5030 */
5031static RelOptInfo *
5032create_distinct_paths(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *input_rel,
5033					  PathTarget *target)
5034{
5035	RelOptInfo *distinct_rel;
5036
5037	/* For now, do all work in the (DISTINCT, NULL) upperrel */
5038	distinct_rel = fetch_upper_rel(root, UPPERREL_DISTINCT, NULL);
5039
5040	/*
5041	 * We don't compute anything at this level, so distinct_rel will be
5042	 * parallel-safe if the input rel is parallel-safe.  In particular, if
5043	 * there is a DISTINCT ON (...) clause, any path for the input_rel will
5044	 * output those expressions, and will not be parallel-safe unless those
5045	 * expressions are parallel-safe.
5046	 */
5047	distinct_rel->consider_parallel = input_rel->consider_parallel;
5048
5049	/*
5050	 * If the input rel belongs to a single FDW, so does the distinct_rel.
5051	 */
5052	distinct_rel->serverid = input_rel->serverid;
5053	distinct_rel->userid = input_rel->userid;
5054	distinct_rel->useridiscurrent = input_rel->useridiscurrent;
5055	distinct_rel->fdwroutine = input_rel->fdwroutine;
5056
5057	/* build distinct paths based on input_rel's pathlist */
5058	create_final_distinct_paths(root, input_rel, distinct_rel);
5059
5060	/* now build distinct paths based on input_rel's partial_pathlist */
5061	create_partial_distinct_paths(root, input_rel, distinct_rel, target);
5062
5063	/* Give a helpful error if we failed to create any paths */
5064	if (distinct_rel->pathlist == NIL)
5065		ereport(ERROR,
5066				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
5067				 errmsg("could not implement DISTINCT"),
5068				 errdetail("Some of the datatypes only support hashing, while others only support sorting.")));
5069
5070	/*
5071	 * If there is an FDW that's responsible for all baserels of the query,
5072	 * let it consider adding ForeignPaths.
5073	 */
5074	if (distinct_rel->fdwroutine &&
5075		distinct_rel->fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths)
5076		distinct_rel->fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths(root,
5077													   UPPERREL_DISTINCT,
5078													   input_rel,
5079													   distinct_rel,
5080													   NULL);
5081
5082	/* Let extensions possibly add some more paths */
5083	if (create_upper_paths_hook)
5084		(*create_upper_paths_hook) (root, UPPERREL_DISTINCT, input_rel,
5085									distinct_rel, NULL);
5086
5087	/* Now choose the best path(s) */
5088	set_cheapest(distinct_rel);
5089
5090	return distinct_rel;
5091}
5092
5093/*
5094 * create_partial_distinct_paths
5095 *
5096 * Process 'input_rel' partial paths and add unique/aggregate paths to the
5097 * UPPERREL_PARTIAL_DISTINCT rel.  For paths created, add Gather/GatherMerge
5098 * paths on top and add a final unique/aggregate path to remove any duplicate
5099 * produced from combining rows from parallel workers.
5100 */
5101static void
5102create_partial_distinct_paths(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *input_rel,
5103							  RelOptInfo *final_distinct_rel,
5104							  PathTarget *target)
5105{
5106	RelOptInfo *partial_distinct_rel;
5107	Query	   *parse;
5108	List	   *distinctExprs;
5109	double		numDistinctRows;
5110	Path	   *cheapest_partial_path;
5111	ListCell   *lc;
5112
5113	/* nothing to do when there are no partial paths in the input rel */
5114	if (!input_rel->consider_parallel || input_rel->partial_pathlist == NIL)
5115		return;
5116
5117	parse = root->parse;
5118
5119	/* can't do parallel DISTINCT ON */
5120	if (parse->hasDistinctOn)
5121		return;
5122
5123	partial_distinct_rel = fetch_upper_rel(root, UPPERREL_PARTIAL_DISTINCT,
5124										   NULL);
5125	partial_distinct_rel->reltarget = target;
5126	partial_distinct_rel->consider_parallel = input_rel->consider_parallel;
5127
5128	/*
5129	 * If input_rel belongs to a single FDW, so does the partial_distinct_rel.
5130	 */
5131	partial_distinct_rel->serverid = input_rel->serverid;
5132	partial_distinct_rel->userid = input_rel->userid;
5133	partial_distinct_rel->useridiscurrent = input_rel->useridiscurrent;
5134	partial_distinct_rel->fdwroutine = input_rel->fdwroutine;
5135
5136	cheapest_partial_path = linitial(input_rel->partial_pathlist);
5137
5138	distinctExprs = get_sortgrouplist_exprs(root->processed_distinctClause,
5139											parse->targetList);
5140
5141	/* estimate how many distinct rows we'll get from each worker */
5142	numDistinctRows = estimate_num_groups(root, distinctExprs,
5143										  cheapest_partial_path->rows,
5144										  NULL, NULL);
5145
5146	/*
5147	 * Try sorting the cheapest path and incrementally sorting any paths with
5148	 * presorted keys and put a unique paths atop of those.  We'll also
5149	 * attempt to reorder the required pathkeys to match the input path's
5150	 * pathkeys as much as possible, in hopes of avoiding a possible need to
5151	 * re-sort.
5152	 */
5153	if (grouping_is_sortable(root->processed_distinctClause))
5154	{
5155		foreach(lc, input_rel->partial_pathlist)
5156		{
5157			Path	   *input_path = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
5158			Path	   *sorted_path;
5159			List	   *useful_pathkeys_list = NIL;
5160
5161			useful_pathkeys_list =
5162				get_useful_pathkeys_for_distinct(root,
5163												 root->distinct_pathkeys,
5164												 input_path->pathkeys);
5165			Assert(list_length(useful_pathkeys_list) > 0);
5166
5167			foreach_node(List, useful_pathkeys, useful_pathkeys_list)
5168			{
5169				sorted_path = make_ordered_path(root,
5170												partial_distinct_rel,
5171												input_path,
5172												cheapest_partial_path,
5173												useful_pathkeys,
5174												-1.0);
5175
5176				if (sorted_path == NULL)
5177					continue;
5178
5179				/*
5180				 * An empty distinct_pathkeys means all tuples have the same
5181				 * value for the DISTINCT clause.  See
5182				 * create_final_distinct_paths()
5183				 */
5184				if (root->distinct_pathkeys == NIL)
5185				{
5186					Node	   *limitCount;
5187
5188					limitCount = (Node *) makeConst(INT8OID, -1, InvalidOid,
5189													sizeof(int64),
5190													Int64GetDatum(1), false,
5191													FLOAT8PASSBYVAL);
5192
5193					/*
5194					 * Apply a LimitPath onto the partial path to restrict the
5195					 * tuples from each worker to 1.
5196					 * create_final_distinct_paths will need to apply an
5197					 * additional LimitPath to restrict this to a single row
5198					 * after the Gather node.  If the query already has a
5199					 * LIMIT clause, then we could end up with three Limit
5200					 * nodes in the final plan.  Consolidating the top two of
5201					 * these could be done, but does not seem worth troubling
5202					 * over.
5203					 */
5204					add_partial_path(partial_distinct_rel, (Path *)
5205									 create_limit_path(root, partial_distinct_rel,
5206													   sorted_path,
5207													   NULL,
5208													   limitCount,
5209													   LIMIT_OPTION_COUNT,
5210													   0, 1));
5211				}
5212				else
5213				{
5214					add_partial_path(partial_distinct_rel, (Path *)
5215									 create_upper_unique_path(root, partial_distinct_rel,
5216															  sorted_path,
5217															  list_length(root->distinct_pathkeys),
5218															  numDistinctRows));
5219				}
5220			}
5221		}
5222	}
5223
5224	/*
5225	 * Now try hash aggregate paths, if enabled and hashing is possible. Since
5226	 * we're not on the hook to ensure we do our best to create at least one
5227	 * path here, we treat enable_hashagg as a hard off-switch rather than the
5228	 * slightly softer variant in create_final_distinct_paths.
5229	 */
5230	if (enable_hashagg && grouping_is_hashable(root->processed_distinctClause))
5231	{
5232		add_partial_path(partial_distinct_rel, (Path *)
5233						 create_agg_path(root,
5234										 partial_distinct_rel,
5235										 cheapest_partial_path,
5236										 cheapest_partial_path->pathtarget,
5237										 AGG_HASHED,
5238										 AGGSPLIT_SIMPLE,
5239										 root->processed_distinctClause,
5240										 NIL,
5241										 NULL,
5242										 numDistinctRows));
5243	}
5244
5245	/*
5246	 * If there is an FDW that's responsible for all baserels of the query,
5247	 * let it consider adding ForeignPaths.
5248	 */
5249	if (partial_distinct_rel->fdwroutine &&
5250		partial_distinct_rel->fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths)
5251		partial_distinct_rel->fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths(root,
5252															   UPPERREL_PARTIAL_DISTINCT,
5253															   input_rel,
5254															   partial_distinct_rel,
5255															   NULL);
5256
5257	/* Let extensions possibly add some more partial paths */
5258	if (create_upper_paths_hook)
5259		(*create_upper_paths_hook) (root, UPPERREL_PARTIAL_DISTINCT,
5260									input_rel, partial_distinct_rel, NULL);
5261
5262	if (partial_distinct_rel->partial_pathlist != NIL)
5263	{
5264		generate_useful_gather_paths(root, partial_distinct_rel, true);
5265		set_cheapest(partial_distinct_rel);
5266
5267		/*
5268		 * Finally, create paths to distinctify the final result.  This step
5269		 * is needed to remove any duplicates due to combining rows from
5270		 * parallel workers.
5271		 */
5272		create_final_distinct_paths(root, partial_distinct_rel,
5273									final_distinct_rel);
5274	}
5275}
5276
5277/*
5278 * create_final_distinct_paths
5279 *		Create distinct paths in 'distinct_rel' based on 'input_rel' pathlist
5280 *
5281 * input_rel: contains the source-data paths
5282 * distinct_rel: destination relation for storing created paths
5283 */
5284static RelOptInfo *
5285create_final_distinct_paths(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *input_rel,
5286							RelOptInfo *distinct_rel)
5287{
5288	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
5289	Path	   *cheapest_input_path = input_rel->cheapest_total_path;
5290	double		numDistinctRows;
5291	bool		allow_hash;
5292
5293	/* Estimate number of distinct rows there will be */
5294	if (parse->groupClause || parse->groupingSets || parse->hasAggs ||
5295		root->hasHavingQual)
5296	{
5297		/*
5298		 * If there was grouping or aggregation, use the number of input rows
5299		 * as the estimated number of DISTINCT rows (ie, assume the input is
5300		 * already mostly unique).
5301		 */
5302		numDistinctRows = cheapest_input_path->rows;
5303	}
5304	else
5305	{
5306		/*
5307		 * Otherwise, the UNIQUE filter has effects comparable to GROUP BY.
5308		 */
5309		List	   *distinctExprs;
5310
5311		distinctExprs = get_sortgrouplist_exprs(root->processed_distinctClause,
5312												parse->targetList);
5313		numDistinctRows = estimate_num_groups(root, distinctExprs,
5314											  cheapest_input_path->rows,
5315											  NULL, NULL);
5316	}
5317
5318	/*
5319	 * Consider sort-based implementations of DISTINCT, if possible.
5320	 */
5321	if (grouping_is_sortable(root->processed_distinctClause))
5322	{
5323		/*
5324		 * Firstly, if we have any adequately-presorted paths, just stick a
5325		 * Unique node on those.  We also, consider doing an explicit sort of
5326		 * the cheapest input path and Unique'ing that.  If any paths have
5327		 * presorted keys then we'll create an incremental sort atop of those
5328		 * before adding a unique node on the top.  We'll also attempt to
5329		 * reorder the required pathkeys to match the input path's pathkeys as
5330		 * much as possible, in hopes of avoiding a possible need to re-sort.
5331		 *
5332		 * When we have DISTINCT ON, we must sort by the more rigorous of
5333		 * DISTINCT and ORDER BY, else it won't have the desired behavior.
5334		 * Also, if we do have to do an explicit sort, we might as well use
5335		 * the more rigorous ordering to avoid a second sort later.  (Note
5336		 * that the parser will have ensured that one clause is a prefix of
5337		 * the other.)
5338		 */
5339		List	   *needed_pathkeys;
5340		ListCell   *lc;
5341		double		limittuples = root->distinct_pathkeys == NIL ? 1.0 : -1.0;
5342
5343		if (parse->hasDistinctOn &&
5344			list_length(root->distinct_pathkeys) <
5345			list_length(root->sort_pathkeys))
5346			needed_pathkeys = root->sort_pathkeys;
5347		else
5348			needed_pathkeys = root->distinct_pathkeys;
5349
5350		foreach(lc, input_rel->pathlist)
5351		{
5352			Path	   *input_path = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
5353			Path	   *sorted_path;
5354			List	   *useful_pathkeys_list = NIL;
5355
5356			useful_pathkeys_list =
5357				get_useful_pathkeys_for_distinct(root,
5358												 needed_pathkeys,
5359												 input_path->pathkeys);
5360			Assert(list_length(useful_pathkeys_list) > 0);
5361
5362			foreach_node(List, useful_pathkeys, useful_pathkeys_list)
5363			{
5364				sorted_path = make_ordered_path(root,
5365												distinct_rel,
5366												input_path,
5367												cheapest_input_path,
5368												useful_pathkeys,
5369												limittuples);
5370
5371				if (sorted_path == NULL)
5372					continue;
5373
5374				/*
5375				 * distinct_pathkeys may have become empty if all of the
5376				 * pathkeys were determined to be redundant.  If all of the
5377				 * pathkeys are redundant then each DISTINCT target must only
5378				 * allow a single value, therefore all resulting tuples must
5379				 * be identical (or at least indistinguishable by an equality
5380				 * check).  We can uniquify these tuples simply by just taking
5381				 * the first tuple.  All we do here is add a path to do "LIMIT
5382				 * 1" atop of 'sorted_path'.  When doing a DISTINCT ON we may
5383				 * still have a non-NIL sort_pathkeys list, so we must still
5384				 * only do this with paths which are correctly sorted by
5385				 * sort_pathkeys.
5386				 */
5387				if (root->distinct_pathkeys == NIL)
5388				{
5389					Node	   *limitCount;
5390
5391					limitCount = (Node *) makeConst(INT8OID, -1, InvalidOid,
5392													sizeof(int64),
5393													Int64GetDatum(1), false,
5394													FLOAT8PASSBYVAL);
5395
5396					/*
5397					 * If the query already has a LIMIT clause, then we could
5398					 * end up with a duplicate LimitPath in the final plan.
5399					 * That does not seem worth troubling over too much.
5400					 */
5401					add_path(distinct_rel, (Path *)
5402							 create_limit_path(root, distinct_rel, sorted_path,
5403											   NULL, limitCount,
5404											   LIMIT_OPTION_COUNT, 0, 1));
5405				}
5406				else
5407				{
5408					add_path(distinct_rel, (Path *)
5409							 create_upper_unique_path(root, distinct_rel,
5410													  sorted_path,
5411													  list_length(root->distinct_pathkeys),
5412													  numDistinctRows));
5413				}
5414			}
5415		}
5416	}
5417
5418	/*
5419	 * Consider hash-based implementations of DISTINCT, if possible.
5420	 *
5421	 * If we were not able to make any other types of path, we *must* hash or
5422	 * die trying.  If we do have other choices, there are two things that
5423	 * should prevent selection of hashing: if the query uses DISTINCT ON
5424	 * (because it won't really have the expected behavior if we hash), or if
5425	 * enable_hashagg is off.
5426	 *
5427	 * Note: grouping_is_hashable() is much more expensive to check than the
5428	 * other gating conditions, so we want to do it last.
5429	 */
5430	if (distinct_rel->pathlist == NIL)
5431		allow_hash = true;		/* we have no alternatives */
5432	else if (parse->hasDistinctOn || !enable_hashagg)
5433		allow_hash = false;		/* policy-based decision not to hash */
5434	else
5435		allow_hash = true;		/* default */
5436
5437	if (allow_hash && grouping_is_hashable(root->processed_distinctClause))
5438	{
5439		/* Generate hashed aggregate path --- no sort needed */
5440		add_path(distinct_rel, (Path *)
5441				 create_agg_path(root,
5442								 distinct_rel,
5443								 cheapest_input_path,
5444								 cheapest_input_path->pathtarget,
5445								 AGG_HASHED,
5446								 AGGSPLIT_SIMPLE,
5447								 root->processed_distinctClause,
5448								 NIL,
5449								 NULL,
5450								 numDistinctRows));
5451	}
5452
5453	return distinct_rel;
5454}
5455
5456/*
5457 * get_useful_pathkeys_for_distinct
5458 * 	  Get useful orderings of pathkeys for distinctClause by reordering
5459 * 	  'needed_pathkeys' to match the given 'path_pathkeys' as much as possible.
5460 *
5461 * This returns a list of pathkeys that can be useful for DISTINCT or DISTINCT
5462 * ON clause.  For convenience, it always includes the given 'needed_pathkeys'.
5463 */
5464static List *
5465get_useful_pathkeys_for_distinct(PlannerInfo *root, List *needed_pathkeys,
5466								 List *path_pathkeys)
5467{
5468	List	   *useful_pathkeys_list = NIL;
5469	List	   *useful_pathkeys = NIL;
5470
5471	/* always include the given 'needed_pathkeys' */
5472	useful_pathkeys_list = lappend(useful_pathkeys_list,
5473								   needed_pathkeys);
5474
5475	if (!enable_distinct_reordering)
5476		return useful_pathkeys_list;
5477
5478	/*
5479	 * Scan the given 'path_pathkeys' and construct a list of PathKey nodes
5480	 * that match 'needed_pathkeys', but only up to the longest matching
5481	 * prefix.
5482	 *
5483	 * When we have DISTINCT ON, we must ensure that the resulting pathkey
5484	 * list matches initial distinctClause pathkeys; otherwise, it won't have
5485	 * the desired behavior.
5486	 */
5487	foreach_node(PathKey, pathkey, path_pathkeys)
5488	{
5489		/*
5490		 * The PathKey nodes are canonical, so they can be checked for
5491		 * equality by simple pointer comparison.
5492		 */
5493		if (!list_member_ptr(needed_pathkeys, pathkey))
5494			break;
5495		if (root->parse->hasDistinctOn &&
5496			!list_member_ptr(root->distinct_pathkeys, pathkey))
5497			break;
5498
5499		useful_pathkeys = lappend(useful_pathkeys, pathkey);
5500	}
5501
5502	/* If no match at all, no point in reordering needed_pathkeys */
5503	if (useful_pathkeys == NIL)
5504		return useful_pathkeys_list;
5505
5506	/*
5507	 * If not full match, the resulting pathkey list is not useful without
5508	 * incremental sort.
5509	 */
5510	if (list_length(useful_pathkeys) < list_length(needed_pathkeys) &&
5511		!enable_incremental_sort)
5512		return useful_pathkeys_list;
5513
5514	/* Append the remaining PathKey nodes in needed_pathkeys */
5515	useful_pathkeys = list_concat_unique_ptr(useful_pathkeys,
5516											 needed_pathkeys);
5517
5518	/*
5519	 * If the resulting pathkey list is the same as the 'needed_pathkeys',
5520	 * just drop it.
5521	 */
5522	if (compare_pathkeys(needed_pathkeys,
5523						 useful_pathkeys) == PATHKEYS_EQUAL)
5524		return useful_pathkeys_list;
5525
5526	useful_pathkeys_list = lappend(useful_pathkeys_list,
5527								   useful_pathkeys);
5528
5529	return useful_pathkeys_list;
5530}
5531
5532/*
5533 * create_ordered_paths
5534 *
5535 * Build a new upperrel containing Paths for ORDER BY evaluation.
5536 *
5537 * All paths in the result must satisfy the ORDER BY ordering.
5538 * The only new paths we need consider are an explicit full sort
5539 * and incremental sort on the cheapest-total existing path.
5540 *
5541 * input_rel: contains the source-data Paths
5542 * target: the output tlist the result Paths must emit
5543 * limit_tuples: estimated bound on the number of output tuples,
5544 *		or -1 if no LIMIT or couldn't estimate
5545 *
5546 * XXX This only looks at sort_pathkeys. I wonder if it needs to look at the
5547 * other pathkeys (grouping, ...) like generate_useful_gather_paths.
5548 */
5549static RelOptInfo *
5550create_ordered_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
5551					 RelOptInfo *input_rel,
5552					 PathTarget *target,
5553					 bool target_parallel_safe,
5554					 double limit_tuples)
5555{
5556	Path	   *cheapest_input_path = input_rel->cheapest_total_path;
5557	RelOptInfo *ordered_rel;
5558	ListCell   *lc;
5559
5560	/* For now, do all work in the (ORDERED, NULL) upperrel */
5561	ordered_rel = fetch_upper_rel(root, UPPERREL_ORDERED, NULL);
5562
5563	/*
5564	 * If the input relation is not parallel-safe, then the ordered relation
5565	 * can't be parallel-safe, either.  Otherwise, it's parallel-safe if the
5566	 * target list is parallel-safe.
5567	 */
5568	if (input_rel->consider_parallel && target_parallel_safe)
5569		ordered_rel->consider_parallel = true;
5570
5571	/*
5572	 * If the input rel belongs to a single FDW, so does the ordered_rel.
5573	 */
5574	ordered_rel->serverid = input_rel->serverid;
5575	ordered_rel->userid = input_rel->userid;
5576	ordered_rel->useridiscurrent = input_rel->useridiscurrent;
5577	ordered_rel->fdwroutine = input_rel->fdwroutine;
5578
5579	foreach(lc, input_rel->pathlist)
5580	{
5581		Path	   *input_path = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
5582		Path	   *sorted_path;
5583		bool		is_sorted;
5584		int			presorted_keys;
5585
5586		is_sorted = pathkeys_count_contained_in(root->sort_pathkeys,
5587												input_path->pathkeys, &presorted_keys);
5588
5589		if (is_sorted)
5590			sorted_path = input_path;
5591		else
5592		{
5593			/*
5594			 * Try at least sorting the cheapest path and also try
5595			 * incrementally sorting any path which is partially sorted
5596			 * already (no need to deal with paths which have presorted keys
5597			 * when incremental sort is disabled unless it's the cheapest
5598			 * input path).
5599			 */
5600			if (input_path != cheapest_input_path &&
5601				(presorted_keys == 0 || !enable_incremental_sort))
5602				continue;
5603
5604			/*
5605			 * We've no need to consider both a sort and incremental sort.
5606			 * We'll just do a sort if there are no presorted keys and an
5607			 * incremental sort when there are presorted keys.
5608			 */
5609			if (presorted_keys == 0 || !enable_incremental_sort)
5610				sorted_path = (Path *) create_sort_path(root,
5611														ordered_rel,
5612														input_path,
5613														root->sort_pathkeys,
5614														limit_tuples);
5615			else
5616				sorted_path = (Path *) create_incremental_sort_path(root,
5617																	ordered_rel,
5618																	input_path,
5619																	root->sort_pathkeys,
5620																	presorted_keys,
5621																	limit_tuples);
5622		}
5623
5624		/*
5625		 * If the pathtarget of the result path has different expressions from
5626		 * the target to be applied, a projection step is needed.
5627		 */
5628		if (!equal(sorted_path->pathtarget->exprs, target->exprs))
5629			sorted_path = apply_projection_to_path(root, ordered_rel,
5630												   sorted_path, target);
5631
5632		add_path(ordered_rel, sorted_path);
5633	}
5634
5635	/*
5636	 * generate_gather_paths() will have already generated a simple Gather
5637	 * path for the best parallel path, if any, and the loop above will have
5638	 * considered sorting it.  Similarly, generate_gather_paths() will also
5639	 * have generated order-preserving Gather Merge plans which can be used
5640	 * without sorting if they happen to match the sort_pathkeys, and the loop
5641	 * above will have handled those as well.  However, there's one more
5642	 * possibility: it may make sense to sort the cheapest partial path or
5643	 * incrementally sort any partial path that is partially sorted according
5644	 * to the required output order and then use Gather Merge.
5645	 */
5646	if (ordered_rel->consider_parallel && root->sort_pathkeys != NIL &&
5647		input_rel->partial_pathlist != NIL)
5648	{
5649		Path	   *cheapest_partial_path;
5650
5651		cheapest_partial_path = linitial(input_rel->partial_pathlist);
5652
5653		foreach(lc, input_rel->partial_pathlist)
5654		{
5655			Path	   *input_path = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
5656			Path	   *sorted_path;
5657			bool		is_sorted;
5658			int			presorted_keys;
5659			double		total_groups;
5660
5661			is_sorted = pathkeys_count_contained_in(root->sort_pathkeys,
5662													input_path->pathkeys,
5663													&presorted_keys);
5664
5665			if (is_sorted)
5666				continue;
5667
5668			/*
5669			 * Try at least sorting the cheapest path and also try
5670			 * incrementally sorting any path which is partially sorted
5671			 * already (no need to deal with paths which have presorted keys
5672			 * when incremental sort is disabled unless it's the cheapest
5673			 * partial path).
5674			 */
5675			if (input_path != cheapest_partial_path &&
5676				(presorted_keys == 0 || !enable_incremental_sort))
5677				continue;
5678
5679			/*
5680			 * We've no need to consider both a sort and incremental sort.
5681			 * We'll just do a sort if there are no presorted keys and an
5682			 * incremental sort when there are presorted keys.
5683			 */
5684			if (presorted_keys == 0 || !enable_incremental_sort)
5685				sorted_path = (Path *) create_sort_path(root,
5686														ordered_rel,
5687														input_path,
5688														root->sort_pathkeys,
5689														limit_tuples);
5690			else
5691				sorted_path = (Path *) create_incremental_sort_path(root,
5692																	ordered_rel,
5693																	input_path,
5694																	root->sort_pathkeys,
5695																	presorted_keys,
5696																	limit_tuples);
5697			total_groups = compute_gather_rows(sorted_path);
5698			sorted_path = (Path *)
5699				create_gather_merge_path(root, ordered_rel,
5700										 sorted_path,
5701										 sorted_path->pathtarget,
5702										 root->sort_pathkeys, NULL,
5703										 &total_groups);
5704
5705			/*
5706			 * If the pathtarget of the result path has different expressions
5707			 * from the target to be applied, a projection step is needed.
5708			 */
5709			if (!equal(sorted_path->pathtarget->exprs, target->exprs))
5710				sorted_path = apply_projection_to_path(root, ordered_rel,
5711													   sorted_path, target);
5712
5713			add_path(ordered_rel, sorted_path);
5714		}
5715	}
5716
5717	/*
5718	 * If there is an FDW that's responsible for all baserels of the query,
5719	 * let it consider adding ForeignPaths.
5720	 */
5721	if (ordered_rel->fdwroutine &&
5722		ordered_rel->fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths)
5723		ordered_rel->fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths(root, UPPERREL_ORDERED,
5724													  input_rel, ordered_rel,
5725													  NULL);
5726
5727	/* Let extensions possibly add some more paths */
5728	if (create_upper_paths_hook)
5729		(*create_upper_paths_hook) (root, UPPERREL_ORDERED,
5730									input_rel, ordered_rel, NULL);
5731
5732	/*
5733	 * No need to bother with set_cheapest here; grouping_planner does not
5734	 * need us to do it.
5735	 */
5736	Assert(ordered_rel->pathlist != NIL);
5737
5738	return ordered_rel;
5739}
5740
5741
5742/*
5743 * make_group_input_target
5744 *	  Generate appropriate PathTarget for initial input to grouping nodes.
5745 *
5746 * If there is grouping or aggregation, the scan/join subplan cannot emit
5747 * the query's final targetlist; for example, it certainly can't emit any
5748 * aggregate function calls.  This routine generates the correct target
5749 * for the scan/join subplan.
5750 *
5751 * The query target list passed from the parser already contains entries
5752 * for all ORDER BY and GROUP BY expressions, but it will not have entries
5753 * for variables used only in HAVING clauses; so we need to add those
5754 * variables to the subplan target list.  Also, we flatten all expressions
5755 * except GROUP BY items into their component variables; other expressions
5756 * will be computed by the upper plan nodes rather than by the subplan.
5757 * For example, given a query like
5758 *		SELECT a+b,SUM(c+d) FROM table GROUP BY a+b;
5759 * we want to pass this targetlist to the subplan:
5760 *		a+b,c,d
5761 * where the a+b target will be used by the Sort/Group steps, and the
5762 * other targets will be used for computing the final results.
5763 *
5764 * 'final_target' is the query's final target list (in PathTarget form)
5765 *
5766 * The result is the PathTarget to be computed by the Paths returned from
5767 * query_planner().
5768 */
5769static PathTarget *
5770make_group_input_target(PlannerInfo *root, PathTarget *final_target)
5771{
5772	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
5773	PathTarget *input_target;
5774	List	   *non_group_cols;
5775	List	   *non_group_vars;
5776	int			i;
5777	ListCell   *lc;
5778
5779	/*
5780	 * We must build a target containing all grouping columns, plus any other
5781	 * Vars mentioned in the query's targetlist and HAVING qual.
5782	 */
5783	input_target = create_empty_pathtarget();
5784	non_group_cols = NIL;
5785
5786	i = 0;
5787	foreach(lc, final_target->exprs)
5788	{
5789		Expr	   *expr = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
5790		Index		sgref = get_pathtarget_sortgroupref(final_target, i);
5791
5792		if (sgref && root->processed_groupClause &&
5793			get_sortgroupref_clause_noerr(sgref,
5794										  root->processed_groupClause) != NULL)
5795		{
5796			/*
5797			 * It's a grouping column, so add it to the input target as-is.
5798			 *
5799			 * Note that the target is logically below the grouping step.  So
5800			 * with grouping sets we need to remove the RT index of the
5801			 * grouping step if there is any from the target expression.
5802			 */
5803			if (parse->hasGroupRTE && parse->groupingSets != NIL)
5804			{
5805				Assert(root->group_rtindex > 0);
5806				expr = (Expr *)
5807					remove_nulling_relids((Node *) expr,
5808										  bms_make_singleton(root->group_rtindex),
5809										  NULL);
5810			}
5811			add_column_to_pathtarget(input_target, expr, sgref);
5812		}
5813		else
5814		{
5815			/*
5816			 * Non-grouping column, so just remember the expression for later
5817			 * call to pull_var_clause.
5818			 */
5819			non_group_cols = lappend(non_group_cols, expr);
5820		}
5821
5822		i++;
5823	}
5824
5825	/*
5826	 * If there's a HAVING clause, we'll need the Vars it uses, too.
5827	 */
5828	if (parse->havingQual)
5829		non_group_cols = lappend(non_group_cols, parse->havingQual);
5830
5831	/*
5832	 * Pull out all the Vars mentioned in non-group cols (plus HAVING), and
5833	 * add them to the input target if not already present.  (A Var used
5834	 * directly as a GROUP BY item will be present already.)  Note this
5835	 * includes Vars used in resjunk items, so we are covering the needs of
5836	 * ORDER BY and window specifications.  Vars used within Aggrefs and
5837	 * WindowFuncs will be pulled out here, too.
5838	 *
5839	 * Note that the target is logically below the grouping step.  So with
5840	 * grouping sets we need to remove the RT index of the grouping step if
5841	 * there is any from the non-group Vars.
5842	 */
5843	non_group_vars = pull_var_clause((Node *) non_group_cols,
5844									 PVC_RECURSE_AGGREGATES |
5845									 PVC_RECURSE_WINDOWFUNCS |
5846									 PVC_INCLUDE_PLACEHOLDERS);
5847	if (parse->hasGroupRTE && parse->groupingSets != NIL)
5848	{
5849		Assert(root->group_rtindex > 0);
5850		non_group_vars = (List *)
5851			remove_nulling_relids((Node *) non_group_vars,
5852								  bms_make_singleton(root->group_rtindex),
5853								  NULL);
5854	}
5855	add_new_columns_to_pathtarget(input_target, non_group_vars);
5856
5857	/* clean up cruft */
5858	list_free(non_group_vars);
5859	list_free(non_group_cols);
5860
5861	/* XXX this causes some redundant cost calculation ... */
5862	return set_pathtarget_cost_width(root, input_target);
5863}
5864
5865/*
5866 * make_partial_grouping_target
5867 *	  Generate appropriate PathTarget for output of partial aggregate
5868 *	  (or partial grouping, if there are no aggregates) nodes.
5869 *
5870 * A partial aggregation node needs to emit all the same aggregates that
5871 * a regular aggregation node would, plus any aggregates used in HAVING;
5872 * except that the Aggref nodes should be marked as partial aggregates.
5873 *
5874 * In addition, we'd better emit any Vars and PlaceHolderVars that are
5875 * used outside of Aggrefs in the aggregation tlist and HAVING.  (Presumably,
5876 * these would be Vars that are grouped by or used in grouping expressions.)
5877 *
5878 * grouping_target is the tlist to be emitted by the topmost aggregation step.
5879 * havingQual represents the HAVING clause.
5880 */
5881static PathTarget *
5882make_partial_grouping_target(PlannerInfo *root,
5883							 PathTarget *grouping_target,
5884							 Node *havingQual)
5885{
5886	PathTarget *partial_target;
5887	List	   *non_group_cols;
5888	List	   *non_group_exprs;
5889	int			i;
5890	ListCell   *lc;
5891
5892	partial_target = create_empty_pathtarget();
5893	non_group_cols = NIL;
5894
5895	i = 0;
5896	foreach(lc, grouping_target->exprs)
5897	{
5898		Expr	   *expr = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
5899		Index		sgref = get_pathtarget_sortgroupref(grouping_target, i);
5900
5901		if (sgref && root->processed_groupClause &&
5902			get_sortgroupref_clause_noerr(sgref,
5903										  root->processed_groupClause) != NULL)
5904		{
5905			/*
5906			 * It's a grouping column, so add it to the partial_target as-is.
5907			 * (This allows the upper agg step to repeat the grouping calcs.)
5908			 */
5909			add_column_to_pathtarget(partial_target, expr, sgref);
5910		}
5911		else
5912		{
5913			/*
5914			 * Non-grouping column, so just remember the expression for later
5915			 * call to pull_var_clause.
5916			 */
5917			non_group_cols = lappend(non_group_cols, expr);
5918		}
5919
5920		i++;
5921	}
5922
5923	/*
5924	 * If there's a HAVING clause, we'll need the Vars/Aggrefs it uses, too.
5925	 */
5926	if (havingQual)
5927		non_group_cols = lappend(non_group_cols, havingQual);
5928
5929	/*
5930	 * Pull out all the Vars, PlaceHolderVars, and Aggrefs mentioned in
5931	 * non-group cols (plus HAVING), and add them to the partial_target if not
5932	 * already present.  (An expression used directly as a GROUP BY item will
5933	 * be present already.)  Note this includes Vars used in resjunk items, so
5934	 * we are covering the needs of ORDER BY and window specifications.
5935	 */
5936	non_group_exprs = pull_var_clause((Node *) non_group_cols,
5937									  PVC_INCLUDE_AGGREGATES |
5938									  PVC_RECURSE_WINDOWFUNCS |
5939									  PVC_INCLUDE_PLACEHOLDERS);
5940
5941	add_new_columns_to_pathtarget(partial_target, non_group_exprs);
5942
5943	/*
5944	 * Adjust Aggrefs to put them in partial mode.  At this point all Aggrefs
5945	 * are at the top level of the target list, so we can just scan the list
5946	 * rather than recursing through the expression trees.
5947	 */
5948	foreach(lc, partial_target->exprs)
5949	{
5950		Aggref	   *aggref = (Aggref *) lfirst(lc);
5951
5952		if (IsA(aggref, Aggref))
5953		{
5954			Aggref	   *newaggref;
5955
5956			/*
5957			 * We shouldn't need to copy the substructure of the Aggref node,
5958			 * but flat-copy the node itself to avoid damaging other trees.
5959			 */
5960			newaggref = makeNode(Aggref);
5961			memcpy(newaggref, aggref, sizeof(Aggref));
5962
5963			/* For now, assume serialization is required */
5964			mark_partial_aggref(newaggref, AGGSPLIT_INITIAL_SERIAL);
5965
5966			lfirst(lc) = newaggref;
5967		}
5968	}
5969
5970	/* clean up cruft */
5971	list_free(non_group_exprs);
5972	list_free(non_group_cols);
5973
5974	/* XXX this causes some redundant cost calculation ... */
5975	return set_pathtarget_cost_width(root, partial_target);
5976}
5977
5978/*
5979 * mark_partial_aggref
5980 *	  Adjust an Aggref to make it represent a partial-aggregation step.
5981 *
5982 * The Aggref node is modified in-place; caller must do any copying required.
5983 */
5984void
5985mark_partial_aggref(Aggref *agg, AggSplit aggsplit)
5986{
5987	/* aggtranstype should be computed by this point */
5988	Assert(OidIsValid(agg->aggtranstype));
5989	/* ... but aggsplit should still be as the parser left it */
5990	Assert(agg->aggsplit == AGGSPLIT_SIMPLE);
5991
5992	/* Mark the Aggref with the intended partial-aggregation mode */
5993	agg->aggsplit = aggsplit;
5994
5995	/*
5996	 * Adjust result type if needed.  Normally, a partial aggregate returns
5997	 * the aggregate's transition type; but if that's INTERNAL and we're
5998	 * serializing, it returns BYTEA instead.
5999	 */
6000	if (DO_AGGSPLIT_SKIPFINAL(aggsplit))
6001	{
6002		if (agg->aggtranstype == INTERNALOID && DO_AGGSPLIT_SERIALIZE(aggsplit))
6003			agg->aggtype = BYTEAOID;
6004		else
6005			agg->aggtype = agg->aggtranstype;
6006	}
6007}
6008
6009/*
6010 * postprocess_setop_tlist
6011 *	  Fix up targetlist returned by plan_set_operations().
6012 *
6013 * We need to transpose sort key info from the orig_tlist into new_tlist.
6014 * NOTE: this would not be good enough if we supported resjunk sort keys
6015 * for results of set operations --- then, we'd need to project a whole
6016 * new tlist to evaluate the resjunk columns.  For now, just ereport if we
6017 * find any resjunk columns in orig_tlist.
6018 */
6019static List *
6020postprocess_setop_tlist(List *new_tlist, List *orig_tlist)
6021{
6022	ListCell   *l;
6023	ListCell   *orig_tlist_item = list_head(orig_tlist);
6024
6025	foreach(l, new_tlist)
6026	{
6027		TargetEntry *new_tle = lfirst_node(TargetEntry, l);
6028		TargetEntry *orig_tle;
6029
6030		/* ignore resjunk columns in setop result */
6031		if (new_tle->resjunk)
6032			continue;
6033
6034		Assert(orig_tlist_item != NULL);
6035		orig_tle = lfirst_node(TargetEntry, orig_tlist_item);
6036		orig_tlist_item = lnext(orig_tlist, orig_tlist_item);
6037		if (orig_tle->resjunk)	/* should not happen */
6038			elog(ERROR, "resjunk output columns are not implemented");
6039		Assert(new_tle->resno == orig_tle->resno);
6040		new_tle->ressortgroupref = orig_tle->ressortgroupref;
6041	}
6042	if (orig_tlist_item != NULL)
6043		elog(ERROR, "resjunk output columns are not implemented");
6044	return new_tlist;
6045}
6046
6047/*
6048 * optimize_window_clauses
6049 *		Call each WindowFunc's prosupport function to see if we're able to
6050 *		make any adjustments to any of the WindowClause's so that the executor
6051 *		can execute the window functions in a more optimal way.
6052 *
6053 * Currently we only allow adjustments to the WindowClause's frameOptions.  We
6054 * may allow more things to be done here in the future.
6055 */
6056static void
6057optimize_window_clauses(PlannerInfo *root, WindowFuncLists *wflists)
6058{
6059	List	   *windowClause = root->parse->windowClause;
6060	ListCell   *lc;
6061
6062	foreach(lc, windowClause)
6063	{
6064		WindowClause *wc = lfirst_node(WindowClause, lc);
6065		ListCell   *lc2;
6066		int			optimizedFrameOptions = 0;
6067
6068		Assert(wc->winref <= wflists->maxWinRef);
6069
6070		/* skip any WindowClauses that have no WindowFuncs */
6071		if (wflists->windowFuncs[wc->winref] == NIL)
6072			continue;
6073
6074		foreach(lc2, wflists->windowFuncs[wc->winref])
6075		{
6076			SupportRequestOptimizeWindowClause req;
6077			SupportRequestOptimizeWindowClause *res;
6078			WindowFunc *wfunc = lfirst_node(WindowFunc, lc2);
6079			Oid			prosupport;
6080
6081			prosupport = get_func_support(wfunc->winfnoid);
6082
6083			/* Check if there's a support function for 'wfunc' */
6084			if (!OidIsValid(prosupport))
6085				break;			/* can't optimize this WindowClause */
6086
6087			req.type = T_SupportRequestOptimizeWindowClause;
6088			req.window_clause = wc;
6089			req.window_func = wfunc;
6090			req.frameOptions = wc->frameOptions;
6091
6092			/* call the support function */
6093			res = (SupportRequestOptimizeWindowClause *)
6094				DatumGetPointer(OidFunctionCall1(prosupport,
6095												 PointerGetDatum(&req)));
6096
6097			/*
6098			 * Skip to next WindowClause if the support function does not
6099			 * support this request type.
6100			 */
6101			if (res == NULL)
6102				break;
6103
6104			/*
6105			 * Save these frameOptions for the first WindowFunc for this
6106			 * WindowClause.
6107			 */
6108			if (foreach_current_index(lc2) == 0)
6109				optimizedFrameOptions = res->frameOptions;
6110
6111			/*
6112			 * On subsequent WindowFuncs, if the frameOptions are not the same
6113			 * then we're unable to optimize the frameOptions for this
6114			 * WindowClause.
6115			 */
6116			else if (optimizedFrameOptions != res->frameOptions)
6117				break;			/* skip to the next WindowClause, if any */
6118		}
6119
6120		/* adjust the frameOptions if all WindowFunc's agree that it's ok */
6121		if (lc2 == NULL && wc->frameOptions != optimizedFrameOptions)
6122		{
6123			ListCell   *lc3;
6124
6125			/* apply the new frame options */
6126			wc->frameOptions = optimizedFrameOptions;
6127
6128			/*
6129			 * We now check to see if changing the frameOptions has caused
6130			 * this WindowClause to be a duplicate of some other WindowClause.
6131			 * This can only happen if we have multiple WindowClauses, so
6132			 * don't bother if there's only 1.
6133			 */
6134			if (list_length(windowClause) == 1)
6135				continue;
6136
6137			/*
6138			 * Do the duplicate check and reuse the existing WindowClause if
6139			 * we find a duplicate.
6140			 */
6141			foreach(lc3, windowClause)
6142			{
6143				WindowClause *existing_wc = lfirst_node(WindowClause, lc3);
6144
6145				/* skip over the WindowClause we're currently editing */
6146				if (existing_wc == wc)
6147					continue;
6148
6149				/*
6150				 * Perform the same duplicate check that is done in
6151				 * transformWindowFuncCall.
6152				 */
6153				if (equal(wc->partitionClause, existing_wc->partitionClause) &&
6154					equal(wc->orderClause, existing_wc->orderClause) &&
6155					wc->frameOptions == existing_wc->frameOptions &&
6156					equal(wc->startOffset, existing_wc->startOffset) &&
6157					equal(wc->endOffset, existing_wc->endOffset))
6158				{
6159					ListCell   *lc4;
6160
6161					/*
6162					 * Now move each WindowFunc in 'wc' into 'existing_wc'.
6163					 * This required adjusting each WindowFunc's winref and
6164					 * moving the WindowFuncs in 'wc' to the list of
6165					 * WindowFuncs in 'existing_wc'.
6166					 */
6167					foreach(lc4, wflists->windowFuncs[wc->winref])
6168					{
6169						WindowFunc *wfunc = lfirst_node(WindowFunc, lc4);
6170
6171						wfunc->winref = existing_wc->winref;
6172					}
6173
6174					/* move list items */
6175					wflists->windowFuncs[existing_wc->winref] = list_concat(wflists->windowFuncs[existing_wc->winref],
6176																			wflists->windowFuncs[wc->winref]);
6177					wflists->windowFuncs[wc->winref] = NIL;
6178
6179					/*
6180					 * transformWindowFuncCall() should have made sure there
6181					 * are no other duplicates, so we needn't bother looking
6182					 * any further.
6183					 */
6184					break;
6185				}
6186			}
6187		}
6188	}
6189
6190	/*
6191	 * XXX remove any duplicate WindowFuncs from each WindowClause.  This has
6192	 * been done only in the back branches.  Previously, the deduplication was
6193	 * done in find_window_functions(), but that caused issues with the code
6194	 * above when moving a WindowFunc to another WindowClause as any duplicate
6195	 * WindowFuncs won't receive the adjusted winref when merging
6196	 * WindowClauses.  The deduplication below has been done only so that we
6197	 * maintain the same cost calculations.  As it turns out, the previous
6198	 * deduplication code thought it was saving effort during execution by
6199	 * getting rid of duplicates, but that was not true as the expression
6200	 * evaluation code will evaluate each WindowFunc mentioned in the
6201	 * targetlist.
6202	 */
6203	foreach(lc, windowClause)
6204	{
6205		WindowClause *wc = lfirst_node(WindowClause, lc);
6206		ListCell   *lc2;
6207		List	   *list = wflists->windowFuncs[wc->winref];
6208		List	   *newlist = NIL;
6209
6210		if (list == NIL)
6211			continue;
6212
6213		foreach(lc2, list)
6214		{
6215			if (!list_member(newlist, lfirst(lc2)))
6216				newlist = lappend(newlist, lfirst(lc2));
6217			else
6218				wflists->numWindowFuncs--;
6219		}
6220		list_free(list);
6221
6222		wflists->windowFuncs[wc->winref] = newlist;
6223	}
6224}
6225
6226/*
6227 * select_active_windows
6228 *		Create a list of the "active" window clauses (ie, those referenced
6229 *		by non-deleted WindowFuncs) in the order they are to be executed.
6230 */
6231static List *
6232select_active_windows(PlannerInfo *root, WindowFuncLists *wflists)
6233{
6234	List	   *windowClause = root->parse->windowClause;
6235	List	   *result = NIL;
6236	ListCell   *lc;
6237	int			nActive = 0;
6238	WindowClauseSortData *actives = palloc(sizeof(WindowClauseSortData)
6239										   * list_length(windowClause));
6240
6241	/* First, construct an array of the active windows */
6242	foreach(lc, windowClause)
6243	{
6244		WindowClause *wc = lfirst_node(WindowClause, lc);
6245
6246		/* It's only active if wflists shows some related WindowFuncs */
6247		Assert(wc->winref <= wflists->maxWinRef);
6248		if (wflists->windowFuncs[wc->winref] == NIL)
6249			continue;
6250
6251		actives[nActive].wc = wc;	/* original clause */
6252
6253		/*
6254		 * For sorting, we want the list of partition keys followed by the
6255		 * list of sort keys. But pathkeys construction will remove duplicates
6256		 * between the two, so we can as well (even though we can't detect all
6257		 * of the duplicates, since some may come from ECs - that might mean
6258		 * we miss optimization chances here). We must, however, ensure that
6259		 * the order of entries is preserved with respect to the ones we do
6260		 * keep.
6261		 *
6262		 * partitionClause and orderClause had their own duplicates removed in
6263		 * parse analysis, so we're only concerned here with removing
6264		 * orderClause entries that also appear in partitionClause.
6265		 */
6266		actives[nActive].uniqueOrder =
6267			list_concat_unique(list_copy(wc->partitionClause),
6268							   wc->orderClause);
6269		nActive++;
6270	}
6271
6272	/*
6273	 * Sort active windows by their partitioning/ordering clauses, ignoring
6274	 * any framing clauses, so that the windows that need the same sorting are
6275	 * adjacent in the list. When we come to generate paths, this will avoid
6276	 * inserting additional Sort nodes.
6277	 *
6278	 * This is how we implement a specific requirement from the SQL standard,
6279	 * which says that when two or more windows are order-equivalent (i.e.
6280	 * have matching partition and order clauses, even if their names or
6281	 * framing clauses differ), then all peer rows must be presented in the
6282	 * same order in all of them. If we allowed multiple sort nodes for such
6283	 * cases, we'd risk having the peer rows end up in different orders in
6284	 * equivalent windows due to sort instability. (See General Rule 4 of
6285	 * <window clause> in SQL2008 - SQL2016.)
6286	 *
6287	 * Additionally, if the entire list of clauses of one window is a prefix
6288	 * of another, put first the window with stronger sorting requirements.
6289	 * This way we will first sort for stronger window, and won't have to sort
6290	 * again for the weaker one.
6291	 */
6292	qsort(actives, nActive, sizeof(WindowClauseSortData), common_prefix_cmp);
6293
6294	/* build ordered list of the original WindowClause nodes */
6295	for (int i = 0; i < nActive; i++)
6296		result = lappend(result, actives[i].wc);
6297
6298	pfree(actives);
6299
6300	return result;
6301}
6302
6303/*
6304 * name_active_windows
6305 *	  Ensure all active windows have unique names.
6306 *
6307 * The parser will have checked that user-assigned window names are unique
6308 * within the Query.  Here we assign made-up names to any unnamed
6309 * WindowClauses for the benefit of EXPLAIN.  (We don't want to do this
6310 * at parse time, because it'd mess up decompilation of views.)
6311 *
6312 * activeWindows: result of select_active_windows
6313 */
6314static void
6315name_active_windows(List *activeWindows)
6316{
6317	int			next_n = 1;
6318	char		newname[16];
6319	ListCell   *lc;
6320
6321	foreach(lc, activeWindows)
6322	{
6323		WindowClause *wc = lfirst_node(WindowClause, lc);
6324
6325		/* Nothing to do if it has a name already. */
6326		if (wc->name)
6327			continue;
6328
6329		/* Select a name not currently present in the list. */
6330		for (;;)
6331		{
6332			ListCell   *lc2;
6333
6334			snprintf(newname, sizeof(newname), "w%d", next_n++);
6335			foreach(lc2, activeWindows)
6336			{
6337				WindowClause *wc2 = lfirst_node(WindowClause, lc2);
6338
6339				if (wc2->name && strcmp(wc2->name, newname) == 0)
6340					break;		/* matched */
6341			}
6342			if (lc2 == NULL)
6343				break;			/* reached the end with no match */
6344		}
6345		wc->name = pstrdup(newname);
6346	}
6347}
6348
6349/*
6350 * common_prefix_cmp
6351 *	  QSort comparison function for WindowClauseSortData
6352 *
6353 * Sort the windows by the required sorting clauses. First, compare the sort
6354 * clauses themselves. Second, if one window's clauses are a prefix of another
6355 * one's clauses, put the window with more sort clauses first.
6356 *
6357 * We purposefully sort by the highest tleSortGroupRef first.  Since
6358 * tleSortGroupRefs are assigned for the query's DISTINCT and ORDER BY first
6359 * and because here we sort the lowest tleSortGroupRefs last, if a
6360 * WindowClause is sharing a tleSortGroupRef with the query's DISTINCT or
6361 * ORDER BY clause, this makes it more likely that the final WindowAgg will
6362 * provide presorted input for the query's DISTINCT or ORDER BY clause, thus
6363 * reducing the total number of sorts required for the query.
6364 */
6365static int
6366common_prefix_cmp(const void *a, const void *b)
6367{
6368	const WindowClauseSortData *wcsa = a;
6369	const WindowClauseSortData *wcsb = b;
6370	ListCell   *item_a;
6371	ListCell   *item_b;
6372
6373	forboth(item_a, wcsa->uniqueOrder, item_b, wcsb->uniqueOrder)
6374	{
6375		SortGroupClause *sca = lfirst_node(SortGroupClause, item_a);
6376		SortGroupClause *scb = lfirst_node(SortGroupClause, item_b);
6377
6378		if (sca->tleSortGroupRef > scb->tleSortGroupRef)
6379			return -1;
6380		else if (sca->tleSortGroupRef < scb->tleSortGroupRef)
6381			return 1;
6382		else if (sca->sortop > scb->sortop)
6383			return -1;
6384		else if (sca->sortop < scb->sortop)
6385			return 1;
6386		else if (sca->nulls_first && !scb->nulls_first)
6387			return -1;
6388		else if (!sca->nulls_first && scb->nulls_first)
6389			return 1;
6390		/* no need to compare eqop, since it is fully determined by sortop */
6391	}
6392
6393	if (list_length(wcsa->uniqueOrder) > list_length(wcsb->uniqueOrder))
6394		return -1;
6395	else if (list_length(wcsa->uniqueOrder) < list_length(wcsb->uniqueOrder))
6396		return 1;
6397
6398	return 0;
6399}
6400
6401/*
6402 * make_window_input_target
6403 *	  Generate appropriate PathTarget for initial input to WindowAgg nodes.
6404 *
6405 * When the query has window functions, this function computes the desired
6406 * target to be computed by the node just below the first WindowAgg.
6407 * This tlist must contain all values needed to evaluate the window functions,
6408 * compute the final target list, and perform any required final sort step.
6409 * If multiple WindowAggs are needed, each intermediate one adds its window
6410 * function results onto this base tlist; only the topmost WindowAgg computes
6411 * the actual desired target list.
6412 *
6413 * This function is much like make_group_input_target, though not quite enough
6414 * like it to share code.  As in that function, we flatten most expressions
6415 * into their component variables.  But we do not want to flatten window
6416 * PARTITION BY/ORDER BY clauses, since that might result in multiple
6417 * evaluations of them, which would be bad (possibly even resulting in
6418 * inconsistent answers, if they contain volatile functions).
6419 * Also, we must not flatten GROUP BY clauses that were left unflattened by
6420 * make_group_input_target, because we may no longer have access to the
6421 * individual Vars in them.
6422 *
6423 * Another key difference from make_group_input_target is that we don't
6424 * flatten Aggref expressions, since those are to be computed below the
6425 * window functions and just referenced like Vars above that.
6426 *
6427 * 'final_target' is the query's final target list (in PathTarget form)
6428 * 'activeWindows' is the list of active windows previously identified by
6429 *			select_active_windows.
6430 *
6431 * The result is the PathTarget to be computed by the plan node immediately
6432 * below the first WindowAgg node.
6433 */
6434static PathTarget *
6435make_window_input_target(PlannerInfo *root,
6436						 PathTarget *final_target,
6437						 List *activeWindows)
6438{
6439	PathTarget *input_target;
6440	Bitmapset  *sgrefs;
6441	List	   *flattenable_cols;
6442	List	   *flattenable_vars;
6443	int			i;
6444	ListCell   *lc;
6445
6446	Assert(root->parse->hasWindowFuncs);
6447
6448	/*
6449	 * Collect the sortgroupref numbers of window PARTITION/ORDER BY clauses
6450	 * into a bitmapset for convenient reference below.
6451	 */
6452	sgrefs = NULL;
6453	foreach(lc, activeWindows)
6454	{
6455		WindowClause *wc = lfirst_node(WindowClause, lc);
6456		ListCell   *lc2;
6457
6458		foreach(lc2, wc->partitionClause)
6459		{
6460			SortGroupClause *sortcl = lfirst_node(SortGroupClause, lc2);
6461
6462			sgrefs = bms_add_member(sgrefs, sortcl->tleSortGroupRef);
6463		}
6464		foreach(lc2, wc->orderClause)
6465		{
6466			SortGroupClause *sortcl = lfirst_node(SortGroupClause, lc2);
6467
6468			sgrefs = bms_add_member(sgrefs, sortcl->tleSortGroupRef);
6469		}
6470	}
6471
6472	/* Add in sortgroupref numbers of GROUP BY clauses, too */
6473	foreach(lc, root->processed_groupClause)
6474	{
6475		SortGroupClause *grpcl = lfirst_node(SortGroupClause, lc);
6476
6477		sgrefs = bms_add_member(sgrefs, grpcl->tleSortGroupRef);
6478	}
6479
6480	/*
6481	 * Construct a target containing all the non-flattenable targetlist items,
6482	 * and save aside the others for a moment.
6483	 */
6484	input_target = create_empty_pathtarget();
6485	flattenable_cols = NIL;
6486
6487	i = 0;
6488	foreach(lc, final_target->exprs)
6489	{
6490		Expr	   *expr = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
6491		Index		sgref = get_pathtarget_sortgroupref(final_target, i);
6492
6493		/*
6494		 * Don't want to deconstruct window clauses or GROUP BY items.  (Note
6495		 * that such items can't contain window functions, so it's okay to
6496		 * compute them below the WindowAgg nodes.)
6497		 */
6498		if (sgref != 0 && bms_is_member(sgref, sgrefs))
6499		{
6500			/*
6501			 * Don't want to deconstruct this value, so add it to the input
6502			 * target as-is.
6503			 */
6504			add_column_to_pathtarget(input_target, expr, sgref);
6505		}
6506		else
6507		{
6508			/*
6509			 * Column is to be flattened, so just remember the expression for
6510			 * later call to pull_var_clause.
6511			 */
6512			flattenable_cols = lappend(flattenable_cols, expr);
6513		}
6514
6515		i++;
6516	}
6517
6518	/*
6519	 * Pull out all the Vars and Aggrefs mentioned in flattenable columns, and
6520	 * add them to the input target if not already present.  (Some might be
6521	 * there already because they're used directly as window/group clauses.)
6522	 *
6523	 * Note: it's essential to use PVC_INCLUDE_AGGREGATES here, so that any
6524	 * Aggrefs are placed in the Agg node's tlist and not left to be computed
6525	 * at higher levels.  On the other hand, we should recurse into
6526	 * WindowFuncs to make sure their input expressions are available.
6527	 */
6528	flattenable_vars = pull_var_clause((Node *) flattenable_cols,
6529									   PVC_INCLUDE_AGGREGATES |
6530									   PVC_RECURSE_WINDOWFUNCS |
6531									   PVC_INCLUDE_PLACEHOLDERS);
6532	add_new_columns_to_pathtarget(input_target, flattenable_vars);
6533
6534	/* clean up cruft */
6535	list_free(flattenable_vars);
6536	list_free(flattenable_cols);
6537
6538	/* XXX this causes some redundant cost calculation ... */
6539	return set_pathtarget_cost_width(root, input_target);
6540}
6541
6542/*
6543 * make_pathkeys_for_window
6544 *		Create a pathkeys list describing the required input ordering
6545 *		for the given WindowClause.
6546 *
6547 * Modifies wc's partitionClause to remove any clauses which are deemed
6548 * redundant by the pathkey logic.
6549 *
6550 * The required ordering is first the PARTITION keys, then the ORDER keys.
6551 * In the future we might try to implement windowing using hashing, in which
6552 * case the ordering could be relaxed, but for now we always sort.
6553 */
6554static List *
6555make_pathkeys_for_window(PlannerInfo *root, WindowClause *wc,
6556						 List *tlist)
6557{
6558	List	   *window_pathkeys = NIL;
6559
6560	/* Throw error if can't sort */
6561	if (!grouping_is_sortable(wc->partitionClause))
6562		ereport(ERROR,
6563				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
6564				 errmsg("could not implement window PARTITION BY"),
6565				 errdetail("Window partitioning columns must be of sortable datatypes.")));
6566	if (!grouping_is_sortable(wc->orderClause))
6567		ereport(ERROR,
6568				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
6569				 errmsg("could not implement window ORDER BY"),
6570				 errdetail("Window ordering columns must be of sortable datatypes.")));
6571
6572	/*
6573	 * First fetch the pathkeys for the PARTITION BY clause.  We can safely
6574	 * remove any clauses from the wc->partitionClause for redundant pathkeys.
6575	 */
6576	if (wc->partitionClause != NIL)
6577	{
6578		bool		sortable;
6579
6580		window_pathkeys = make_pathkeys_for_sortclauses_extended(root,
6581																 &wc->partitionClause,
6582																 tlist,
6583																 true,
6584																 false,
6585																 &sortable,
6586																 false);
6587
6588		Assert(sortable);
6589	}
6590
6591	/*
6592	 * In principle, we could also consider removing redundant ORDER BY items
6593	 * too as doing so does not alter the result of peer row checks done by
6594	 * the executor.  However, we must *not* remove the ordering column for
6595	 * RANGE OFFSET cases, as the executor needs that for in_range tests even
6596	 * if it's known to be equal to some partitioning column.
6597	 */
6598	if (wc->orderClause != NIL)
6599	{
6600		List	   *orderby_pathkeys;
6601
6602		orderby_pathkeys = make_pathkeys_for_sortclauses(root,
6603														 wc->orderClause,
6604														 tlist);
6605
6606		/* Okay, make the combined pathkeys */
6607		if (window_pathkeys != NIL)
6608			window_pathkeys = append_pathkeys(window_pathkeys, orderby_pathkeys);
6609		else
6610			window_pathkeys = orderby_pathkeys;
6611	}
6612
6613	return window_pathkeys;
6614}
6615
6616/*
6617 * make_sort_input_target
6618 *	  Generate appropriate PathTarget for initial input to Sort step.
6619 *
6620 * If the query has ORDER BY, this function chooses the target to be computed
6621 * by the node just below the Sort (and DISTINCT, if any, since Unique can't
6622 * project) steps.  This might or might not be identical to the query's final
6623 * output target.
6624 *
6625 * The main argument for keeping the sort-input tlist the same as the final
6626 * is that we avoid a separate projection node (which will be needed if
6627 * they're different, because Sort can't project).  However, there are also
6628 * advantages to postponing tlist evaluation till after the Sort: it ensures
6629 * a consistent order of evaluation for any volatile functions in the tlist,
6630 * and if there's also a LIMIT, we can stop the query without ever computing
6631 * tlist functions for later rows, which is beneficial for both volatile and
6632 * expensive functions.
6633 *
6634 * Our current policy is to postpone volatile expressions till after the sort
6635 * unconditionally (assuming that that's possible, ie they are in plain tlist
6636 * columns and not ORDER BY/GROUP BY/DISTINCT columns).  We also prefer to
6637 * postpone set-returning expressions, because running them beforehand would
6638 * bloat the sort dataset, and because it might cause unexpected output order
6639 * if the sort isn't stable.  However there's a constraint on that: all SRFs
6640 * in the tlist should be evaluated at the same plan step, so that they can
6641 * run in sync in nodeProjectSet.  So if any SRFs are in sort columns, we
6642 * mustn't postpone any SRFs.  (Note that in principle that policy should
6643 * probably get applied to the group/window input targetlists too, but we
6644 * have not done that historically.)  Lastly, expensive expressions are
6645 * postponed if there is a LIMIT, or if root->tuple_fraction shows that
6646 * partial evaluation of the query is possible (if neither is true, we expect
6647 * to have to evaluate the expressions for every row anyway), or if there are
6648 * any volatile or set-returning expressions (since once we've put in a
6649 * projection at all, it won't cost any more to postpone more stuff).
6650 *
6651 * Another issue that could potentially be considered here is that
6652 * evaluating tlist expressions could result in data that's either wider
6653 * or narrower than the input Vars, thus changing the volume of data that
6654 * has to go through the Sort.  However, we usually have only a very bad
6655 * idea of the output width of any expression more complex than a Var,
6656 * so for now it seems too risky to try to optimize on that basis.
6657 *
6658 * Note that if we do produce a modified sort-input target, and then the
6659 * query ends up not using an explicit Sort, no particular harm is done:
