6660 * we'll initially use the modified target for the preceding path nodes,
6661 * but then change them to the final target with apply_projection_to_path.
6662 * Moreover, in such a case the guarantees about evaluation order of
6663 * volatile functions still hold, since the rows are sorted already.
6664 *
6665 * This function has some things in common with make_group_input_target and
6666 * make_window_input_target, though the detailed rules for what to do are
6667 * different.  We never flatten/postpone any grouping or ordering columns;
6668 * those are needed before the sort.  If we do flatten a particular
6669 * expression, we leave Aggref and WindowFunc nodes alone, since those were
6670 * computed earlier.
6671 *
6672 * 'final_target' is the query's final target list (in PathTarget form)
6673 * 'have_postponed_srfs' is an output argument, see below
6674 *
6675 * The result is the PathTarget to be computed by the plan node immediately
6676 * below the Sort step (and the Distinct step, if any).  This will be
6677 * exactly final_target if we decide a projection step wouldn't be helpful.
6678 *
6679 * In addition, *have_postponed_srfs is set to true if we choose to postpone
6680 * any set-returning functions to after the Sort.
6681 */
6682static PathTarget *
6683make_sort_input_target(PlannerInfo *root,
6684					   PathTarget *final_target,
6685					   bool *have_postponed_srfs)
6686{
6687	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
6688	PathTarget *input_target;
6689	int			ncols;
6690	bool	   *col_is_srf;
6691	bool	   *postpone_col;
6692	bool		have_srf;
6693	bool		have_volatile;
6694	bool		have_expensive;
6695	bool		have_srf_sortcols;
6696	bool		postpone_srfs;
6697	List	   *postponable_cols;
6698	List	   *postponable_vars;
6699	int			i;
6700	ListCell   *lc;
6701
6702	/* Shouldn't get here unless query has ORDER BY */
6703	Assert(parse->sortClause);
6704
6705	*have_postponed_srfs = false;	/* default result */
6706
6707	/* Inspect tlist and collect per-column information */
6708	ncols = list_length(final_target->exprs);
6709	col_is_srf = (bool *) palloc0(ncols * sizeof(bool));
6710	postpone_col = (bool *) palloc0(ncols * sizeof(bool));
6711	have_srf = have_volatile = have_expensive = have_srf_sortcols = false;
6712
6713	i = 0;
6714	foreach(lc, final_target->exprs)
6715	{
6716		Expr	   *expr = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
6717
6718		/*
6719		 * If the column has a sortgroupref, assume it has to be evaluated
6720		 * before sorting.  Generally such columns would be ORDER BY, GROUP
6721		 * BY, etc targets.  One exception is columns that were removed from
6722		 * GROUP BY by remove_useless_groupby_columns() ... but those would
6723		 * only be Vars anyway.  There don't seem to be any cases where it
6724		 * would be worth the trouble to double-check.
6725		 */
6726		if (get_pathtarget_sortgroupref(final_target, i) == 0)
6727		{
6728			/*
6729			 * Check for SRF or volatile functions.  Check the SRF case first
6730			 * because we must know whether we have any postponed SRFs.
6731			 */
6732			if (parse->hasTargetSRFs &&
6733				expression_returns_set((Node *) expr))
6734			{
6735				/* We'll decide below whether these are postponable */
6736				col_is_srf[i] = true;
6737				have_srf = true;
6738			}
6739			else if (contain_volatile_functions((Node *) expr))
6740			{
6741				/* Unconditionally postpone */
6742				postpone_col[i] = true;
6743				have_volatile = true;
6744			}
6745			else
6746			{
6747				/*
6748				 * Else check the cost.  XXX it's annoying to have to do this
6749				 * when set_pathtarget_cost_width() just did it.  Refactor to
6750				 * allow sharing the work?
6751				 */
6752				QualCost	cost;
6753
6754				cost_qual_eval_node(&cost, (Node *) expr, root);
6755
6756				/*
6757				 * We arbitrarily define "expensive" as "more than 10X
6758				 * cpu_operator_cost".  Note this will take in any PL function
6759				 * with default cost.
6760				 */
6761				if (cost.per_tuple > 10 * cpu_operator_cost)
6762				{
6763					postpone_col[i] = true;
6764					have_expensive = true;
6765				}
6766			}
6767		}
6768		else
6769		{
6770			/* For sortgroupref cols, just check if any contain SRFs */
6771			if (!have_srf_sortcols &&
6772				parse->hasTargetSRFs &&
6773				expression_returns_set((Node *) expr))
6774				have_srf_sortcols = true;
6775		}
6776
6777		i++;
6778	}
6779
6780	/*
6781	 * We can postpone SRFs if we have some but none are in sortgroupref cols.
6782	 */
6783	postpone_srfs = (have_srf && !have_srf_sortcols);
6784
6785	/*
6786	 * If we don't need a post-sort projection, just return final_target.
6787	 */
6788	if (!(postpone_srfs || have_volatile ||
6789		  (have_expensive &&
6790		   (parse->limitCount || root->tuple_fraction > 0))))
6791		return final_target;
6792
6793	/*
6794	 * Report whether the post-sort projection will contain set-returning
6795	 * functions.  This is important because it affects whether the Sort can
6796	 * rely on the query's LIMIT (if any) to bound the number of rows it needs
6797	 * to return.
6798	 */
6799	*have_postponed_srfs = postpone_srfs;
6800
6801	/*
6802	 * Construct the sort-input target, taking all non-postponable columns and
6803	 * then adding Vars, PlaceHolderVars, Aggrefs, and WindowFuncs found in
6804	 * the postponable ones.
6805	 */
6806	input_target = create_empty_pathtarget();
6807	postponable_cols = NIL;
6808
6809	i = 0;
6810	foreach(lc, final_target->exprs)
6811	{
6812		Expr	   *expr = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
6813
6814		if (postpone_col[i] || (postpone_srfs && col_is_srf[i]))
6815			postponable_cols = lappend(postponable_cols, expr);
6816		else
6817			add_column_to_pathtarget(input_target, expr,
6818									 get_pathtarget_sortgroupref(final_target, i));
6819
6820		i++;
6821	}
6822
6823	/*
6824	 * Pull out all the Vars, Aggrefs, and WindowFuncs mentioned in
6825	 * postponable columns, and add them to the sort-input target if not
6826	 * already present.  (Some might be there already.)  We mustn't
6827	 * deconstruct Aggrefs or WindowFuncs here, since the projection node
6828	 * would be unable to recompute them.
6829	 */
6830	postponable_vars = pull_var_clause((Node *) postponable_cols,
6831									   PVC_INCLUDE_AGGREGATES |
6832									   PVC_INCLUDE_WINDOWFUNCS |
6833									   PVC_INCLUDE_PLACEHOLDERS);
6834	add_new_columns_to_pathtarget(input_target, postponable_vars);
6835
6836	/* clean up cruft */
6837	list_free(postponable_vars);
6838	list_free(postponable_cols);
6839
6840	/* XXX this represents even more redundant cost calculation ... */
6841	return set_pathtarget_cost_width(root, input_target);
6842}
6843
6844/*
6845 * get_cheapest_fractional_path
6846 *	  Find the cheapest path for retrieving a specified fraction of all
6847 *	  the tuples expected to be returned by the given relation.
6848 *
6849 * Do not consider parameterized paths.  If the caller needs a path for upper
6850 * rel, it can't have parameterized paths.  If the caller needs an append
6851 * subpath, it could become limited by the treatment of similar
6852 * parameterization of all the subpaths.
6853 *
6854 * We interpret tuple_fraction the same way as grouping_planner.
6855 *
6856 * We assume set_cheapest() has been run on the given rel.
6857 */
6858Path *
6859get_cheapest_fractional_path(RelOptInfo *rel, double tuple_fraction)
6860{
6861	Path	   *best_path = rel->cheapest_total_path;
6862	ListCell   *l;
6863
6864	/* If all tuples will be retrieved, just return the cheapest-total path */
6865	if (tuple_fraction <= 0.0)
6866		return best_path;
6867
6868	/* Convert absolute # of tuples to a fraction; no need to clamp to 0..1 */
6869	if (tuple_fraction >= 1.0 && best_path->rows > 0)
6870		tuple_fraction /= best_path->rows;
6871
6872	foreach(l, rel->pathlist)
6873	{
6874		Path	   *path = (Path *) lfirst(l);
6875
6876		if (path->param_info)
6877			continue;
6878
6879		if (path == rel->cheapest_total_path ||
6880			compare_fractional_path_costs(best_path, path, tuple_fraction) <= 0)
6881			continue;
6882
6883		best_path = path;
6884	}
6885
6886	return best_path;
6887}
6888
6889/*
6890 * adjust_paths_for_srfs
6891 *		Fix up the Paths of the given upperrel to handle tSRFs properly.
6892 *
6893 * The executor can only handle set-returning functions that appear at the
6894 * top level of the targetlist of a ProjectSet plan node.  If we have any SRFs
6895 * that are not at top level, we need to split up the evaluation into multiple
6896 * plan levels in which each level satisfies this constraint.  This function
6897 * modifies each Path of an upperrel that (might) compute any SRFs in its
6898 * output tlist to insert appropriate projection steps.
6899 *
6900 * The given targets and targets_contain_srfs lists are from
6901 * split_pathtarget_at_srfs().  We assume the existing Paths emit the first
6902 * target in targets.
6903 */
6904static void
6905adjust_paths_for_srfs(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *rel,
6906					  List *targets, List *targets_contain_srfs)
6907{
6908	ListCell   *lc;
6909
6910	Assert(list_length(targets) == list_length(targets_contain_srfs));
6911	Assert(!linitial_int(targets_contain_srfs));
6912
6913	/* If no SRFs appear at this plan level, nothing to do */
6914	if (list_length(targets) == 1)
6915		return;
6916
6917	/*
6918	 * Stack SRF-evaluation nodes atop each path for the rel.
6919	 *
6920	 * In principle we should re-run set_cheapest() here to identify the
6921	 * cheapest path, but it seems unlikely that adding the same tlist eval
6922	 * costs to all the paths would change that, so we don't bother. Instead,
6923	 * just assume that the cheapest-startup and cheapest-total paths remain
6924	 * so.  (There should be no parameterized paths anymore, so we needn't
6925	 * worry about updating cheapest_parameterized_paths.)
6926	 */
6927	foreach(lc, rel->pathlist)
6928	{
6929		Path	   *subpath = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
6930		Path	   *newpath = subpath;
6931		ListCell   *lc1,
6932				   *lc2;
6933
6934		Assert(subpath->param_info == NULL);
6935		forboth(lc1, targets, lc2, targets_contain_srfs)
6936		{
6937			PathTarget *thistarget = lfirst_node(PathTarget, lc1);
6938			bool		contains_srfs = (bool) lfirst_int(lc2);
6939
6940			/* If this level doesn't contain SRFs, do regular projection */
6941			if (contains_srfs)
6942				newpath = (Path *) create_set_projection_path(root,
6943															  rel,
6944															  newpath,
6945															  thistarget);
6946			else
6947				newpath = (Path *) apply_projection_to_path(root,
6948															rel,
6949															newpath,
6950															thistarget);
6951		}
6952		lfirst(lc) = newpath;
6953		if (subpath == rel->cheapest_startup_path)
6954			rel->cheapest_startup_path = newpath;
6955		if (subpath == rel->cheapest_total_path)
6956			rel->cheapest_total_path = newpath;
6957	}
6958
6959	/* Likewise for partial paths, if any */
6960	foreach(lc, rel->partial_pathlist)
6961	{
6962		Path	   *subpath = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
6963		Path	   *newpath = subpath;
6964		ListCell   *lc1,
6965				   *lc2;
6966
6967		Assert(subpath->param_info == NULL);
6968		forboth(lc1, targets, lc2, targets_contain_srfs)
6969		{
6970			PathTarget *thistarget = lfirst_node(PathTarget, lc1);
6971			bool		contains_srfs = (bool) lfirst_int(lc2);
6972
6973			/* If this level doesn't contain SRFs, do regular projection */
6974			if (contains_srfs)
6975				newpath = (Path *) create_set_projection_path(root,
6976															  rel,
6977															  newpath,
6978															  thistarget);
6979			else
6980			{
6981				/* avoid apply_projection_to_path, in case of multiple refs */
6982				newpath = (Path *) create_projection_path(root,
6983														  rel,
6984														  newpath,
6985														  thistarget);
6986			}
6987		}
6988		lfirst(lc) = newpath;
6989	}
6990}
6991
6992/*
6993 * expression_planner
6994 *		Perform planner's transformations on a standalone expression.
6995 *
6996 * Various utility commands need to evaluate expressions that are not part
6997 * of a plannable query.  They can do so using the executor's regular
6998 * expression-execution machinery, but first the expression has to be fed
6999 * through here to transform it from parser output to something executable.
7000 *
7001 * Currently, we disallow sublinks in standalone expressions, so there's no
7002 * real "planning" involved here.  (That might not always be true though.)
7003 * What we must do is run eval_const_expressions to ensure that any function
7004 * calls are converted to positional notation and function default arguments
7005 * get inserted.  The fact that constant subexpressions get simplified is a
7006 * side-effect that is useful when the expression will get evaluated more than
7007 * once.  Also, we must fix operator function IDs.
7008 *
7009 * This does not return any information about dependencies of the expression.
7010 * Hence callers should use the results only for the duration of the current
7011 * query.  Callers that would like to cache the results for longer should use
7012 * expression_planner_with_deps, probably via the plancache.
7013 *
7014 * Note: this must not make any damaging changes to the passed-in expression
7015 * tree.  (It would actually be okay to apply fix_opfuncids to it, but since
7016 * we first do an expression_tree_mutator-based walk, what is returned will
7017 * be a new node tree.)  The result is constructed in the current memory
7018 * context; beware that this can leak a lot of additional stuff there, too.
7019 */
7020Expr *
7021expression_planner(Expr *expr)
7022{
7023	Node	   *result;
7024
7025	/*
7026	 * Convert named-argument function calls, insert default arguments and
7027	 * simplify constant subexprs
7028	 */
7029	result = eval_const_expressions(NULL, (Node *) expr);
7030
7031	/* Fill in opfuncid values if missing */
7032	fix_opfuncids(result);
7033
7034	return (Expr *) result;
7035}
7036
7037/*
7038 * expression_planner_with_deps
7039 *		Perform planner's transformations on a standalone expression,
7040 *		returning expression dependency information along with the result.
7041 *
7042 * This is identical to expression_planner() except that it also returns
7043 * information about possible dependencies of the expression, ie identities of
7044 * objects whose definitions affect the result.  As in a PlannedStmt, these
7045 * are expressed as a list of relation Oids and a list of PlanInvalItems.
7046 */
7047Expr *
7048expression_planner_with_deps(Expr *expr,
7049							 List **relationOids,
7050							 List **invalItems)
7051{
7052	Node	   *result;
7053	PlannerGlobal glob;
7054	PlannerInfo root;
7055
7056	/* Make up dummy planner state so we can use setrefs machinery */
7057	MemSet(&glob, 0, sizeof(glob));
7058	glob.type = T_PlannerGlobal;
7059	glob.relationOids = NIL;
7060	glob.invalItems = NIL;
7061
7062	MemSet(&root, 0, sizeof(root));
7063	root.type = T_PlannerInfo;
7064	root.glob = &glob;
7065
7066	/*
7067	 * Convert named-argument function calls, insert default arguments and
7068	 * simplify constant subexprs.  Collect identities of inlined functions
7069	 * and elided domains, too.
7070	 */
7071	result = eval_const_expressions(&root, (Node *) expr);
7072
7073	/* Fill in opfuncid values if missing */
7074	fix_opfuncids(result);
7075
7076	/*
7077	 * Now walk the finished expression to find anything else we ought to
7078	 * record as an expression dependency.
7079	 */
7080	(void) extract_query_dependencies_walker(result, &root);
7081
7082	*relationOids = glob.relationOids;
7083	*invalItems = glob.invalItems;
7084
7085	return (Expr *) result;
7086}
7087
7088
7089/*
7090 * plan_cluster_use_sort
7091 *		Use the planner to decide how CLUSTER should implement sorting
7092 *
7093 * tableOid is the OID of a table to be clustered on its index indexOid
7094 * (which is already known to be a btree index).  Decide whether it's
7095 * cheaper to do an indexscan or a seqscan-plus-sort to execute the CLUSTER.
7096 * Return true to use sorting, false to use an indexscan.
7097 *
7098 * Note: caller had better already hold some type of lock on the table.
7099 */
7100bool
7101plan_cluster_use_sort(Oid tableOid, Oid indexOid)
7102{
7103	PlannerInfo *root;
7104	Query	   *query;
7105	PlannerGlobal *glob;
7106	RangeTblEntry *rte;
7107	RelOptInfo *rel;
7108	IndexOptInfo *indexInfo;
7109	QualCost	indexExprCost;
7110	Cost		comparisonCost;
7111	Path	   *seqScanPath;
7112	Path		seqScanAndSortPath;
7113	IndexPath  *indexScanPath;
7114	ListCell   *lc;
7115
7116	/* We can short-circuit the cost comparison if indexscans are disabled */
7117	if (!enable_indexscan)
7118		return true;			/* use sort */
7119
7120	/* Set up mostly-dummy planner state */
7121	query = makeNode(Query);
7122	query->commandType = CMD_SELECT;
7123
7124	glob = makeNode(PlannerGlobal);
7125
7126	root = makeNode(PlannerInfo);
7127	root->parse = query;
7128	root->glob = glob;
7129	root->query_level = 1;
7130	root->planner_cxt = CurrentMemoryContext;
7131	root->wt_param_id = -1;
7132	root->join_domains = list_make1(makeNode(JoinDomain));
7133
7134	/* Build a minimal RTE for the rel */
7135	rte = makeNode(RangeTblEntry);
7136	rte->rtekind = RTE_RELATION;
7137	rte->relid = tableOid;
7138	rte->relkind = RELKIND_RELATION;	/* Don't be too picky. */
7139	rte->rellockmode = AccessShareLock;
7140	rte->lateral = false;
7141	rte->inh = false;
7142	rte->inFromCl = true;
7143	query->rtable = list_make1(rte);
7144	addRTEPermissionInfo(&query->rteperminfos, rte);
7145
7146	/* Set up RTE/RelOptInfo arrays */
7147	setup_simple_rel_arrays(root);
7148
7149	/* Build RelOptInfo */
7150	rel = build_simple_rel(root, 1, NULL);
7151
7152	/* Locate IndexOptInfo for the target index */
7153	indexInfo = NULL;
7154	foreach(lc, rel->indexlist)
7155	{
7156		indexInfo = lfirst_node(IndexOptInfo, lc);
7157		if (indexInfo->indexoid == indexOid)
7158			break;
7159	}
7160
7161	/*
7162	 * It's possible that get_relation_info did not generate an IndexOptInfo
7163	 * for the desired index; this could happen if it's not yet reached its
7164	 * indcheckxmin usability horizon, or if it's a system index and we're
7165	 * ignoring system indexes.  In such cases we should tell CLUSTER to not
7166	 * trust the index contents but use seqscan-and-sort.
7167	 */
7168	if (lc == NULL)				/* not in the list? */
7169		return true;			/* use sort */
7170
7171	/*
7172	 * Rather than doing all the pushups that would be needed to use
7173	 * set_baserel_size_estimates, just do a quick hack for rows and width.
7174	 */
7175	rel->rows = rel->tuples;
7176	rel->reltarget->width = get_relation_data_width(tableOid, NULL);
7177
7178	root->total_table_pages = rel->pages;
7179
7180	/*
7181	 * Determine eval cost of the index expressions, if any.  We need to
7182	 * charge twice that amount for each tuple comparison that happens during
7183	 * the sort, since tuplesort.c will have to re-evaluate the index
7184	 * expressions each time.  (XXX that's pretty inefficient...)
7185	 */
7186	cost_qual_eval(&indexExprCost, indexInfo->indexprs, root);
7187	comparisonCost = 2.0 * (indexExprCost.startup + indexExprCost.per_tuple);
7188
7189	/* Estimate the cost of seq scan + sort */
7190	seqScanPath = create_seqscan_path(root, rel, NULL, 0);
7191	cost_sort(&seqScanAndSortPath, root, NIL,
7192			  seqScanPath->disabled_nodes,
7193			  seqScanPath->total_cost, rel->tuples, rel->reltarget->width,
7194			  comparisonCost, maintenance_work_mem, -1.0);
7195
7196	/* Estimate the cost of index scan */
7197	indexScanPath = create_index_path(root, indexInfo,
7198									  NIL, NIL, NIL, NIL,
7199									  ForwardScanDirection, false,
7200									  NULL, 1.0, false);
7201
7202	return (seqScanAndSortPath.total_cost < indexScanPath->path.total_cost);
7203}
7204
7205/*
7206 * plan_create_index_workers
7207 *		Use the planner to decide how many parallel worker processes
7208 *		CREATE INDEX should request for use
7209 *
7210 * tableOid is the table on which the index is to be built.  indexOid is the
7211 * OID of an index to be created or reindexed (which must be an index with
7212 * support for parallel builds - currently btree, GIN, or BRIN).
7213 *
7214 * Return value is the number of parallel worker processes to request.  It
7215 * may be unsafe to proceed if this is 0.  Note that this does not include the
7216 * leader participating as a worker (value is always a number of parallel
7217 * worker processes).
7218 *
7219 * Note: caller had better already hold some type of lock on the table and
7220 * index.
7221 */
7222int
7223plan_create_index_workers(Oid tableOid, Oid indexOid)
7224{
7225	PlannerInfo *root;
7226	Query	   *query;
7227	PlannerGlobal *glob;
7228	RangeTblEntry *rte;
7229	Relation	heap;
7230	Relation	index;
7231	RelOptInfo *rel;
7232	int			parallel_workers;
7233	BlockNumber heap_blocks;
7234	double		reltuples;
7235	double		allvisfrac;
7236
7237	/*
7238	 * We don't allow performing parallel operation in standalone backend or
7239	 * when parallelism is disabled.
7240	 */
7241	if (!IsUnderPostmaster || max_parallel_maintenance_workers == 0)
7242		return 0;
7243
7244	/* Set up largely-dummy planner state */
7245	query = makeNode(Query);
7246	query->commandType = CMD_SELECT;
7247
7248	glob = makeNode(PlannerGlobal);
7249
7250	root = makeNode(PlannerInfo);
7251	root->parse = query;
7252	root->glob = glob;
7253	root->query_level = 1;
7254	root->planner_cxt = CurrentMemoryContext;
7255	root->wt_param_id = -1;
7256	root->join_domains = list_make1(makeNode(JoinDomain));
7257
7258	/*
7259	 * Build a minimal RTE.
7260	 *
7261	 * Mark the RTE with inh = true.  This is a kludge to prevent
7262	 * get_relation_info() from fetching index info, which is necessary
7263	 * because it does not expect that any IndexOptInfo is currently
7264	 * undergoing REINDEX.
7265	 */
7266	rte = makeNode(RangeTblEntry);
7267	rte->rtekind = RTE_RELATION;
7268	rte->relid = tableOid;
7269	rte->relkind = RELKIND_RELATION;	/* Don't be too picky. */
7270	rte->rellockmode = AccessShareLock;
7271	rte->lateral = false;
7272	rte->inh = true;
7273	rte->inFromCl = true;
7274	query->rtable = list_make1(rte);
7275	addRTEPermissionInfo(&query->rteperminfos, rte);
7276
7277	/* Set up RTE/RelOptInfo arrays */
7278	setup_simple_rel_arrays(root);
7279
7280	/* Build RelOptInfo */
7281	rel = build_simple_rel(root, 1, NULL);
7282
7283	/* Rels are assumed already locked by the caller */
7284	heap = table_open(tableOid, NoLock);
7285	index = index_open(indexOid, NoLock);
7286
7287	/*
7288	 * Determine if it's safe to proceed.
7289	 *
7290	 * Currently, parallel workers can't access the leader's temporary tables.
7291	 * Furthermore, any index predicate or index expressions must be parallel
7292	 * safe.
7293	 */
7294	if (heap->rd_rel->relpersistence == RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP ||
7295		!is_parallel_safe(root, (Node *) RelationGetIndexExpressions(index)) ||
7296		!is_parallel_safe(root, (Node *) RelationGetIndexPredicate(index)))
7297	{
7298		parallel_workers = 0;
7299		goto done;
7300	}
7301
7302	/*
7303	 * If parallel_workers storage parameter is set for the table, accept that
7304	 * as the number of parallel worker processes to launch (though still cap
7305	 * at max_parallel_maintenance_workers).  Note that we deliberately do not
7306	 * consider any other factor when parallel_workers is set. (e.g., memory
7307	 * use by workers.)
7308	 */
7309	if (rel->rel_parallel_workers != -1)
7310	{
7311		parallel_workers = Min(rel->rel_parallel_workers,
7312							   max_parallel_maintenance_workers);
7313		goto done;
7314	}
7315
7316	/*
7317	 * Estimate heap relation size ourselves, since rel->pages cannot be
7318	 * trusted (heap RTE was marked as inheritance parent)
7319	 */
7320	estimate_rel_size(heap, NULL, &heap_blocks, &reltuples, &allvisfrac);
7321
7322	/*
7323	 * Determine number of workers to scan the heap relation using generic
7324	 * model
7325	 */
7326	parallel_workers = compute_parallel_worker(rel, heap_blocks, -1,
7327											   max_parallel_maintenance_workers);
7328
7329	/*
7330	 * Cap workers based on available maintenance_work_mem as needed.
7331	 *
7332	 * Note that each tuplesort participant receives an even share of the
7333	 * total maintenance_work_mem budget.  Aim to leave participants
7334	 * (including the leader as a participant) with no less than 32MB of
7335	 * memory.  This leaves cases where maintenance_work_mem is set to 64MB
7336	 * immediately past the threshold of being capable of launching a single
7337	 * parallel worker to sort.
7338	 */
7339	while (parallel_workers > 0 &&
7340		   maintenance_work_mem / (parallel_workers + 1) < 32 * 1024)
7341		parallel_workers--;
7342
7343done:
7344	index_close(index, NoLock);
7345	table_close(heap, NoLock);
7346
7347	return parallel_workers;
7348}
7349
7350/*
7351 * add_paths_to_grouping_rel
7352 *
7353 * Add non-partial paths to grouping relation.
7354 */
7355static void
7356add_paths_to_grouping_rel(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *input_rel,
7357						  RelOptInfo *grouped_rel,
7358						  RelOptInfo *partially_grouped_rel,
7359						  const AggClauseCosts *agg_costs,
7360						  grouping_sets_data *gd, double dNumGroups,
7361						  GroupPathExtraData *extra)
7362{
7363	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
7364	Path	   *cheapest_path = input_rel->cheapest_total_path;
7365	ListCell   *lc;
7366	bool		can_hash = (extra->flags & GROUPING_CAN_USE_HASH) != 0;
7367	bool		can_sort = (extra->flags & GROUPING_CAN_USE_SORT) != 0;
7368	List	   *havingQual = (List *) extra->havingQual;
7369	AggClauseCosts *agg_final_costs = &extra->agg_final_costs;
7370
7371	if (can_sort)
7372	{
7373		/*
7374		 * Use any available suitably-sorted path as input, and also consider
7375		 * sorting the cheapest-total path and incremental sort on any paths
7376		 * with presorted keys.
7377		 */
7378		foreach(lc, input_rel->pathlist)
7379		{
7380			ListCell   *lc2;
7381			Path	   *path = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
7382			Path	   *path_save = path;
7383			List	   *pathkey_orderings = NIL;
7384
7385			/* generate alternative group orderings that might be useful */
7386			pathkey_orderings = get_useful_group_keys_orderings(root, path);
7387
7388			Assert(list_length(pathkey_orderings) > 0);
7389
7390			foreach(lc2, pathkey_orderings)
7391			{
7392				GroupByOrdering *info = (GroupByOrdering *) lfirst(lc2);
7393
7394				/* restore the path (we replace it in the loop) */
7395				path = path_save;
7396
7397				path = make_ordered_path(root,
7398										 grouped_rel,
7399										 path,
7400										 cheapest_path,
7401										 info->pathkeys,
7402										 -1.0);
7403				if (path == NULL)
7404					continue;
7405
7406				/* Now decide what to stick atop it */
7407				if (parse->groupingSets)
7408				{
7409					consider_groupingsets_paths(root, grouped_rel,
7410												path, true, can_hash,
7411												gd, agg_costs, dNumGroups);
7412				}
7413				else if (parse->hasAggs)
7414				{
7415					/*
7416					 * We have aggregation, possibly with plain GROUP BY. Make
7417					 * an AggPath.
7418					 */
7419					add_path(grouped_rel, (Path *)
7420							 create_agg_path(root,
7421											 grouped_rel,
7422											 path,
7423											 grouped_rel->reltarget,
7424											 parse->groupClause ? AGG_SORTED : AGG_PLAIN,
7425											 AGGSPLIT_SIMPLE,
7426											 info->clauses,
7427											 havingQual,
7428											 agg_costs,
7429											 dNumGroups));
7430				}
7431				else if (parse->groupClause)
7432				{
7433					/*
7434					 * We have GROUP BY without aggregation or grouping sets.
7435					 * Make a GroupPath.
7436					 */
7437					add_path(grouped_rel, (Path *)
7438							 create_group_path(root,
7439											   grouped_rel,
7440											   path,
7441											   info->clauses,
7442											   havingQual,
7443											   dNumGroups));
7444				}
7445				else
7446				{
7447					/* Other cases should have been handled above */
7448					Assert(false);
7449				}
7450			}
7451		}
7452
7453		/*
7454		 * Instead of operating directly on the input relation, we can
7455		 * consider finalizing a partially aggregated path.
7456		 */
7457		if (partially_grouped_rel != NULL)
7458		{
7459			foreach(lc, partially_grouped_rel->pathlist)
7460			{
7461				ListCell   *lc2;
7462				Path	   *path = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
7463				Path	   *path_save = path;
7464				List	   *pathkey_orderings = NIL;
7465
7466				/* generate alternative group orderings that might be useful */
7467				pathkey_orderings = get_useful_group_keys_orderings(root, path);
7468
7469				Assert(list_length(pathkey_orderings) > 0);
7470
7471				/* process all potentially interesting grouping reorderings */
7472				foreach(lc2, pathkey_orderings)
7473				{
7474					GroupByOrdering *info = (GroupByOrdering *) lfirst(lc2);
7475
7476					/* restore the path (we replace it in the loop) */
7477					path = path_save;
7478
7479					path = make_ordered_path(root,
7480											 grouped_rel,
7481											 path,
7482											 partially_grouped_rel->cheapest_total_path,
7483											 info->pathkeys,
7484											 -1.0);
7485
7486					if (path == NULL)
7487						continue;
7488
7489					if (parse->hasAggs)
7490						add_path(grouped_rel, (Path *)
7491								 create_agg_path(root,
7492												 grouped_rel,
7493												 path,
7494												 grouped_rel->reltarget,
7495												 parse->groupClause ? AGG_SORTED : AGG_PLAIN,
7496												 AGGSPLIT_FINAL_DESERIAL,
7497												 info->clauses,
7498												 havingQual,
7499												 agg_final_costs,
7500												 dNumGroups));
7501					else
7502						add_path(grouped_rel, (Path *)
7503								 create_group_path(root,
7504												   grouped_rel,
7505												   path,
7506												   info->clauses,
7507												   havingQual,
7508												   dNumGroups));
7509
7510				}
7511			}
7512		}
7513	}
7514
7515	if (can_hash)
7516	{
7517		if (parse->groupingSets)
7518		{
7519			/*
7520			 * Try for a hash-only groupingsets path over unsorted input.
7521			 */
7522			consider_groupingsets_paths(root, grouped_rel,
7523										cheapest_path, false, true,
7524										gd, agg_costs, dNumGroups);
7525		}
7526		else
7527		{
7528			/*
7529			 * Generate a HashAgg Path.  We just need an Agg over the
7530			 * cheapest-total input path, since input order won't matter.
7531			 */
7532			add_path(grouped_rel, (Path *)
7533					 create_agg_path(root, grouped_rel,
7534									 cheapest_path,
7535									 grouped_rel->reltarget,
7536									 AGG_HASHED,
7537									 AGGSPLIT_SIMPLE,
7538									 root->processed_groupClause,
7539									 havingQual,
7540									 agg_costs,
7541									 dNumGroups));
7542		}
7543
7544		/*
7545		 * Generate a Finalize HashAgg Path atop of the cheapest partially
7546		 * grouped path, assuming there is one
7547		 */
7548		if (partially_grouped_rel && partially_grouped_rel->pathlist)
7549		{
7550			Path	   *path = partially_grouped_rel->cheapest_total_path;
7551
7552			add_path(grouped_rel, (Path *)
7553					 create_agg_path(root,
7554									 grouped_rel,
7555									 path,
7556									 grouped_rel->reltarget,
7557									 AGG_HASHED,
7558									 AGGSPLIT_FINAL_DESERIAL,
7559									 root->processed_groupClause,
7560									 havingQual,
7561									 agg_final_costs,
7562									 dNumGroups));
7563		}
7564	}
7565
7566	/*
7567	 * When partitionwise aggregate is used, we might have fully aggregated
7568	 * paths in the partial pathlist, because add_paths_to_append_rel() will
7569	 * consider a path for grouped_rel consisting of a Parallel Append of
7570	 * non-partial paths from each child.
7571	 */
7572	if (grouped_rel->partial_pathlist != NIL)
7573		gather_grouping_paths(root, grouped_rel);
7574}
7575
7576/*
7577 * create_partial_grouping_paths
7578 *
7579 * Create a new upper relation representing the result of partial aggregation
7580 * and populate it with appropriate paths.  Note that we don't finalize the
7581 * lists of paths here, so the caller can add additional partial or non-partial
7582 * paths and must afterward call gather_grouping_paths and set_cheapest on
7583 * the returned upper relation.
7584 *
7585 * All paths for this new upper relation -- both partial and non-partial --
7586 * have been partially aggregated but require a subsequent FinalizeAggregate
7587 * step.
7588 *
7589 * NB: This function is allowed to return NULL if it determines that there is
7590 * no real need to create a new RelOptInfo.
7591 */
7592static RelOptInfo *
7593create_partial_grouping_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
7594							  RelOptInfo *grouped_rel,
7595							  RelOptInfo *input_rel,
7596							  grouping_sets_data *gd,
7597							  GroupPathExtraData *extra,
7598							  bool force_rel_creation)
7599{
7600	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
7601	RelOptInfo *partially_grouped_rel;
7602	AggClauseCosts *agg_partial_costs = &extra->agg_partial_costs;
7603	AggClauseCosts *agg_final_costs = &extra->agg_final_costs;
7604	Path	   *cheapest_partial_path = NULL;
7605	Path	   *cheapest_total_path = NULL;
7606	double		dNumPartialGroups = 0;
7607	double		dNumPartialPartialGroups = 0;
7608	ListCell   *lc;
7609	bool		can_hash = (extra->flags & GROUPING_CAN_USE_HASH) != 0;
7610	bool		can_sort = (extra->flags & GROUPING_CAN_USE_SORT) != 0;
7611
7612	/*
7613	 * Consider whether we should generate partially aggregated non-partial
7614	 * paths.  We can only do this if we have a non-partial path, and only if
7615	 * the parent of the input rel is performing partial partitionwise
7616	 * aggregation.  (Note that extra->patype is the type of partitionwise
7617	 * aggregation being used at the parent level, not this level.)
7618	 */
7619	if (input_rel->pathlist != NIL &&
7620		extra->patype == PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_PARTIAL)
7621		cheapest_total_path = input_rel->cheapest_total_path;
7622
7623	/*
7624	 * If parallelism is possible for grouped_rel, then we should consider
7625	 * generating partially-grouped partial paths.  However, if the input rel
7626	 * has no partial paths, then we can't.
7627	 */
7628	if (grouped_rel->consider_parallel && input_rel->partial_pathlist != NIL)
7629		cheapest_partial_path = linitial(input_rel->partial_pathlist);
7630
7631	/*
7632	 * If we can't partially aggregate partial paths, and we can't partially
7633	 * aggregate non-partial paths, then don't bother creating the new
7634	 * RelOptInfo at all, unless the caller specified force_rel_creation.
7635	 */
7636	if (cheapest_total_path == NULL &&
7637		cheapest_partial_path == NULL &&
7638		!force_rel_creation)
7639		return NULL;
7640
7641	/*
7642	 * Build a new upper relation to represent the result of partially
7643	 * aggregating the rows from the input relation.
7644	 */
7645	partially_grouped_rel = fetch_upper_rel(root,
7646											UPPERREL_PARTIAL_GROUP_AGG,
7647											grouped_rel->relids);
7648	partially_grouped_rel->consider_parallel =
7649		grouped_rel->consider_parallel;
7650	partially_grouped_rel->reloptkind = grouped_rel->reloptkind;
7651	partially_grouped_rel->serverid = grouped_rel->serverid;
7652	partially_grouped_rel->userid = grouped_rel->userid;
7653	partially_grouped_rel->useridiscurrent = grouped_rel->useridiscurrent;
7654	partially_grouped_rel->fdwroutine = grouped_rel->fdwroutine;
7655
7656	/*
7657	 * Build target list for partial aggregate paths.  These paths cannot just
7658	 * emit the same tlist as regular aggregate paths, because (1) we must
7659	 * include Vars and Aggrefs needed in HAVING, which might not appear in
7660	 * the result tlist, and (2) the Aggrefs must be set in partial mode.
7661	 */
7662	partially_grouped_rel->reltarget =
7663		make_partial_grouping_target(root, grouped_rel->reltarget,
7664									 extra->havingQual);
7665
7666	if (!extra->partial_costs_set)
7667	{
7668		/*
7669		 * Collect statistics about aggregates for estimating costs of
7670		 * performing aggregation in parallel.
7671		 */
7672		MemSet(agg_partial_costs, 0, sizeof(AggClauseCosts));
7673		MemSet(agg_final_costs, 0, sizeof(AggClauseCosts));
7674		if (parse->hasAggs)
7675		{
7676			/* partial phase */
7677			get_agg_clause_costs(root, AGGSPLIT_INITIAL_SERIAL,
7678								 agg_partial_costs);
7679
7680			/* final phase */
7681			get_agg_clause_costs(root, AGGSPLIT_FINAL_DESERIAL,
7682								 agg_final_costs);
7683		}
7684
7685		extra->partial_costs_set = true;
7686	}
7687
7688	/* Estimate number of partial groups. */
7689	if (cheapest_total_path != NULL)
7690		dNumPartialGroups =
7691			get_number_of_groups(root,
7692								 cheapest_total_path->rows,
7693								 gd,
7694								 extra->targetList);
7695	if (cheapest_partial_path != NULL)
7696		dNumPartialPartialGroups =
7697			get_number_of_groups(root,
7698								 cheapest_partial_path->rows,
7699								 gd,
7700								 extra->targetList);
7701
7702	if (can_sort && cheapest_total_path != NULL)
7703	{
7704		/* This should have been checked previously */
7705		Assert(parse->hasAggs || parse->groupClause);
7706
7707		/*
7708		 * Use any available suitably-sorted path as input, and also consider
7709		 * sorting the cheapest partial path.
7710		 */
7711		foreach(lc, input_rel->pathlist)
7712		{
7713			ListCell   *lc2;
7714			Path	   *path = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
7715			Path	   *path_save = path;
7716			List	   *pathkey_orderings = NIL;
7717
7718			/* generate alternative group orderings that might be useful */
7719			pathkey_orderings = get_useful_group_keys_orderings(root, path);
7720
7721			Assert(list_length(pathkey_orderings) > 0);
7722
7723			/* process all potentially interesting grouping reorderings */
7724			foreach(lc2, pathkey_orderings)
7725			{
7726				GroupByOrdering *info = (GroupByOrdering *) lfirst(lc2);
7727
7728				/* restore the path (we replace it in the loop) */
7729				path = path_save;
7730
7731				path = make_ordered_path(root,
7732										 partially_grouped_rel,
7733										 path,
7734										 cheapest_total_path,
7735										 info->pathkeys,
7736										 -1.0);
7737
7738				if (path == NULL)
7739					continue;
7740
7741				if (parse->hasAggs)
7742					add_path(partially_grouped_rel, (Path *)
7743							 create_agg_path(root,
7744											 partially_grouped_rel,
7745											 path,
7746											 partially_grouped_rel->reltarget,
7747											 parse->groupClause ? AGG_SORTED : AGG_PLAIN,
7748											 AGGSPLIT_INITIAL_SERIAL,
7749											 info->clauses,
7750											 NIL,
7751											 agg_partial_costs,
7752											 dNumPartialGroups));
7753				else
7754					add_path(partially_grouped_rel, (Path *)
7755							 create_group_path(root,
7756											   partially_grouped_rel,
7757											   path,
7758											   info->clauses,
7759											   NIL,
7760											   dNumPartialGroups));
7761			}
7762		}
7763	}
7764
7765	if (can_sort && cheapest_partial_path != NULL)
7766	{
7767		/* Similar to above logic, but for partial paths. */
7768		foreach(lc, input_rel->partial_pathlist)
7769		{
7770			ListCell   *lc2;
7771			Path	   *path = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
7772			Path	   *path_save = path;
7773			List	   *pathkey_orderings = NIL;
7774
7775			/* generate alternative group orderings that might be useful */
7776			pathkey_orderings = get_useful_group_keys_orderings(root, path);
7777
7778			Assert(list_length(pathkey_orderings) > 0);
7779
7780			/* process all potentially interesting grouping reorderings */
7781			foreach(lc2, pathkey_orderings)
7782			{
7783				GroupByOrdering *info = (GroupByOrdering *) lfirst(lc2);
7784
7785
7786				/* restore the path (we replace it in the loop) */
7787				path = path_save;
7788
7789				path = make_ordered_path(root,
7790										 partially_grouped_rel,
7791										 path,
7792										 cheapest_partial_path,
7793										 info->pathkeys,
7794										 -1.0);
7795
7796				if (path == NULL)
7797					continue;
7798
7799				if (parse->hasAggs)
7800					add_partial_path(partially_grouped_rel, (Path *)
7801									 create_agg_path(root,
7802													 partially_grouped_rel,
7803													 path,
7804													 partially_grouped_rel->reltarget,
7805													 parse->groupClause ? AGG_SORTED : AGG_PLAIN,
7806													 AGGSPLIT_INITIAL_SERIAL,
7807													 info->clauses,
7808													 NIL,
7809													 agg_partial_costs,
7810													 dNumPartialPartialGroups));
7811				else
7812					add_partial_path(partially_grouped_rel, (Path *)
7813									 create_group_path(root,
7814													   partially_grouped_rel,
7815													   path,
7816													   info->clauses,
7817													   NIL,
7818													   dNumPartialPartialGroups));
7819			}
7820		}
7821	}
7822
7823	/*
7824	 * Add a partially-grouped HashAgg Path where possible
7825	 */
7826	if (can_hash && cheapest_total_path != NULL)
7827	{
7828		/* Checked above */
7829		Assert(parse->hasAggs || parse->groupClause);
7830
7831		add_path(partially_grouped_rel, (Path *)
7832				 create_agg_path(root,
7833								 partially_grouped_rel,
7834								 cheapest_total_path,
7835								 partially_grouped_rel->reltarget,
7836								 AGG_HASHED,
7837								 AGGSPLIT_INITIAL_SERIAL,
7838								 root->processed_groupClause,
7839								 NIL,
7840								 agg_partial_costs,
7841								 dNumPartialGroups));
7842	}
7843
7844	/*
7845	 * Now add a partially-grouped HashAgg partial Path where possible
7846	 */
7847	if (can_hash && cheapest_partial_path != NULL)
7848	{
7849		add_partial_path(partially_grouped_rel, (Path *)
7850						 create_agg_path(root,
7851										 partially_grouped_rel,
7852										 cheapest_partial_path,
7853										 partially_grouped_rel->reltarget,
7854										 AGG_HASHED,
7855										 AGGSPLIT_INITIAL_SERIAL,
7856										 root->processed_groupClause,
7857										 NIL,
7858										 agg_partial_costs,
7859										 dNumPartialPartialGroups));
7860	}
7861
7862	/*
7863	 * If there is an FDW that's responsible for all baserels of the query,
7864	 * let it consider adding partially grouped ForeignPaths.
7865	 */
7866	if (partially_grouped_rel->fdwroutine &&
7867		partially_grouped_rel->fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths)
7868	{
7869		FdwRoutine *fdwroutine = partially_grouped_rel->fdwroutine;
7870
7871		fdwroutine->GetForeignUpperPaths(root,
7872										 UPPERREL_PARTIAL_GROUP_AGG,
7873										 input_rel, partially_grouped_rel,
7874										 extra);
7875	}
7876
7877	return partially_grouped_rel;
7878}
7879
7880/*
7881 * make_ordered_path
7882 *		Return a path ordered by 'pathkeys' based on the given 'path'.  May
7883 *		return NULL if it doesn't make sense to generate an ordered path in
7884 *		this case.
7885 */
7886static Path *
7887make_ordered_path(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *rel, Path *path,
7888				  Path *cheapest_path, List *pathkeys, double limit_tuples)
7889{
7890	bool		is_sorted;
7891	int			presorted_keys;
7892
7893	is_sorted = pathkeys_count_contained_in(pathkeys,
7894											path->pathkeys,
7895											&presorted_keys);
7896
7897	if (!is_sorted)
7898	{
7899		/*
7900		 * Try at least sorting the cheapest path and also try incrementally
7901		 * sorting any path which is partially sorted already (no need to deal
7902		 * with paths which have presorted keys when incremental sort is
7903		 * disabled unless it's the cheapest input path).
7904		 */
7905		if (path != cheapest_path &&
7906			(presorted_keys == 0 || !enable_incremental_sort))
7907			return NULL;
7908
7909		/*
7910		 * We've no need to consider both a sort and incremental sort. We'll
7911		 * just do a sort if there are no presorted keys and an incremental
7912		 * sort when there are presorted keys.
7913		 */
7914		if (presorted_keys == 0 || !enable_incremental_sort)
7915			path = (Path *) create_sort_path(root,
7916											 rel,
7917											 path,
7918											 pathkeys,
7919											 limit_tuples);
7920		else
7921			path = (Path *) create_incremental_sort_path(root,
7922														 rel,
7923														 path,
7924														 pathkeys,
7925														 presorted_keys,
7926														 limit_tuples);
7927	}
7928
7929	return path;
7930}
7931
7932/*
7933 * Generate Gather and Gather Merge paths for a grouping relation or partial
7934 * grouping relation.
7935 *
7936 * generate_useful_gather_paths does most of the work, but we also consider a
7937 * special case: we could try sorting the data by the group_pathkeys and then
7938 * applying Gather Merge.
7939 *
7940 * NB: This function shouldn't be used for anything other than a grouped or
7941 * partially grouped relation not only because of the fact that it explicitly
7942 * references group_pathkeys but we pass "true" as the third argument to
7943 * generate_useful_gather_paths().
7944 */
7945static void
7946gather_grouping_paths(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *rel)
7947{
7948	ListCell   *lc;
7949	Path	   *cheapest_partial_path;
7950	List	   *groupby_pathkeys;
7951
7952	/*
7953	 * This occurs after any partial aggregation has taken place, so trim off
7954	 * any pathkeys added for ORDER BY / DISTINCT aggregates.
7955	 */
7956	if (list_length(root->group_pathkeys) > root->num_groupby_pathkeys)
7957		groupby_pathkeys = list_copy_head(root->group_pathkeys,
7958										  root->num_groupby_pathkeys);
7959	else
7960		groupby_pathkeys = root->group_pathkeys;
7961
7962	/* Try Gather for unordered paths and Gather Merge for ordered ones. */
7963	generate_useful_gather_paths(root, rel, true);
7964
7965	cheapest_partial_path = linitial(rel->partial_pathlist);
7966
7967	/* XXX Shouldn't this also consider the group-key-reordering? */
7968	foreach(lc, rel->partial_pathlist)
7969	{
7970		Path	   *path = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
7971		bool		is_sorted;
7972		int			presorted_keys;
7973		double		total_groups;
7974
7975		is_sorted = pathkeys_count_contained_in(groupby_pathkeys,
7976												path->pathkeys,
7977												&presorted_keys);
7978
7979		if (is_sorted)
7980			continue;
7981
7982		/*
7983		 * Try at least sorting the cheapest path and also try incrementally
7984		 * sorting any path which is partially sorted already (no need to deal
7985		 * with paths which have presorted keys when incremental sort is
7986		 * disabled unless it's the cheapest input path).
7987		 */
7988		if (path != cheapest_partial_path &&
7989			(presorted_keys == 0 || !enable_incremental_sort))
7990			continue;
7991
7992		/*
7993		 * We've no need to consider both a sort and incremental sort. We'll
7994		 * just do a sort if there are no presorted keys and an incremental
7995		 * sort when there are presorted keys.
7996		 */
7997		if (presorted_keys == 0 || !enable_incremental_sort)
7998			path = (Path *) create_sort_path(root, rel, path,
7999											 groupby_pathkeys,
8000											 -1.0);
8001		else
8002			path = (Path *) create_incremental_sort_path(root,
8003														 rel,
8004														 path,
8005														 groupby_pathkeys,
8006														 presorted_keys,
8007														 -1.0);
8008		total_groups = compute_gather_rows(path);
8009		path = (Path *)
8010			create_gather_merge_path(root,
8011									 rel,
8012									 path,
8013									 rel->reltarget,
8014									 groupby_pathkeys,
8015									 NULL,
8016									 &total_groups);
8017
8018		add_path(rel, path);
8019	}
8020}
8021
8022/*
8023 * can_partial_agg
8024 *
8025 * Determines whether or not partial grouping and/or aggregation is possible.
8026 * Returns true when possible, false otherwise.
8027 */
8028static bool
8029can_partial_agg(PlannerInfo *root)
8030{
8031	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
8032
8033	if (!parse->hasAggs && parse->groupClause == NIL)
8034	{
8035		/*
8036		 * We don't know how to do parallel aggregation unless we have either
8037		 * some aggregates or a grouping clause.
8038		 */
8039		return false;
8040	}
8041	else if (parse->groupingSets)
8042	{
8043		/* We don't know how to do grouping sets in parallel. */
8044		return false;
8045	}
8046	else if (root->hasNonPartialAggs || root->hasNonSerialAggs)
8047	{
8048		/* Insufficient support for partial mode. */
8049		return false;
8050	}
8051
8052	/* Everything looks good. */
8053	return true;
8054}
8055
8056/*
8057 * apply_scanjoin_target_to_paths
8058 *
8059 * Adjust the final scan/join relation, and recursively all of its children,
8060 * to generate the final scan/join target.  It would be more correct to model
8061 * this as a separate planning step with a new RelOptInfo at the toplevel and
8062 * for each child relation, but doing it this way is noticeably cheaper.
8063 * Maybe that problem can be solved at some point, but for now we do this.
8064 *
8065 * If tlist_same_exprs is true, then the scan/join target to be applied has
8066 * the same expressions as the existing reltarget, so we need only insert the
8067 * appropriate sortgroupref information.  By avoiding the creation of
8068 * projection paths we save effort both immediately and at plan creation time.
8069 */
8070static void
8071apply_scanjoin_target_to_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
8072							   RelOptInfo *rel,
8073							   List *scanjoin_targets,
8074							   List *scanjoin_targets_contain_srfs,
8075							   bool scanjoin_target_parallel_safe,
8076							   bool tlist_same_exprs)
8077{
8078	bool		rel_is_partitioned = IS_PARTITIONED_REL(rel);
8079	PathTarget *scanjoin_target;
8080	ListCell   *lc;
8081
8082	/* This recurses, so be paranoid. */
8083	check_stack_depth();
8084
8085	/*
8086	 * If the rel is partitioned, we want to drop its existing paths and
8087	 * generate new ones.  This function would still be correct if we kept the
8088	 * existing paths: we'd modify them to generate the correct target above
8089	 * the partitioning Append, and then they'd compete on cost with paths
8090	 * generating the target below the Append.  However, in our current cost
8091	 * model the latter way is always the same or cheaper cost, so modifying
8092	 * the existing paths would just be useless work.  Moreover, when the cost
8093	 * is the same, varying roundoff errors might sometimes allow an existing
8094	 * path to be picked, resulting in undesirable cross-platform plan
8095	 * variations.  So we drop old paths and thereby force the work to be done
8096	 * below the Append, except in the case of a non-parallel-safe target.
8097	 *
8098	 * Some care is needed, because we have to allow
8099	 * generate_useful_gather_paths to see the old partial paths in the next
8100	 * stanza.  Hence, zap the main pathlist here, then allow
8101	 * generate_useful_gather_paths to add path(s) to the main list, and
8102	 * finally zap the partial pathlist.
8103	 */
8104	if (rel_is_partitioned)
8105		rel->pathlist = NIL;
8106
8107	/*
8108	 * If the scan/join target is not parallel-safe, partial paths cannot
8109	 * generate it.
8110	 */
8111	if (!scanjoin_target_parallel_safe)
8112	{
8113		/*
8114		 * Since we can't generate the final scan/join target in parallel
8115		 * workers, this is our last opportunity to use any partial paths that
8116		 * exist; so build Gather path(s) that use them and emit whatever the
8117		 * current reltarget is.  We don't do this in the case where the
8118		 * target is parallel-safe, since we will be able to generate superior
8119		 * paths by doing it after the final scan/join target has been
8120		 * applied.
8121		 */
8122		generate_useful_gather_paths(root, rel, false);
8123
8124		/* Can't use parallel query above this level. */
8125		rel->partial_pathlist = NIL;
8126		rel->consider_parallel = false;
8127	}
8128
8129	/* Finish dropping old paths for a partitioned rel, per comment above */
8130	if (rel_is_partitioned)
8131		rel->partial_pathlist = NIL;
8132
8133	/* Extract SRF-free scan/join target. */
8134	scanjoin_target = linitial_node(PathTarget, scanjoin_targets);
8135
8136	/*
8137	 * Apply the SRF-free scan/join target to each existing path.
8138	 *
8139	 * If the tlist exprs are the same, we can just inject the sortgroupref
8140	 * information into the existing pathtargets.  Otherwise, replace each
8141	 * path with a projection path that generates the SRF-free scan/join
8142	 * target.  This can't change the ordering of paths within rel->pathlist,
8143	 * so we just modify the list in place.
8144	 */
8145	foreach(lc, rel->pathlist)
8146	{
8147		Path	   *subpath = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
8148
8149		/* Shouldn't have any parameterized paths anymore */
8150		Assert(subpath->param_info == NULL);
8151
8152		if (tlist_same_exprs)
8153			subpath->pathtarget->sortgrouprefs =
8154				scanjoin_target->sortgrouprefs;
8155		else
8156		{
8157			Path	   *newpath;
8158
8159			newpath = (Path *) create_projection_path(root, rel, subpath,
8160													  scanjoin_target);
8161			lfirst(lc) = newpath;
8162		}
8163	}
8164
8165	/* Likewise adjust the targets for any partial paths. */
8166	foreach(lc, rel->partial_pathlist)
8167	{
8168		Path	   *subpath = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
8169
8170		/* Shouldn't have any parameterized paths anymore */
8171		Assert(subpath->param_info == NULL);
8172
8173		if (tlist_same_exprs)
8174			subpath->pathtarget->sortgrouprefs =
8175				scanjoin_target->sortgrouprefs;
8176		else
8177		{
8178			Path	   *newpath;
8179
8180			newpath = (Path *) create_projection_path(root, rel, subpath,
8181													  scanjoin_target);
8182			lfirst(lc) = newpath;
8183		}
8184	}
8185
8186	/*
8187	 * Now, if final scan/join target contains SRFs, insert ProjectSetPath(s)
8188	 * atop each existing path.  (Note that this function doesn't look at the
8189	 * cheapest-path fields, which is a good thing because they're bogus right
8190	 * now.)
8191	 */
8192	if (root->parse->hasTargetSRFs)
8193		adjust_paths_for_srfs(root, rel,
8194							  scanjoin_targets,
8195							  scanjoin_targets_contain_srfs);
8196
8197	/*
8198	 * Update the rel's target to be the final (with SRFs) scan/join target.
8199	 * This now matches the actual output of all the paths, and we might get
8200	 * confused in createplan.c if they don't agree.  We must do this now so
8201	 * that any append paths made in the next part will use the correct
8202	 * pathtarget (cf. create_append_path).
8203	 *
8204	 * Note that this is also necessary if GetForeignUpperPaths() gets called
8205	 * on the final scan/join relation or on any of its children, since the
8206	 * FDW might look at the rel's target to create ForeignPaths.
8207	 */
8208	rel->reltarget = llast_node(PathTarget, scanjoin_targets);
8209
8210	/*
8211	 * If the relation is partitioned, recursively apply the scan/join target
8212	 * to all partitions, and generate brand-new Append paths in which the
8213	 * scan/join target is computed below the Append rather than above it.
8214	 * Since Append is not projection-capable, that might save a separate
8215	 * Result node, and it also is important for partitionwise aggregate.
8216	 */
8217	if (rel_is_partitioned)
8218	{
8219		List	   *live_children = NIL;
8220		int			i;
8221
8222		/* Adjust each partition. */
8223		i = -1;
8224		while ((i = bms_next_member(rel->live_parts, i)) >= 0)
8225		{
8226			RelOptInfo *child_rel = rel->part_rels[i];
8227			AppendRelInfo **appinfos;
8228			int			nappinfos;
8229			List	   *child_scanjoin_targets = NIL;
8230
8231			Assert(child_rel != NULL);
8232
8233			/* Dummy children can be ignored. */
8234			if (IS_DUMMY_REL(child_rel))
8235				continue;
8236
8237			/* Translate scan/join targets for this child. */
8238			appinfos = find_appinfos_by_relids(root, child_rel->relids,
8239											   &nappinfos);
8240			foreach(lc, scanjoin_targets)
8241			{
8242				PathTarget *target = lfirst_node(PathTarget, lc);
8243
8244				target = copy_pathtarget(target);
8245				target->exprs = (List *)
8246					adjust_appendrel_attrs(root,
8247										   (Node *) target->exprs,
8248										   nappinfos, appinfos);
8249				child_scanjoin_targets = lappend(child_scanjoin_targets,
8250												 target);
8251			}
8252			pfree(appinfos);
8253
8254			/* Recursion does the real work. */
8255			apply_scanjoin_target_to_paths(root, child_rel,
8256										   child_scanjoin_targets,
8257										   scanjoin_targets_contain_srfs,
8258										   scanjoin_target_parallel_safe,
8259										   tlist_same_exprs);
8260
8261			/* Save non-dummy children for Append paths. */
8262			if (!IS_DUMMY_REL(child_rel))
8263				live_children = lappend(live_children, child_rel);
8264		}
8265
8266		/* Build new paths for this relation by appending child paths. */
8267		add_paths_to_append_rel(root, rel, live_children);
8268	}
8269
8270	/*
8271	 * Consider generating Gather or Gather Merge paths.  We must only do this
8272	 * if the relation is parallel safe, and we don't do it for child rels to
8273	 * avoid creating multiple Gather nodes within the same plan. We must do
8274	 * this after all paths have been generated and before set_cheapest, since
8275	 * one of the generated paths may turn out to be the cheapest one.
8276	 */
8277	if (rel->consider_parallel && !IS_OTHER_REL(rel))
8278		generate_useful_gather_paths(root, rel, false);
8279
8280	/*
8281	 * Reassess which paths are the cheapest, now that we've potentially added
8282	 * new Gather (or Gather Merge) and/or Append (or MergeAppend) paths to
8283	 * this relation.
8284	 */
8285	set_cheapest(rel);
8286}
8287
8288/*
8289 * create_partitionwise_grouping_paths
8290 *
8291 * If the partition keys of input relation are part of the GROUP BY clause, all
8292 * the rows belonging to a given group come from a single partition.  This
8293 * allows aggregation/grouping over a partitioned relation to be broken down
8294 * into aggregation/grouping on each partition.  This should be no worse, and
8295 * often better, than the normal approach.
8296 *
8297 * However, if the GROUP BY clause does not contain all the partition keys,
8298 * rows from a given group may be spread across multiple partitions. In that
8299 * case, we perform partial aggregation for each group, append the results,
8300 * and then finalize aggregation.  This is less certain to win than the
8301 * previous case.  It may win if the PartialAggregate stage greatly reduces
8302 * the number of groups, because fewer rows will pass through the Append node.
8303 * It may lose if we have lots of small groups.
8304 */
8305static void
8306create_partitionwise_grouping_paths(PlannerInfo *root,
8307									RelOptInfo *input_rel,
8308									RelOptInfo *grouped_rel,
8309									RelOptInfo *partially_grouped_rel,
8310									const AggClauseCosts *agg_costs,
8311									grouping_sets_data *gd,
8312									PartitionwiseAggregateType patype,
8313									GroupPathExtraData *extra)
8314{
8315	List	   *grouped_live_children = NIL;
8316	List	   *partially_grouped_live_children = NIL;
8317	PathTarget *target = grouped_rel->reltarget;
8318	bool		partial_grouping_valid = true;
8319	int			i;
8320
8321	Assert(patype != PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_NONE);
8322	Assert(patype != PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_PARTIAL ||
8323		   partially_grouped_rel != NULL);
8324
8325	/* Add paths for partitionwise aggregation/grouping. */
8326	i = -1;
8327	while ((i = bms_next_member(input_rel->live_parts, i)) >= 0)
8328	{
8329		RelOptInfo *child_input_rel = input_rel->part_rels[i];
8330		PathTarget *child_target;
8331		AppendRelInfo **appinfos;
8332		int			nappinfos;
8333		GroupPathExtraData child_extra;
8334		RelOptInfo *child_grouped_rel;
8335		RelOptInfo *child_partially_grouped_rel;
8336
8337		Assert(child_input_rel != NULL);
8338
8339		/* Dummy children can be ignored. */
8340		if (IS_DUMMY_REL(child_input_rel))
8341			continue;
8342
8343		child_target = copy_pathtarget(target);
8344
8345		/*
8346		 * Copy the given "extra" structure as is and then override the
8347		 * members specific to this child.
8348		 */
8349		memcpy(&child_extra, extra, sizeof(child_extra));
8350
8351		appinfos = find_appinfos_by_relids(root, child_input_rel->relids,
8352										   &nappinfos);
8353
8354		child_target->exprs = (List *)
8355			adjust_appendrel_attrs(root,
8356								   (Node *) target->exprs,
8357								   nappinfos, appinfos);
8358
8359		/* Translate havingQual and targetList. */
8360		child_extra.havingQual = (Node *)
8361			adjust_appendrel_attrs(root,
8362								   extra->havingQual,
8363								   nappinfos, appinfos);
8364		child_extra.targetList = (List *)
8365			adjust_appendrel_attrs(root,
8366								   (Node *) extra->targetList,
8367								   nappinfos, appinfos);
8368
8369		/*
8370		 * extra->patype was the value computed for our parent rel; patype is
8371		 * the value for this relation.  For the child, our value is its
8372		 * parent rel's value.
8373		 */
8374		child_extra.patype = patype;
8375
8376		/*
8377		 * Create grouping relation to hold fully aggregated grouping and/or
8378		 * aggregation paths for the child.
8379		 */
8380		child_grouped_rel = make_grouping_rel(root, child_input_rel,
8381											  child_target,
8382											  extra->target_parallel_safe,
8383											  child_extra.havingQual);
8384
8385		/* Create grouping paths for this child relation. */
8386		create_ordinary_grouping_paths(root, child_input_rel,
8387									   child_grouped_rel,
8388									   agg_costs, gd, &child_extra,
8389									   &child_partially_grouped_rel);
8390
8391		if (child_partially_grouped_rel)
8392		{
8393			partially_grouped_live_children =
8394				lappend(partially_grouped_live_children,
8395						child_partially_grouped_rel);
8396		}
8397		else
8398			partial_grouping_valid = false;
8399
8400		if (patype == PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_FULL)
8401		{
8402			set_cheapest(child_grouped_rel);
8403			grouped_live_children = lappend(grouped_live_children,
8404											child_grouped_rel);
8405		}
8406
8407		pfree(appinfos);
8408	}
8409
8410	/*
8411	 * Try to create append paths for partially grouped children. For full
8412	 * partitionwise aggregation, we might have paths in the partial_pathlist
8413	 * if parallel aggregation is possible.  For partial partitionwise
8414	 * aggregation, we may have paths in both pathlist and partial_pathlist.
8415	 *
8416	 * NB: We must have a partially grouped path for every child in order to
8417	 * generate a partially grouped path for this relation.
8418	 */
8419	if (partially_grouped_rel && partial_grouping_valid)
8420	{
8421		Assert(partially_grouped_live_children != NIL);
8422
8423		add_paths_to_append_rel(root, partially_grouped_rel,
8424								partially_grouped_live_children);
8425
8426		/*
8427		 * We need call set_cheapest, since the finalization step will use the
8428		 * cheapest path from the rel.
8429		 */
8430		if (partially_grouped_rel->pathlist)
8431			set_cheapest(partially_grouped_rel);
8432	}
8433
8434	/* If possible, create append paths for fully grouped children. */
8435	if (patype == PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_FULL)
8436	{
8437		Assert(grouped_live_children != NIL);
8438
8439		add_paths_to_append_rel(root, grouped_rel, grouped_live_children);
8440	}
8441}
8442
8443/*
8444 * group_by_has_partkey
8445 *
8446 * Returns true if all the partition keys of the given relation are part of
8447 * the GROUP BY clauses, including having matching collation, false otherwise.
8448 */
8449static bool
8450group_by_has_partkey(RelOptInfo *input_rel,
8451					 List *targetList,
8452					 List *groupClause)
8453{
8454	List	   *groupexprs = get_sortgrouplist_exprs(groupClause, targetList);
8455	int			cnt = 0;
8456	int			partnatts;
8457
8458	/* Input relation should be partitioned. */
8459	Assert(input_rel->part_scheme);
8460
8461	/* Rule out early, if there are no partition keys present. */
8462	if (!input_rel->partexprs)
8463		return false;
8464
8465	partnatts = input_rel->part_scheme->partnatts;
8466
8467	for (cnt = 0; cnt < partnatts; cnt++)
8468	{
8469		List	   *partexprs = input_rel->partexprs[cnt];
8470		ListCell   *lc;
8471		bool		found = false;
8472
8473		foreach(lc, partexprs)
8474		{
8475			ListCell   *lg;
8476			Expr	   *partexpr = lfirst(lc);
8477			Oid			partcoll = input_rel->part_scheme->partcollation[cnt];
8478
8479			foreach(lg, groupexprs)
8480			{
8481				Expr	   *groupexpr = lfirst(lg);
8482				Oid			groupcoll = exprCollation((Node *) groupexpr);
8483
8484				/*
8485				 * Note: we can assume there is at most one RelabelType node;
8486				 * eval_const_expressions() will have simplified if more than
8487				 * one.
8488				 */
8489				if (IsA(groupexpr, RelabelType))
8490					groupexpr = ((RelabelType *) groupexpr)->arg;
8491
8492				if (equal(groupexpr, partexpr))
8493				{
8494					/*
8495					 * Reject a match if the grouping collation does not match
8496					 * the partitioning collation.
8497					 */
8498					if (OidIsValid(partcoll) && OidIsValid(groupcoll) &&
8499						partcoll != groupcoll)
8500						return false;
8501
8502					found = true;
8503					break;
8504				}
8505			}
8506
8507			if (found)
8508				break;
8509		}
8510
8511		/*
8512		 * If none of the partition key expressions match with any of the
8513		 * GROUP BY expression, return false.
8514		 */
8515		if (!found)
8516			return false;
8517	}
8518
8519	return true;
8520}
8521
8522/*
8523 * generate_setop_child_grouplist
8524 *		Build a SortGroupClause list defining the sort/grouping properties
8525 *		of the child of a set operation.
8526 *
8527 * This is similar to generate_setop_grouplist() but differs as the setop
8528 * child query's targetlist entries may already have a tleSortGroupRef
8529 * assigned for other purposes, such as GROUP BYs.  Here we keep the
8530 * SortGroupClause list in the same order as 'op' groupClauses and just adjust
8531 * the tleSortGroupRef to reference the TargetEntry's 'ressortgroupref'.  If
8532 * any of the columns in the targetlist don't match to the setop's colTypes
8533 * then we return an empty list.  This may leave some TLEs with unreferenced
8534 * ressortgroupref markings, but that's harmless.
8535 */
8536static List *
8537generate_setop_child_grouplist(SetOperationStmt *op, List *targetlist)
8538{
8539	List	   *grouplist = copyObject(op->groupClauses);
8540	ListCell   *lg;
8541	ListCell   *lt;
8542	ListCell   *ct;
8543
8544	lg = list_head(grouplist);
8545	ct = list_head(op->colTypes);
8546	foreach(lt, targetlist)
8547	{
8548		TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(lt);
8549		SortGroupClause *sgc;
8550		Oid			coltype;
8551
8552		/* resjunk columns could have sortgrouprefs.  Leave these alone */
8553		if (tle->resjunk)
8554			continue;
8555
8556		/*
8557		 * We expect every non-resjunk target to have a SortGroupClause and
8558		 * colTypes.
8559		 */
8560		Assert(lg != NULL);
8561		Assert(ct != NULL);
8562		sgc = (SortGroupClause *) lfirst(lg);
8563		coltype = lfirst_oid(ct);
8564
8565		/* reject if target type isn't the same as the setop target type */
8566		if (coltype != exprType((Node *) tle->expr))
8567			return NIL;
8568
8569		lg = lnext(grouplist, lg);
8570		ct = lnext(op->colTypes, ct);
8571
8572		/* assign a tleSortGroupRef, or reuse the existing one */
8573		sgc->tleSortGroupRef = assignSortGroupRef(tle, targetlist);
8574	}
8575
8576	Assert(lg == NULL);
8577	Assert(ct == NULL);
8578
8579	return grouplist;
8580}
